Another example is bootstrapping upon consumer starting up where there are\n403:  * a lot of history data to catch up, the applications usually want to get the latest data on some of the topics before consider\n404:  * fetching other topics.\n405:  *\n406:  * <p>\n407:  * Kafka supports dynamic controlling of consumption flows by using {@link #pause(Collection)} and {@link #resume(Collection)}\n408:  * to pause the consumption on the specified assigned partitions and resume the consumption\n409:  * on the specified paused partitions respectively in the future {@link #poll(Duration)} calls.\n410:  *\n411:  * <h3>Reading Transactional Messages</h3>\n412:  *\n413:  * <p>\n414:  * Transactions were introduced in Kafka 0.11.0 wherein applications can write to multiple topics and partitions atomically.\n415:  * In order for this to work, consumers reading from these partitions should be configured to only read committed data.\n416:  * This can be achieved by setting the {@code isolation.level=read_committed} in the consumer's configuration.\n417:  *\n418:  * <p>\n419:  * In <code>read_committed</code> mode, the consumer will read only those transactional messages which have been\n420:  * successfully committed. It will continue to read non-transactional messages as before. There is no client-side\n421:  * buffering in <code>read_committed</code> mode. Instead, the end offset of a partition for a <code>read_committed</code>\n422:  * consumer would be the offset of the first message in the partition belonging to an open transaction. This offset\n423:  * is known as the 'Last Stable Offset'(LSO).</p>\n424:  *\n425:  * <p>\n426:  * A {@code read_committed} consumer will only read up to the LSO and filter out any transactional\n427:  * messages which have been aborted. The LSO also affects the behavior of {@link #seekToEnd(Collection)} and\n428:  * {@link #endOffsets(Collection)} for {@code read_committed} consumers, details of which are in each method's documentation.\n429:  * Finally, the fetch lag metrics are also adjusted to be relative to the LSO for {@code read_committed} consumers.\n430:  *\n431:  * <p>\n432:  * Partitions with transactional messages will include commit or abort markers which indicate the result of a transaction.\n433:  * There markers are not returned to applications, yet have an offset in the log. As a result, applications reading from\n434:  * topics with transactional messages will see gaps in the consumed offsets. These missing messages would be the transaction\n435:  * markers, and they are filtered out for consumers in both isolation levels. Additionally, applications using\n436:  * {@code read_committed} consumers may also see gaps due to aborted transactions, since those messages would not\n437:  * be returned by the consumer and yet would have valid offsets.\n438:  *\n439:  * <h3><a name=\"multithreaded\">Multi-threaded Processing</a></h3>\n440:  *\n441:  * The Kafka consumer is NOT thread-safe. It is the responsibility of the user to ensure that multi-threaded access\n442:  * is properly synchronized. Un-synchronized access will result in {@link ConcurrentModificationException}.\n443:  *\n444:  * <p>\n445:  * The only exception to this rule is {@link #wakeup()}, which can safely be used from an external thread to\n446:  * interrupt an active operation. In this case, a {@link org.apache.kafka.common.errors.WakeupException} will be\n447:  * thrown from the thread blocking on the operation. This can be used to shutdown the consumer from another thread.\n448:  * The following snippet shows the typical pattern:\n449:  *\n450:  * <pre>\n451:  * {@code\n452:  * public class KafkaConsumerRunner implements Runnable {\n453:  *     private final AtomicBoolean closed = new AtomicBoolean(false);\n454:  *     private final KafkaConsumer consumer;\n455:  *\n456:  *     public KafkaConsumerRunner(KafkaConsumer consumer) {\n457:  *       this.consumer = consumer;\n458:  *     }\n459:  *\n460:  *     @Override\n461:  *     public void run() {\n462:  *         try {\n463:  *             consumer.subscribe(Arrays.asList(\"topic\"));\n464:  *             while (!closed.get()) {\n465:  *                 ConsumerRecords records = consumer.poll(Duration.ofMillis(10000));\n466:  *                 // Handle new records\n467:  *             }\n468:  *         } catch (WakeupException e) {\n469:  *             // Ignore exception if closing\n470:  *             if (!closed.get()) throw e;\n471:  *         } finally {\n472:  *             consumer.close();\n473:  *         }\n474:  *     }\n475:  *\n476:  *     // Shutdown hook which can be called from a separate thread\n477:  *     public void shutdown() {\n478:  *         closed.set(true);\n479:  *         consumer.wakeup();\n480:  *     }\n481:  * }\n482:  * }</pre>\n483:  *\n484:  * Then in a separate thread, the consumer can be shutdown by setting the closed flag and waking up the consumer.\n485:  *\n486:  * <p>\n487:  * <pre>\n488:  * {@code\n489:  *     closed.set(true);\n490:  *     consumer.wakeup();\n491:  * }</pre>\n492:  *\n493:  * <p>\n494:  * Note that while it is possible to use thread interrupts instead of {@link #wakeup()} to abort a blocking operation\n495:  * (in which case, {@link InterruptException} will be raised), we discourage their use since they may cause a clean\n496:  * shutdown of the consumer to be aborted. Interrupts are mainly supported for those cases where using {@link #wakeup()}\n497:  * is impossible, e.g. when a consumer thread is managed by code that is unaware of the Kafka client.\n498:  *\n499:  * <p>\n500:  * We have intentionally avoided implementing a particular threading model for processing. This leaves several\n501:  * options for implementing multi-threaded processing of records.\n502:  *\n503:  * <h4>1. One Consumer Per Thread</h4>\n504:  *\n505:  * A simple option is to give each thread its own consumer instance. Here are the pros and cons of this approach:\n506:  * <ul>\n507:  * <li><b>PRO</b>: It is the easiest to implement\n508:  * <li><b>PRO</b>: It is often the fastest as no inter-thread co-ordination is needed\n509:  * <li><b>PRO</b>: It makes in-order processing on a per-partition basis very easy to implement (each thread just\n510:  * processes messages in the order it receives them).\n511:  * <li><b>CON</b>: More consumers means more TCP connections to the cluster (one per thread). In general Kafka handles\n512:  * connections very efficiently so this is generally a small cost.\n513:  * <li><b>CON</b>: Multiple consumers means more requests being sent to the server and slightly less batching of data\n514:  * which can cause some drop in I/O throughput.\n515:  * <li><b>CON</b>: The number of total threads across all processes will be limited by the total number of partitions.\n516:  * </ul>\n517:  *\n518:  * <h4>2. Decouple Consumption and Processing</h4>\n519:  *\n520:  * Another alternative is to have one or more consumer threads that do all data consumption and hands off\n521:  * {@link ConsumerRecords} instances to a blocking queue consumed by a pool of processor threads that actually handle\n522:  * the record processing.\n523:  *\n524:  * This option likewise has pros and cons:\n525:  * <ul>\n526:  * <li><b>PRO</b>: This option allows independently scaling the number of consumers and processors. This makes it\n527:  * possible to have a single consumer that feeds many processor threads, avoiding any limitation on partitions.\n528:  * <li><b>CON</b>: Guaranteeing order across the processors requires particular care as the threads will execute\n529:  * independently an earlier chunk of data may actually be processed after a later chunk of data just due to the luck of\n530:  * thread execution timing. For processing that has no ordering requirements this is not a problem.\n531:  * <li><b>CON</b>: Manually committing the position becomes harder as it requires that all threads co-ordinate to ensure\n532:  * that processing is complete for that partition.\n533:  * </ul>\n534:  *\n535:  * There are many possible variations on this approach. For example each processor thread can have its own queue, and\n536:  * the consumer threads can hash into these queues using the TopicPartition to ensure in-order consumption and simplify\n537:  * commit.\n538:  */\n539: public class KafkaConsumer<K, V> implements Consumer<K, V> {\n540: \n541:     private static final ConsumerDelegateCreator CREATOR = new ConsumerDelegateCreator();\n542: \n543:     private final ConsumerDelegate<K, V> delegate;\n544: \n545:     /**\n546:      * A consumer is instantiated by providing a set of key-value pairs as configuration. Valid configuration strings\n547:      * are documented <a href=\"http://kafka.apache.org/documentation.html#consumerconfigs\" >here</a>. Values can be\n548:      * either strings or objects of the appropriate type (for example a numeric configuration would accept either the\n549:      * string \"42\" or the integer 42).\n550:      * <p>\n551:      * Valid configuration strings are documented at {@link ConsumerConfig}.\n552:      * <p>\n553:      * Note: after creating a {@code KafkaConsumer} you must always {@link #close()} it to avoid resource leaks.\n554:      *\n555:      * @param configs The consumer configs\n556:      */\n557:     public KafkaConsumer(Map<String, Object> configs) {\n558:         this(configs, null, null);\n559:     }\n560: \n561:     /**\n562:      * A consumer is instantiated by providing a {@link java.util.Properties} object as configuration.\n563:      * <p>\n564:      * Valid configuration strings are documented at {@link ConsumerConfig}.\n565:      * <p>\n566:      * Note: after creating a {@code KafkaConsumer} you must always {@link #close()} it to avoid resource leaks.\n567:      *\n568:      * @param properties The consumer configuration properties\n569:      */\n570:     public KafkaConsumer(Properties properties) {\n571:         this(properties, null, null);\n572:     }\n573: \n574:     /**\n575:      * A consumer is instantiated by providing a {@link java.util.Properties} object as configuration, and a\n576:      * key and a value {@link Deserializer}.\n577:      * <p>\n578:      * Valid configuration strings are documented at {@link ConsumerConfig}.\n579:      * <p>\n580:      * Note: after creating a {@code KafkaConsumer} you must always {@link #close()} it to avoid resource leaks.\n581:      *\n582:      * @param properties The consumer configuration properties\n583:      * @param keyDeserializer The deserializer for key that implements {@link Deserializer}. The configure() method\n584:      *            won't be called in the consumer when the deserializer is passed in directly.\n585:      * @param valueDeserializer The deserializer for value that implements {@link Deserializer}. The configure() method\n586:      *            won't be called in the consumer when the deserializer is passed in directly.\n587:      */\n588:     public KafkaConsumer(Properties properties,\n589:                          Deserializer<K> keyDeserializer,\n590:                          Deserializer<V> valueDeserializer) {\n591:         this(propsToMap(properties), keyDeserializer, valueDeserializer);\n592:     }\n593: \n594:     /**\n595:      * A consumer is instantiated by providing a set of key-value pairs as configuration, and a key and a value {@link Deserializer}.\n596:      * <p>\n597:      * Valid configuration strings are documented at {@link ConsumerConfig}.\n598:      * <p>\n599:      * Note: after creating a {@code KafkaConsumer} you must always {@link #close()} it to avoid resource leaks.\n600:      *\n601:      * @param configs The consumer configs\n602:      * @param keyDeserializer The deserializer for key that implements {@link Deserializer}. The configure() method\n603:      *            won't be called in the consumer when the deserializer is passed in directly.\n604:      * @param valueDeserializer The deserializer for value that implements {@link Deserializer}. The configure() method\n605:      *            won't be called in the consumer when the deserializer is passed in directly.\n606:      */\n607:     public KafkaConsumer(Map<String, Object> configs,\n608:                          Deserializer<K> keyDeserializer,\n609:                          Deserializer<V> valueDeserializer) {\n610:         this(new ConsumerConfig(ConsumerConfig.appendDeserializerToConfig(configs, keyDeserializer, valueDeserializer)),\n611:                 keyDeserializer, valueDeserializer);\n612:     }\n613: \n614:     KafkaConsumer(ConsumerConfig config, Deserializer<K> keyDeserializer, Deserializer<V> valueDeserializer) {\n615:         delegate = CREATOR.create(config, keyDeserializer, valueDeserializer);\n616:     }\n617: \n618:     KafkaConsumer(LogContext logContext,\n619:                   Time time,\n620:                   ConsumerConfig config,\n621:                   Deserializer<K> keyDeserializer,\n622:                   Deserializer<V> valueDeserializer,\n623:                   KafkaClient client,\n624:                   SubscriptionState subscriptions,\n625:                   ConsumerMetadata metadata,\n626:                   List<ConsumerPartitionAssignor> assignors) {\n627:         delegate = CREATOR.create(\n628:             logContext,\n629:             time,\n630:             config,\n631:             keyDeserializer,\n632:             valueDeserializer,\n633:             client,\n634:             subscriptions,\n635:             metadata,\n636:             assignors\n637:         );\n638:     }\n639: \n640:     /**\n641:      * Get the set of partitions currently assigned to this consumer. If subscription happened by directly assigning\n642:      * partitions using {@link #assign(Collection)} then this will simply return the same partitions that\n643:      * were assigned. If topic subscription was used, then this will give the set of topic partitions currently assigned\n644:      * to the consumer (which may be none if the assignment hasn't happened yet, or the partitions are in the\n645:      * process of getting reassigned).\n646:      * @return The set of partitions currently assigned to this consumer\n647:      */\n648:     public Set<TopicPartition> assignment() {\n649:         return delegate.assignment();\n650:     }\n651: \n652:     /**\n653:      * Get the current subscription. Will return the same topics used in the most recent call to\n654:      * {@link #subscribe(Collection, ConsumerRebalanceListener)}, or an empty set if no such call has been made.\n655:      * @return The set of topics currently subscribed to\n656:      */\n657:     public Set<String> subscription() {\n658:         return delegate.subscription();\n659:     }\n660: \n661:     /**\n662:      * Subscribe to the given list of topics to get dynamically\n663:      * assigned partitions. <b>Topic subscriptions are not incremental. This list will replace the current\n664:      * assignment (if there is one).</b> Note that it is not possible to combine topic subscription with group management\n665:      * with manual partition assignment through {@link #assign(Collection)}.\n666:      *\n667:      * If the given list of topics is empty, it is treated the same as {@link #unsubscribe()}.\n668:      *\n669:      * <p>\n670:      * As part of group management, the group coordinator will keep track of the list of consumers that belong to a particular\n671:      * group and will trigger a rebalance operation if any one of the following events are triggered:\n672:      * <ul>\n673:      * <li>Number of partitions change for any of the subscribed topics\n674:      * <li>A subscribed topic is created or deleted\n675:      * <li>An existing member of the consumer group is shutdown or fails\n676:      * <li>A new member is added to the consumer group\n677:      * </ul>\n678:      * <p>\n679:      * When any of these events are triggered, the provided listener will be invoked in this way:\n680:      * <ul>\n681:      *     <li>{@link ConsumerRebalanceListener#onPartitionsRevoked(Collection)} will be invoked with the partitions to revoke, before re-assigning those partitions to another consumer.</li>\n682:      *     <li>{@link ConsumerRebalanceListener#onPartitionsAssigned(Collection)} will be invoked when the rebalance completes (even if no new partitions are assigned to the consumer)</li>\n683:      * </ul>\n684:      * Note that rebalances will only occur during an active call to {@link #poll(Duration)}, so callbacks will\n685:      * also only be invoked during that time.\n686:      *\n687:      * The provided listener will immediately override any listener set in a previous call to subscribe.\n688:      * It is guaranteed, however, that the partitions revoked/assigned through this interface are from topics\n689:      * subscribed in this call. See {@link ConsumerRebalanceListener} for more details.\n690:      *\n691:      * @param topics The list of topics to subscribe to\n692:      * @param listener Non-null listener instance to get notifications on partition assignment/revocation for the\n693:      *                 subscribed topics\n694:      * @throws IllegalArgumentException If topics is null or contains null or empty elements, or if listener is null\n695:      * @throws IllegalStateException If {@code subscribe()} is called previously with pattern, or assign is called\n696:      *                               previously (without a subsequent call to {@link #unsubscribe()}), or if not\n697:      *                               configured at-least one partition assignment strategy\n698:      */\n699:     @Override\n700:     public void subscribe(Collection<String> topics, ConsumerRebalanceListener listener) {\n701:         delegate.subscribe(topics, listener);\n702:     }\n703: \n704:     /**\n705:      * Subscribe to the given list of topics to get dynamically assigned partitions.\n706:      * <b>Topic subscriptions are not incremental. This list will replace the current\n707:      * assignment (if there is one).</b> It is not possible to combine topic subscription with group management\n708:      * with manual partition assignment through {@link #assign(Collection)}.\n709:      *\n710:      * If the given list of topics is empty, it is treated the same as {@link #unsubscribe()}.\n711:      *\n712:      * <p>\n713:      * This is a short-hand for {@link #subscribe(Collection, ConsumerRebalanceListener)}, which\n714:      * uses a no-op listener. If you need the ability to seek to particular offsets, you should prefer\n715:      * {@link #subscribe(Collection, ConsumerRebalanceListener)}, since group rebalances will cause partition offsets\n716:      * to be reset. You should also provide your own listener if you are doing your own offset\n717:      * management since the listener gives you an opportunity to commit offsets before a rebalance finishes.\n718:      *\n719:      * @param topics The list of topics to subscribe to\n720:      * @throws IllegalArgumentException If topics is null or contains null or empty elements\n721:      * @throws IllegalStateException If {@code subscribe()} is called previously with pattern, or assign is called\n722:      *                               previously (without a subsequent call to {@link #unsubscribe()}), or if not\n723:      *                               configured at-least one partition assignment strategy\n724:      */\n725:     @Override\n726:     public void subscribe(Collection<String> topics) {\n727:         delegate.subscribe(topics);\n728:     }\n729: \n730:     /**\n731:      * Subscribe to all topics matching specified pattern to get dynamically assigned partitions.\n732:      * The pattern matching will be done periodically against all topics existing at the time of check.\n733:      * This can be controlled through the {@code metadata.max.age.ms} configuration: by lowering\n734:      * the max metadata age, the consumer will refresh metadata more often and check for matching topics.\n735:      * <p>\n736:      * See {@link #subscribe(Collection, ConsumerRebalanceListener)} for details on the\n737:      * use of the {@link ConsumerRebalanceListener}. Generally rebalances are triggered when there\n738:      * is a change to the topics matching the provided pattern and when consumer group membership changes.\n739:      * Group rebalances only take place during an active call to {@link #poll(Duration)}.\n740:      *\n741:      * @param pattern Pattern to subscribe to\n742:      * @param listener Non-null listener instance to get notifications on partition assignment/revocation for the\n743:      *                 subscribed topics\n744:      * @throws IllegalArgumentException If pattern or listener is null\n745:      * @throws IllegalStateException If {@code subscribe()} is called previously with topics, or assign is called\n746:      *                               previously (without a subsequent call to {@link #unsubscribe()}), or if not\n747:      *                               configured at-least one partition assignment strategy\n748:      */\n749:     @Override\n750:     public void subscribe(Pattern pattern, ConsumerRebalanceListener listener) {\n751:         delegate.subscribe(pattern, listener);\n752:     }\n753: \n754:     /**\n755:      * Subscribe to all topics matching specified pattern to get dynamically assigned partitions.\n756:      * The pattern matching will be done periodically against topics existing at the time of check.\n757:      * <p>\n758:      * This is a short-hand for {@link #subscribe(Pattern, ConsumerRebalanceListener)}, which\n759:      * uses a no-op listener. If you need the ability to seek to particular offsets, you should prefer\n760:      * {@link #subscribe(Pattern, ConsumerRebalanceListener)}, since group rebalances will cause partition offsets\n761:      * to be reset. You should also provide your own listener if you are doing your own offset\n762:      * management since the listener gives you an opportunity to commit offsets before a rebalance finishes.\n763:      *\n764:      * @param pattern Pattern to subscribe to\n765:      * @throws IllegalArgumentException If pattern is null\n766:      * @throws IllegalStateException If {@code subscribe()} is called previously with topics, or assign is called\n767:      *                               previously (without a subsequent call to {@link #unsubscribe()}), or if not\n768:      *                               configured at-least one partition assignment strategy\n769:      */\n770:     @Override\n771:     public void subscribe(Pattern pattern) {\n772:         delegate.subscribe(pattern);\n773:     }\n774: \n775:     /**\n776:      * Subscribe to all topics matching the specified pattern, to get dynamically assigned partitions.\n777:      * The pattern matching will be done periodically against all topics. This is only supported under the\n778:      * CONSUMER group protocol (see {@link ConsumerConfig#GROUP_PROTOCOL_CONFIG}).\n779:      * <p>\n780:      * If the provided pattern is not compatible with Google RE2/J, an {@link InvalidRegularExpression} will be\n781:      * eventually thrown on a call to {@link #poll(Duration)} following this call to subscribe.\n782:      * <p>\n783:      * See {@link #subscribe(Collection, ConsumerRebalanceListener)} for details on the\n784:      * use of the {@link ConsumerRebalanceListener}. Generally, rebalances are triggered when there\n785:      * is a change to the topics matching the provided pattern and when consumer group membership changes.\n786:      * Group rebalances only take place during an active call to {@link #poll(Duration)}.\n787:      *\n788:      * @param pattern  Pattern to subscribe to, that must be compatible with Google RE2/J.\n789:      * @param listener Non-null listener instance to get notifications on partition assignment/revocation for the\n790:      *                 subscribed topics.\n791:      * @throws IllegalArgumentException If pattern is null or empty, or if the listener is null.\n792:      * @throws IllegalStateException    If {@code subscribe()} is called previously with topics, or assign is called\n793:      *                                  previously (without a subsequent call to {@link #unsubscribe()}).\n794:      */\n795:     @Override\n796:     public void subscribe(SubscriptionPattern pattern, ConsumerRebalanceListener listener) {\n797:         delegate.subscribe(pattern, listener);\n798:     }\n799: \n800:     /**\n801:      * Subscribe to all topics matching the specified pattern, to get dynamically assigned partitions.\n802:      * The pattern matching will be done periodically against topics. This is only supported under the\n803:      * CONSUMER group protocol (see {@link ConsumerConfig#GROUP_PROTOCOL_CONFIG})\n804:      * <p>\n805:      * If the provided pattern is not compatible with Google RE2/J, an {@link InvalidRegularExpression} will be\n806:      * eventually thrown on a call to {@link #poll(Duration)} following this call to subscribe.\n807:      * <p>\n808:      * This is a short-hand for {@link #subscribe(Pattern, ConsumerRebalanceListener)}, which\n809:      * uses a no-op listener. If you need the ability to seek to particular offsets, you should prefer\n810:      * {@link #subscribe(Pattern, ConsumerRebalanceListener)}, since group rebalances will cause partition offsets\n811:      * to be reset. You should also provide your own listener if you are doing your own offset\n812:      * management since the listener gives you an opportunity to commit offsets before a rebalance finishes.\n813:      *\n814:      * @param pattern Pattern to subscribe to, that must be compatible with Google RE2/J.\n815:      * @throws IllegalArgumentException If pattern is null or empty.\n816:      * @throws IllegalStateException    If {@code subscribe()} is called previously with topics, or assign is called\n817:      *                                  previously (without a subsequent call to {@link #unsubscribe()}).\n818:      */\n819:     @Override\n820:     public void subscribe(SubscriptionPattern pattern) {\n821:         delegate.subscribe(pattern);\n822:     }\n823: \n824:     /**\n825:      * Unsubscribe from topics currently subscribed with {@link #subscribe(Collection)} or {@link #subscribe(Pattern)}.\n826:      * This also clears any partitions directly assigned through {@link #assign(Collection)}.\n827:      *\n828:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.KafkaException for any other unrecoverable errors (e.g. rebalance callback errors)\n829:      */\n830:     public void unsubscribe() {\n831:         delegate.unsubscribe();\n832:     }\n833: \n834:     /**\n835:      * Manually assign a list of partitions to this consumer. This interface does not allow for incremental assignment\n836:      * and will replace the previous assignment (if there is one).\n837:      * <p>\n838:      * If the given list of topic partitions is empty, it is treated the same as {@link #unsubscribe()}.\n839:      * <p>\n840:      * Manual topic assignment through this method does not use the consumer's group management\n841:      * functionality. As such, there will be no rebalance operation triggered when group membership or cluster and topic\n842:      * metadata change. Note that it is not possible to use both manual partition assignment with {@link #assign(Collection)}\n843:      * and group assignment with {@link #subscribe(Collection, ConsumerRebalanceListener)}.\n844:      * <p>\n845:      * If auto-commit is enabled, an async commit (based on the old assignment) will be triggered before the new\n846:      * assignment replaces the old one.\n847:      *\n848:      * @param partitions The list of partitions to assign this consumer\n849:      * @throws IllegalArgumentException If partitions is null or contains null or empty topics\n850:      * @throws IllegalStateException If {@code subscribe()} is called previously with topics or pattern\n851:      *                               (without a subsequent call to {@link #unsubscribe()})\n852:      */\n853:     @Override\n854:     public void assign(Collection<TopicPartition> partitions) {\n855:         delegate.assign(partitions);\n856:     }\n857: \n858:     /**\n859:      * Fetch data for the topics or partitions specified using one of the subscribe/assign APIs. It is an error to not have\n860:      * subscribed to any topics or partitions before polling for data.\n861:      * <p>\n862:      * On each poll, consumer will try to use the last consumed offset as the starting offset and fetch sequentially. The last\n863:      * consumed offset can be manually set through {@link #seek(TopicPartition, long)} or automatically set as the last committed\n864:      * offset for the subscribed list of partitions\n865:      *\n866:      * <p>\n867:      * This method returns immediately if there are records available or if the position advances past control records\n868:      * or aborted transactions when isolation.level=read_committed.\n869:      * Otherwise, it will await the passed timeout. If the timeout expires, an empty record set will be returned.\n870:      * Note that this method may block beyond the timeout in order to execute custom\n871:      * {@link ConsumerRebalanceListener} callbacks.\n872:      *\n873:      *\n874:      * @param timeout The maximum time to block (must not be greater than {@link Long#MAX_VALUE} milliseconds)\n875:      *\n876:      * @return map of topic to records since the last fetch for the subscribed list of topics and partitions\n877:      *\n878:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.clients.consumer.InvalidOffsetException if the offset for a partition or set of\n879:      *             partitions is undefined or out of range and no offset reset policy has been configured\n880:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.errors.WakeupException if {@link #wakeup()} is called before or while this\n881:      *             function is called\n882:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.errors.InterruptException if the calling thread is interrupted before or while\n883:      *             this function is called\n884:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.errors.AuthenticationException if authentication fails. See the exception for more details\n885:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.errors.AuthorizationException if caller lacks Read access to any of the subscribed\n886:      *             topics or to the configured groupId. See the exception for more details\n887:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.KafkaException for any other unrecoverable errors (e.g. invalid groupId or\n888:      *             session timeout, errors deserializing key/value pairs, your rebalance callback thrown exceptions,\n889:      *             or any new error cases in future versions)\n890:      * @throws java.lang.IllegalArgumentException if the timeout value is negative\n891:      * @throws java.lang.IllegalStateException if the consumer is not subscribed to any topics or manually assigned any\n892:      *             partitions to consume from\n893:      * @throws java.lang.ArithmeticException if the timeout is greater than {@link Long#MAX_VALUE} milliseconds.\n894:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.errors.InvalidTopicException if the current subscription contains any invalid\n895:      *             topic (per {@link org.apache.kafka.common.internals.Topic#validate(String)})\n896:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.errors.UnsupportedVersionException if the consumer attempts to fetch stable offsets\n897:      *             when the broker doesn't support this feature. Also, if the consumer attempts to subscribe to a\n898:      *             SubscriptionPattern via {@link #subscribe(SubscriptionPattern)} or\n899:      *             {@link #subscribe(SubscriptionPattern, ConsumerRebalanceListener)} and the broker doesn't\n900:      *             support this feature.\n901:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.errors.FencedInstanceIdException if this consumer instance gets fenced by broker.\n902:      */\n903:     @Override\n904:     public ConsumerRecords<K, V> poll(final Duration timeout) {\n905:         return delegate.poll(timeout);\n906:     }\n907: \n908:     /**\n909:      * Commit offsets returned on the last {@link #poll(Duration) poll()} for all the subscribed list of topics and\n910:      * partitions.\n911:      * <p>\n912:      * This commits offsets only to Kafka. The offsets committed using this API will be used on the first fetch after\n913:      * every rebalance and also on startup. As such, if you need to store offsets in anything other than Kafka, this API\n914:      * should not be used.\n915:      * <p>\n916:      * This is a synchronous commit and will block until either the commit succeeds, an unrecoverable error is\n917:      * encountered (in which case it is thrown to the caller), or the timeout specified by {@code default.api.timeout.ms} expires\n918:      * (in which case a {@link org.apache.kafka.common.errors.TimeoutException} is thrown to the caller).\n919:      * <p>\n920:      * Note that asynchronous offset commits sent previously with the {@link #commitAsync(OffsetCommitCallback)}\n921:      * (or similar) are guaranteed to have their callbacks invoked prior to completion of this method,\n922:      * but only when the consumer is using the consumer group protocol.\n923:      *\n924:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.clients.consumer.CommitFailedException if the commit failed and cannot be retried.\n925:      *             This fatal error can only occur if you are using automatic group management with {@link #subscribe(Collection)},\n926:      *             or if there is an active group with the same <code>group.id</code> which is using group management. In such cases,\n927:      *             when you are trying to commit to partitions that are no longer assigned to this consumer because the\n928:      *             consumer is for example no longer part of the group this exception would be thrown.\n929:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.errors.RebalanceInProgressException if the consumer instance is in the middle of a rebalance\n930:      *            so it is not yet determined which partitions would be assigned to the consumer. In such cases you can first\n931:      *            complete the rebalance by calling {@link #poll(Duration)} and commit can be reconsidered afterwards.\n932:      *            NOTE when you reconsider committing after the rebalance, the assigned partitions may have changed,\n933:      *            and also for those partitions that are still assigned their fetch positions may have changed too\n934:      *            if more records are returned from the {@link #poll(Duration)} call.\n935:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.errors.WakeupException if {@link #wakeup()} is called before or while this\n936:      *             function is called\n937:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.errors.InterruptException if the calling thread is interrupted before or while\n938:      *             this function is called\n939:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.errors.AuthenticationException if authentication fails. See the exception for more details\n940:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.errors.AuthorizationException if not authorized to the topic or to the\n941:      *             configured groupId. See the exception for more details\n942:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.KafkaException for any other unrecoverable errors (e.g. if offset metadata\n943:      *             is too large or if the topic does not exist).\n944:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.errors.TimeoutException if the timeout specified by {@code default.api.timeout.ms} expires\n945:      *            before successful completion of the offset commit\n946:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.errors.FencedInstanceIdException if this consumer is using the classic group protocol\n947:      *            and this instance gets fenced by broker.\n948:      */\n949:     @Override\n950:     public void commitSync() {\n951:         delegate.commitSync();\n952:     }\n953: \n954:     /**\n955:      * Commit offsets returned on the last {@link #poll(Duration) poll()} for all the subscribed list of topics and\n956:      * partitions.\n957:      * <p>\n958:      * This commits offsets only to Kafka. The offsets committed using this API will be used on the first fetch after\n959:      * every rebalance and also on startup. As such, if you need to store offsets in anything other than Kafka, this API\n960:      * should not be used.\n961:      * <p>\n962:      * This is a synchronous commit and will block until either the commit succeeds, an unrecoverable error is\n963:      * encountered (in which case it is thrown to the caller), or the passed timeout expires.\n964:      * <p>\n965:      * Note that asynchronous offset commits sent previously with the {@link #commitAsync(OffsetCommitCallback)}\n966:      * (or similar) are guaranteed to have their callbacks invoked prior to completion of this method,\n967:      * but only when the consumer is using the consumer group protocol.\n968:      *\n969:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.clients.consumer.CommitFailedException if the commit failed and cannot be retried.\n970:      *             This can only occur if you are using automatic group management with {@link #subscribe(Collection)},\n971:      *             or if there is an active group with the same <code>group.id</code> which is using group management. In such cases,\n972:      *             when you are trying to commit to partitions that are no longer assigned to this consumer because the\n973:      *             consumer is for example no longer part of the group this exception would be thrown.\n974:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.errors.RebalanceInProgressException if the consumer instance is in the middle of a rebalance\n975:      *            so it is not yet determined which partitions would be assigned to the consumer. In such cases you can first\n976:      *            complete the rebalance by calling {@link #poll(Duration)} and commit can be reconsidered afterwards.\n977:      *            NOTE when you reconsider committing after the rebalance, the assigned partitions may have changed,\n978:      *            and also for those partitions that are still assigned their fetch positions may have changed too\n979:      *            if more records are returned from the {@link #poll(Duration)} call.\n980:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.errors.WakeupException if {@link #wakeup()} is called before or while this\n981:      *             function is called\n982:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.errors.InterruptException if the calling thread is interrupted before or while\n983:      *             this function is called\n984:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.errors.AuthenticationException if authentication fails. See the exception for more details\n985:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.errors.AuthorizationException if not authorized to the topic or to the\n986:      *             configured groupId. See the exception for more details\n987:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.KafkaException for any other unrecoverable errors (e.g. if offset metadata\n988:      *             is too large or if the topic does not exist).\n989:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.errors.TimeoutException if the timeout expires before successful completion\n990:      *            of the offset commit\n991:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.errors.FencedInstanceIdException if this consumer is using the classic group protocol\n992:      *            and this instance gets fenced by broker.\n993:      */\n994:     @Override\n995:     public void commitSync(Duration timeout) {\n996:         delegate.commitSync(timeout);\n997:     }\n998: \n999:     /**\n1000:      * Commit the specified offsets for the specified list of topics and partitions.\n1001:      * <p>\n1002:      * This commits offsets to Kafka. The offsets committed using this API will be used on the first fetch after every\n1003:      * rebalance and also on startup. As such, if you need to store offsets in anything other than Kafka, this API\n1004:      * should not be used. The committed offset should be the next message your application will consume,\n1005:      * i.e. {@code nextRecordToBeProcessed.offset()} (or {@link ConsumerRecords#nextOffsets()}).\n1006:      * You should also add the leader epoch as commit metadata, which can be obtained from\n1007:      * {@link ConsumerRecord#leaderEpoch()} or {@link ConsumerRecords#nextOffsets()}.\n1008:      * If automatic group management with {@link #subscribe(Collection)} is used,\n1009:      * then the committed offsets must belong to the currently auto-assigned partitions.\n1010:      * <p>\n1011:      * This is a synchronous commit and will block until either the commit succeeds or an unrecoverable error is\n1012:      * encountered (in which case it is thrown to the caller), or the timeout specified by {@code default.api.timeout.ms} expires\n1013:      * (in which case a {@link org.apache.kafka.common.errors.TimeoutException} is thrown to the caller).\n1014:      * <p>\n1015:      * Note that asynchronous offset commits sent previously with the {@link #commitAsync(OffsetCommitCallback)}\n1016:      * (or similar) are guaranteed to have their callbacks invoked prior to completion of this method,\n1017:      * but only when the consumer is using the consumer group protocol.\n1018:      *\n1019:      * @param offsets A map of offsets by partition with associated metadata. This map will be copied internally, so it\n1020:      *                is safe to mutate the map after returning.\n1021:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.clients.consumer.CommitFailedException if the commit failed and cannot be retried.\n1022:      *             This can only occur if you are using automatic group management with {@link #subscribe(Collection)},\n1023:      *             or if there is an active group with the same <code>group.id</code> which is using group management. In such cases,\n1024:      *             when you are trying to commit to partitions that are no longer assigned to this consumer because the\n1025:      *             consumer is for example no longer part of the group this exception would be thrown.\n1026:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.errors.RebalanceInProgressException if the consumer instance is in the middle of a rebalance\n1027:      *            so it is not yet determined which partitions would be assigned to the consumer. In such cases you can first\n1028:      *            complete the rebalance by calling {@link #poll(Duration)} and commit can be reconsidered afterwards.\n1029:      *            NOTE when you reconsider committing after the rebalance, the assigned partitions may have changed,\n1030:      *            and also for those partitions that are still assigned their fetch positions may have changed too\n1031:      *            if more records are returned from the {@link #poll(Duration)} call, so when you retry committing\n1032:      *            you should consider updating the passed in {@code offset} parameter.\n1033:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.errors.WakeupException if {@link #wakeup()} is called before or while this\n1034:      *             function is called\n1035:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.errors.InterruptException if the calling thread is interrupted before or while\n1036:      *             this function is called\n1037:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.errors.AuthenticationException if authentication fails. See the exception for more details\n1038:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.errors.AuthorizationException if not authorized to the topic or to the\n1039:      *             configured groupId. See the exception for more details\n1040:      * @throws java.lang.IllegalArgumentException if the committed offset is negative\n1041:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.KafkaException for any other unrecoverable errors (e.g. if offset metadata\n1042:      *             is too large or if the topic does not exist).\n1043:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.errors.TimeoutException if the timeout expires before successful completion\n1044:      *            of the offset commit\n1045:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.errors.FencedInstanceIdException if this consumer is using the classic group protocol\n1046:      *            and this instance gets fenced by broker.\n1047:      */\n1048:     @Override\n1049:     public void commitSync(final Map<TopicPartition, OffsetAndMetadata> offsets) {\n1050:         delegate.commitSync(offsets);\n1051:     }\n1052: \n1053:     /**\n1054:      * Commit the specified offsets for the specified list of topics and partitions.\n1055:      * <p>\n1056:      * This commits offsets to Kafka. The offsets committed using this API will be used on the first fetch after every\n1057:      * rebalance and also on startup. As such, if you need to store offsets in anything other than Kafka, this API\n1058:      * should not be used. The committed offset should be the next message your application will consume,\n1059:      * i.e. {@code nextRecordToBeProcessed.offset()} (or {@link ConsumerRecords#nextOffsets()}).\n1060:      * You should also add the leader epoch as commit metadata, which can be obtained from\n1061:      * {@link ConsumerRecord#leaderEpoch()} or {@link ConsumerRecords#nextOffsets()}.\n1062:      * If automatic group management with {@link #subscribe(Collection)} is used,\n1063:      * then the committed offsets must belong to the currently auto-assigned partitions.\n1064:      * <p>\n1065:      * This is a synchronous commit and will block until either the commit succeeds, an unrecoverable error is\n1066:      * encountered (in which case it is thrown to the caller), or the timeout expires.\n1067:      * <p>\n1068:      * Note that asynchronous offset commits sent previously with the {@link #commitAsync(OffsetCommitCallback)}\n1069:      * (or similar) are guaranteed to have their callbacks invoked prior to completion of this method,\n1070:      * but only when the consumer is using the consumer group protocol.\n1071:      *\n1072:      * @param offsets A map of offsets by partition with associated metadata. This map will be copied internally, so it\n1073:      *                is safe to mutate the map after returning.\n1074:      * @param timeout The maximum amount of time to await completion of the offset commit\n1075:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.clients.consumer.CommitFailedException if the commit failed and cannot be retried.\n1076:      *             This can only occur if you are using automatic group management with {@link #subscribe(Collection)},\n1077:      *             or if there is an active group with the same <code>group.id</code> which is using group management. In such cases,\n1078:      *             when you are trying to commit to partitions that are no longer assigned to this consumer because the\n1079:      *             consumer is for example no longer part of the group this exception would be thrown.\n1080:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.errors.RebalanceInProgressException if the consumer instance is in the middle of a rebalance\n1081:      *            so it is not yet determined which partitions would be assigned to the consumer. In such cases you can first\n1082:      *            complete the rebalance by calling {@link #poll(Duration)} and commit can be reconsidered afterwards.\n1083:      *            NOTE when you reconsider committing after the rebalance, the assigned partitions may have changed,\n1084:      *            and also for those partitions that are still assigned their fetch positions may have changed too\n1085:      *            if more records are returned from the {@link #poll(Duration)} call, so when you retry committing\n1086:      *            you should consider updating the passed in {@code offset} parameter.\n1087:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.errors.WakeupException if {@link #wakeup()} is called before or while this\n1088:      *             function is called\n1089:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.errors.InterruptException if the calling thread is interrupted before or while\n1090:      *             this function is called\n1091:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.errors.AuthenticationException if authentication fails. See the exception for more details\n1092:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.errors.AuthorizationException if not authorized to the topic or to the\n1093:      *             configured groupId. See the exception for more details\n1094:      * @throws java.lang.IllegalArgumentException if the committed offset is negative\n1095:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.KafkaException for any other unrecoverable errors (e.g. if offset metadata\n1096:      *             is too large or if the topic does not exist).\n1097:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.errors.TimeoutException if the timeout expires before successful completion\n1098:      *            of the offset commit\n1099:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.errors.FencedInstanceIdException if this consumer is using the classic group protocol\n1100:      *            and this instance gets fenced by broker.\n1101:      */\n1102:     @Override\n1103:     public void commitSync(final Map<TopicPartition, OffsetAndMetadata> offsets, final Duration timeout) {\n1104:         delegate.commitSync(offsets, timeout);\n1105:     }\n1106: \n1107:     /**\n1108:      * Commit offsets returned on the last {@link #poll(Duration)} for all the subscribed list of topics and partition.\n1109:      * Same as {@link #commitAsync(OffsetCommitCallback) commitAsync(null)}\n1110:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.errors.FencedInstanceIdException if this consumer is using the classic group protocol\n1111:      *            and this instance gets fenced by broker.\n1112:      */\n1113:     @Override\n1114:     public void commitAsync() {\n1115:         delegate.commitAsync();\n1116:     }\n1117: \n1118:     /**\n1119:      * Commit offsets returned on the last {@link #poll(Duration) poll()} for the subscribed list of topics and partitions.\n1120:      * <p>\n1121:      * This commits offsets only to Kafka. The offsets committed using this API will be used on the first fetch after\n1122:      * every rebalance and also on startup. As such, if you need to store offsets in anything other than Kafka, this API\n1123:      * should not be used.\n1124:      * <p>\n1125:      * This is an asynchronous call and will not block. Any errors encountered are either passed to the callback\n1126:      * (if provided) or discarded.\n1127:      * <p>\n1128:      * Offsets committed through multiple calls to this API are guaranteed to be sent in the same order as\n1129:      * the invocations. Corresponding commit callbacks are also invoked in the same order. Additionally note that\n1130:      * offsets committed through this API are guaranteed to complete before a subsequent call to {@link #commitSync()}\n1131:      * (and variants) returns.\n1132:      *\n1133:      * @param callback Callback to invoke when the commit completes\n1134:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.errors.FencedInstanceIdException if this consumer is using the classic group protocol\n1135:      *             and this instance gets fenced by broker.\n1136:      */\n1137:     @Override\n1138:     public void commitAsync(OffsetCommitCallback callback) {\n1139:         delegate.commitAsync(callback);\n1140:     }\n1141: \n1142:     /**\n1143:      * Commit the specified offsets for the specified list of topics and partitions to Kafka.\n1144:      * <p>\n1145:      * This commits offsets to Kafka. The offsets committed using this API will be used on the first fetch after every\n1146:      * rebalance and also on startup. As such, if you need to store offsets in anything other than Kafka, this API\n1147:      * should not be used. The committed offset should be the next message your application will consume,\n1148:      * i.e. {@code nextRecordToBeProcessed.offset()} (or {@link ConsumerRecords#nextOffsets()}).\n1149:      * You should also add the leader epoch as commit metadata, which can be obtained from\n1150:      * {@link ConsumerRecord#leaderEpoch()} or {@link ConsumerRecords#nextOffsets()}.\n1151:      * If automatic group management with {@link #subscribe(Collection)} is used,\n1152:      * then the committed offsets must belong to the currently auto-assigned partitions.\n1153:      * <p>\n1154:      * This is an asynchronous call and will not block. Any errors encountered are either passed to the callback\n1155:      * (if provided) or discarded.\n1156:      * <p>\n1157:      * Offsets committed through multiple calls to this API are guaranteed to be sent in the same order as\n1158:      * the invocations. Corresponding commit callbacks are also invoked in the same order. Additionally note that\n1159:      * offsets committed through this API are guaranteed to complete before a subsequent call to {@link #commitSync()}\n1160:      * (and variants) returns.\n1161:      *\n1162:      * @param offsets A map of offsets by partition with associated metadata. This map will be copied internally, so it\n1163:      *                is safe to mutate the map after returning.\n1164:      * @param callback Callback to invoke when the commit completes\n1165:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.errors.FencedInstanceIdException if this consumer is using the classic group protocol\n1166:      *             and this instance gets fenced by broker.\n1167:      */\n1168:     @Override\n1169:     public void commitAsync(final Map<TopicPartition, OffsetAndMetadata> offsets, OffsetCommitCallback callback) {\n1170:         delegate.commitAsync(offsets, callback);\n1171:     }\n1172: \n1173:     /**\n1174:      * Overrides the fetch offsets that the consumer will use on the next {@link #poll(Duration) poll(timeout)}. If this API\n1175:      * is invoked for the same partition more than once, the latest offset will be used on the next poll(). Note that\n1176:      * you may lose data if this API is arbitrarily used in the middle of consumption, to reset the fetch offsets\n1177:      * <p>\n1178:      * The next Consumer Record which will be retrieved when poll() is invoked will have the offset specified, given that\n1179:      * a record with that offset exists (i.e. it is a valid offset).\n1180:      * <p>\n1181:      * {@link #seekToBeginning(Collection)} will go to the first offset in the topic.\n1182:      * seek(0) is equivalent to seekToBeginning for a TopicPartition with beginning offset 0,\n1183:      * assuming that there is a record at offset 0 still available.\n1184:      * {@link #seekToEnd(Collection)} is equivalent to seeking to the last offset of the partition, but behavior depends on\n1185:      * {@code isolation.level}, so see {@link #seekToEnd(Collection)} documentation for more details.\n1186:      * <p>\n1187:      * Seeking to the offset smaller than the log start offset or larger than the log end offset\n1188:      * means an invalid offset is reached.\n1189:      * Invalid offset behaviour is controlled by the {@code auto.offset.reset} property.\n1190:      * If this is set to \"earliest\", the next poll will return records from the starting offset.\n1191:      * If it is set to \"latest\", it will seek to the last offset (similar to seekToEnd()).\n1192:      * If it is set to \"none\", an {@code OffsetOutOfRangeException} will be thrown.\n1193:      * <p>\n1194:      * Note that, the seek offset won't change to the in-flight fetch request, it will take effect in next fetch request.\n1195:      * So, the consumer might wait for {@code fetch.max.wait.ms} before starting to fetch the records from desired offset.\n1196:      *\n1197:      * @param partition the TopicPartition on which the seek will be performed.\n1198:      * @param offset the next offset returned by poll().\n1199:      * @throws IllegalArgumentException if the provided offset is negative\n1200:      * @throws IllegalStateException if the provided TopicPartition is not assigned to this consumer\n1201:      */\n1202:     @Override\n1203:     public void seek(TopicPartition partition, long offset) {\n1204:         delegate.seek(partition, offset);\n1205:     }\n1206: \n1207:     /**\n1208:      * Overrides the fetch offsets that the consumer will use on the next {@link #poll(Duration) poll(timeout)}. If this API\n1209:      * is invoked for the same partition more than once, the latest offset will be used on the next poll(). Note that\n1210:      * you may lose data if this API is arbitrarily used in the middle of consumption, to reset the fetch offsets. This\n1211:      * method allows for setting the leaderEpoch along with the desired offset.\n1212:      *\n1213:      * @throws IllegalArgumentException if the provided offset is negative\n1214:      * @throws IllegalStateException if the provided TopicPartition is not assigned to this consumer\n1215:      */\n1216:     @Override\n1217:     public void seek(TopicPartition partition, OffsetAndMetadata offsetAndMetadata) {\n1218:         delegate.seek(partition, offsetAndMetadata);\n1219:     }\n1220: \n1221:     /**\n1222:      * Seek to the first offset for each of the given partitions. This function evaluates lazily, seeking to the\n1223:      * first offset in all partitions only when {@link #poll(Duration)} or {@link #position(TopicPartition)} are called.\n1224:      * If no partitions are provided, seek to the first offset for all of the currently assigned partitions.\n1225:      *\n1226:      * @throws IllegalArgumentException if {@code partitions} is {@code null}\n1227:      * @throws IllegalStateException if any of the provided partitions are not currently assigned to this consumer\n1228:      */\n1229:     @Override\n1230:     public void seekToBeginning(Collection<TopicPartition> partitions) {\n1231:         delegate.seekToBeginning(partitions);\n1232:     }\n1233: \n1234:     /**\n1235:      * Seek to the last offset for each of the given partitions. This function evaluates lazily, seeking to the\n1236:      * final offset in all partitions only when {@link #poll(Duration)} or {@link #position(TopicPartition)} are called.\n1237:      * If no partitions are provided, seek to the final offset for all of the currently assigned partitions.\n1238:      * <p>\n1239:      * If {@code isolation.level=read_committed}, the end offset will be the Last Stable Offset, i.e., the offset\n1240:      * of the first message with an open transaction.\n1241:      *\n1242:      * @throws IllegalArgumentException if {@code partitions} is {@code null}\n1243:      * @throws IllegalStateException if any of the provided partitions are not currently assigned to this consumer\n1244:      */\n1245:     @Override\n1246:     public void seekToEnd(Collection<TopicPartition> partitions) {\n1247:         delegate.seekToEnd(partitions);\n1248:     }\n1249: \n1250:     /**\n1251:      * Get the offset of the <i>next record</i> that will be fetched (if a record with that offset exists).\n1252:      * This method may issue a remote call to the server if there is no current position for the given partition.\n1253:      * <p>\n1254:      * This call will block until either the position could be determined or an unrecoverable error is\n1255:      * encountered (in which case it is thrown to the caller), or the timeout specified by {@code default.api.timeout.ms} expires\n1256:      * (in which case a {@link org.apache.kafka.common.errors.TimeoutException} is thrown to the caller).\n1257:      *\n1258:      * @param partition The partition to get the position for\n1259:      * @return The current position of the consumer (that is, the offset of the next record to be fetched)\n1260:      * @throws IllegalStateException if the provided TopicPartition is not assigned to this consumer\n1261:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.clients.consumer.InvalidOffsetException if no offset is currently defined for\n1262:      *             the partition\n1263:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.errors.WakeupException if {@link #wakeup()} is called before or while this\n1264:      *             function is called\n1265:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.errors.InterruptException if the calling thread is interrupted before or while\n1266:      *             this function is called\n1267:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.errors.AuthenticationException if authentication fails. See the exception for more details\n1268:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.errors.AuthorizationException if not authorized to the topic or to the\n1269:      *             configured groupId. See the exception for more details\n1270:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.errors.UnsupportedVersionException if the consumer attempts to fetch stable offsets\n1271:      *             when the broker doesn't support this feature\n1272:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.KafkaException for any other unrecoverable errors\n1273:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.errors.TimeoutException if the position cannot be determined before the\n1274:      *             timeout specified by {@code default.api.timeout.ms} expires\n1275:      */\n1276:     @Override\n1277:     public long position(TopicPartition partition) {\n1278:         return delegate.position(partition);\n1279:     }\n1280: \n1281:     /**\n1282:      * Get the offset of the <i>next record</i> that will be fetched (if a record with that offset exists).\n1283:      * This method may issue a remote call to the server if there is no current position\n1284:      * for the given partition.\n1285:      * <p>\n1286:      * This call will block until the position can be determined, an unrecoverable error is\n1287:      * encountered (in which case it is thrown to the caller), or the timeout expires.\n1288:      *\n1289:      * @param partition The partition to get the position for\n1290:      * @param timeout The maximum amount of time to await determination of the current position\n1291:      * @return The current position of the consumer (that is, the offset of the next record to be fetched)\n1292:      * @throws IllegalStateException if the provided TopicPartition is not assigned to this consumer\n1293:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.clients.consumer.InvalidOffsetException if no offset is currently defined for\n1294:      *             the partition\n1295:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.errors.WakeupException if {@link #wakeup()} is called before or while this\n1296:      *             function is called\n1297:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.errors.InterruptException if the calling thread is interrupted before or while\n1298:      *             this function is called\n1299:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.errors.TimeoutException if the position cannot be determined before the\n1300:      *             passed timeout expires\n1301:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.errors.AuthenticationException if authentication fails. See the exception for more details\n1302:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.errors.AuthorizationException if not authorized to the topic or to the\n1303:      *             configured groupId. See the exception for more details\n1304:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.KafkaException for any other unrecoverable errors\n1305:      */\n1306:     @Override\n1307:     public long position(TopicPartition partition, final Duration timeout) {\n1308:         return delegate.position(partition, timeout);\n1309:     }\n1310: \n1311:     /**\n1312:      * Get the last committed offsets for the given partitions (whether the commit happened by this process or\n1313:      * another). The returned offsets will be used as the position for the consumer in the event of a failure.\n1314:      * <p>\n1315:      * If any of the partitions requested do not exist, the result map will contain null as the value for that partition.\n1316:      * <p>\n1317:      * This call will do a remote call to get the latest committed offsets from the server, and will block until the\n1318:      * committed offsets are gotten successfully, an unrecoverable error is encountered (in which case it is thrown to\n1319:      * the caller), or the timeout specified by {@code default.api.timeout.ms} expires (in which case a\n1320:      * {@link org.apache.kafka.common.errors.TimeoutException} is thrown to the caller).\n1321:      *\n1322:      * @param partitions The partitions to check\n1323:      * @return The latest committed offsets for the given partitions; {@code null} will be returned for the\n1324:      *         partition if there is no such message.\n1325:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.errors.WakeupException if {@link #wakeup()} is called before or while this\n1326:      *             function is called\n1327:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.errors.InterruptException if the calling thread is interrupted before or while\n1328:      *             this function is called\n1329:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.errors.AuthenticationException if authentication fails. See the exception for more details\n1330:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.errors.AuthorizationException if not authorized to the topic or to the\n1331:      *             configured groupId. See the exception for more details\n1332:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.errors.UnsupportedVersionException if the consumer attempts to fetch stable offsets\n1333:      *             when the broker doesn't support this feature\n1334:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.KafkaException for any other unrecoverable errors\n1335:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.errors.TimeoutException if the committed offset cannot be found before\n1336:      *             the timeout specified by {@code default.api.timeout.ms} expires.\n1337:      */\n1338:     @Override\n1339:     public Map<TopicPartition, OffsetAndMetadata> committed(final Set<TopicPartition> partitions) {\n1340:         return delegate.committed(partitions);\n1341:     }\n1342: \n1343:     /**\n1344:      * Get the last committed offsets for the given partitions (whether the commit happened by this process or\n1345:      * another). The returned offsets will be used as the position for the consumer in the event of a failure.\n1346:      * <p>\n1347:      * If any of the partitions requested do not exist, the result map will contain null as the value for that partition.\n1348:      * <p>\n1349:      * This call will block to do a remote call to get the latest committed offsets from the server.\n1350:      *\n1351:      * @param partitions The partitions to check\n1352:      * @param timeout  The maximum amount of time to await the latest committed offsets\n1353:      * @return The latest committed offsets for the given partitions; {@code null} will be returned for the\n1354:      *         partition if there is no such message.\n1355:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.errors.WakeupException if {@link #wakeup()} is called before or while this\n1356:      *             function is called\n1357:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.errors.InterruptException if the calling thread is interrupted before or while\n1358:      *             this function is called\n1359:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.errors.AuthenticationException if authentication fails. See the exception for more details\n1360:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.errors.AuthorizationException if not authorized to the topic or to the\n1361:      *             configured groupId. See the exception for more details\n1362:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.KafkaException for any other unrecoverable errors\n1363:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.errors.TimeoutException if the committed offset cannot be found before\n1364:      *             expiration of the timeout\n1365:      */\n1366:     @Override\n1367:     public Map<TopicPartition, OffsetAndMetadata> committed(final Set<TopicPartition> partitions, final Duration timeout) {\n1368:         return delegate.committed(partitions, timeout);\n1369:     }\n1370: \n1371:     /**\n1372:      * Determines the client's unique client instance ID used for telemetry. This ID is unique to\n1373:      * this specific client instance and will not change after it is initially generated.\n1374:      * The ID is useful for correlating client operations with telemetry sent to the broker and\n1375:      * to its eventual monitoring destinations.\n1376:      * <p>\n1377:      * If telemetry is enabled, this will first require a connection to the cluster to generate\n1378:      * the unique client instance ID. This method waits up to {@code timeout} for the consumer\n1379:      * client to complete the request.\n1380:      * <p>\n1381:      * Client telemetry is controlled by the {@link ConsumerConfig#ENABLE_METRICS_PUSH_CONFIG}\n1382:      * configuration option.\n1383:      *\n1384:      * @param timeout The maximum time to wait for consumer client to determine its client instance ID.\n1385:      *                The value must be non-negative. Specifying a timeout of zero means do not\n1386:      *                wait for the initial request to complete if it hasn't already.\n1387:      * @throws InterruptException If the thread is interrupted while blocked.\n1388:      * @throws KafkaException If an unexpected error occurs while trying to determine the client\n1389:      *                        instance ID, though this error does not necessarily imply the\n1390:      *                        consumer client is otherwise unusable.\n1391:      * @throws IllegalArgumentException If the {@code timeout} is negative.\n1392:      * @throws IllegalStateException If telemetry is not enabled ie, config `{@code enable.metrics.push}`\n1393:      *                               is set to `{@code false}`.\n1394:      * @return The client's assigned instance id used for metrics collection.\n1395:      */\n1396:     @Override\n1397:     public Uuid clientInstanceId(Duration timeout) {\n1398:         return delegate.clientInstanceId(timeout);\n1399:     }\n1400: \n1401:   /**\n1402:      * Get the metrics kept by the consumer\n1403:      */\n1404:     @Override\n1405:     public Map<MetricName, ? extends Metric> metrics() {\n1406:         return delegate.metrics();\n1407:     }\n1408: \n1409:     /**\n1410:      * Get metadata about the partitions for a given topic. This method will issue a remote call to the server if it\n1411:      * does not already have any metadata about the given topic.\n1412:      *\n1413:      * @param topic The topic to get partition metadata for\n1414:      *\n1415:      * @return The list of partitions, which will be empty when the given topic is not found\n1416:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.errors.WakeupException if {@link #wakeup()} is called before or while this\n1417:      *             function is called\n1418:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.errors.InterruptException if the calling thread is interrupted before or while\n1419:      *             this function is called\n1420:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.errors.AuthenticationException if authentication fails. See the exception for more details\n1421:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.errors.AuthorizationException if not authorized to the specified topic. See the exception for more details\n1422:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.KafkaException for any other unrecoverable errors\n1423:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.errors.TimeoutException if the offset metadata could not be fetched before\n1424:      *         the amount of time allocated by {@code default.api.timeout.ms} expires.\n1425:      */\n1426:     @Override\n1427:     public List<PartitionInfo> partitionsFor(String topic) {\n1428:         return delegate.partitionsFor(topic);\n1429:     }\n1430: \n1431:     /**\n1432:      * Get metadata about the partitions for a given topic. This method will issue a remote call to the server if it\n1433:      * does not already have any metadata about the given topic.\n1434:      *\n1435:      * @param topic The topic to get partition metadata for\n1436:      * @param timeout The maximum of time to await topic metadata\n1437:      *\n1438:      * @return The list of partitions, which will be empty when the given topic is not found\n1439:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.errors.WakeupException if {@link #wakeup()} is called before or while this\n1440:      *             function is called\n1441:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.errors.InterruptException if the calling thread is interrupted before or while\n1442:      *             this function is called\n1443:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.errors.AuthenticationException if authentication fails. See the exception for more details\n1444:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.errors.AuthorizationException if not authorized to the specified topic. See\n1445:      *             the exception for more details\n1446:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.errors.TimeoutException if topic metadata cannot be fetched before expiration\n1447:      *             of the passed timeout\n1448:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.KafkaException for any other unrecoverable errors\n1449:      */\n1450:     @Override\n1451:     public List<PartitionInfo> partitionsFor(String topic, Duration timeout) {\n1452:         return delegate.partitionsFor(topic, timeout);\n1453:     }\n1454: \n1455:     /**\n1456:      * Get metadata about partitions for all topics that the user is authorized to view. This method will issue a\n1457:      * remote call to the server.\n1458: \n1459:      * @return The map of topics and its partitions\n1460:      *\n1461:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.errors.WakeupException if {@link #wakeup()} is called before or while this\n1462:      *             function is called\n1463:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.errors.InterruptException if the calling thread is interrupted before or while\n1464:      *             this function is called\n1465:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.KafkaException for any other unrecoverable errors\n1466:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.errors.TimeoutException if the offset metadata could not be fetched before\n1467:      *         the amount of time allocated by {@code default.api.timeout.ms} expires.\n1468:      */\n1469:     @Override\n1470:     public Map<String, List<PartitionInfo>> listTopics() {\n1471:         return delegate.listTopics();\n1472:     }\n1473: \n1474:     /**\n1475:      * Get metadata about partitions for all topics that the user is authorized to view. This method will issue a\n1476:      * remote call to the server.\n1477:      *\n1478:      * @param timeout The maximum time this operation will block to fetch topic metadata\n1479:      *\n1480:      * @return The map of topics and its partitions\n1481:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.errors.WakeupException if {@link #wakeup()} is called before or while this\n1482:      *             function is called\n1483:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.errors.InterruptException if the calling thread is interrupted before or while\n1484:      *             this function is called\n1485:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.errors.TimeoutException if the topic metadata could not be fetched before\n1486:      *             expiration of the passed timeout\n1487:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.KafkaException for any other unrecoverable errors\n1488:      */\n1489:     @Override\n1490:     public Map<String, List<PartitionInfo>> listTopics(Duration timeout) {\n1491:         return delegate.listTopics(timeout);\n1492:     }\n1493: \n1494:     /**\n1495:      * Suspend fetching from the requested partitions. Future calls to {@link #poll(Duration)} will not return\n1496:      * any records from these partitions until they have been resumed using {@link #resume(Collection)}.\n1497:      * Note that this method does not affect partition subscription. In particular, it does not cause a group\n1498:      * rebalance when automatic assignment is used.\n1499:      *\n1500:      * Note: Rebalance will not preserve the pause/resume state.\n1501:      * @param partitions The partitions which should be paused\n1502:      * @throws IllegalStateException if any of the provided partitions are not currently assigned to this consumer\n1503:      */\n1504:     @Override\n1505:     public void pause(Collection<TopicPartition> partitions) {\n1506:         delegate.pause(partitions);\n1507:     }\n1508: \n1509:     /**\n1510:      * Resume specified partitions which have been paused with {@link #pause(Collection)}. New calls to\n1511:      * {@link #poll(Duration)} will return records from these partitions if there are any to be fetched.\n1512:      * If the partitions were not previously paused, this method is a no-op.\n1513:      * @param partitions The partitions which should be resumed\n1514:      * @throws IllegalStateException if any of the provided partitions are not currently assigned to this consumer\n1515:      */\n1516:     @Override\n1517:     public void resume(Collection<TopicPartition> partitions) {\n1518:         delegate.resume(partitions);\n1519:     }\n1520: \n1521:     /**\n1522:      * Add the provided application metric for subscription.\n1523:      * This metric will be added to this client's metrics\n1524:      * that are available for subscription and sent as\n1525:      * telemetry data to the broker.\n1526:      * The provided metric must map to an OTLP metric data point\n1527:      * type in the OpenTelemetry v1 metrics protobuf message types.\n1528:      * Specifically, the metric should be one of the following:\n1529:      * <ul>\n1530:      *  <li>\n1531:      *     `Sum`: Monotonic total count meter (Counter). Suitable for metrics like total number of X, e.g., total bytes sent.\n1532:      *  </li>\n1533:      *  <li>\n1534:      *     `Gauge`: Non-monotonic current value meter (UpDownCounter).
