Suitable for metrics like current value of Y, e.g., current queue count.\n1535:      *  </li>\n1536:      * </ul>\n1537:      * Metrics not matching these types are silently ignored.\n1538:      * Executing this method for a previously registered metric is a benign operation and results in updating that metrics entry.\n1539:      *\n1540:      * @param metric The application metric to register\n1541:      */\n1542:     @Override\n1543:     public void registerMetricForSubscription(KafkaMetric metric) {\n1544:         delegate.registerMetricForSubscription(metric);\n1545:     }\n1546: \n1547:     /**\n1548:      * Remove the provided application metric for subscription.\n1549:      * This metric is removed from this client's metrics\n1550:      * and will not be available for subscription any longer.\n1551:      * Executing this method with a metric that has not been registered is a\n1552:      * benign operation and does not result in any action taken (no-op).\n1553:      *\n1554:      * @param metric The application metric to remove\n1555:      */\n1556:     @Override\n1557:     public void unregisterMetricFromSubscription(KafkaMetric metric) {\n1558:         delegate.unregisterMetricFromSubscription(metric);\n1559:     }\n1560: \n1561:     /**\n1562:      * Get the set of partitions that were previously paused by a call to {@link #pause(Collection)}.\n1563:      *\n1564:      * @return The set of paused partitions\n1565:      */\n1566:     @Override\n1567:     public Set<TopicPartition> paused() {\n1568:         return delegate.paused();\n1569:     }\n1570: \n1571:     /**\n1572:      * Look up the offsets for the given partitions by timestamp. The returned offset for each partition is the\n1573:      * earliest offset whose timestamp is greater than or equal to the given timestamp in the corresponding partition.\n1574:      *\n1575:      * This is a blocking call. The consumer does not have to be assigned the partitions.\n1576:      * If the message format version in a partition is before 0.10.0, i.e. the messages do not have timestamps, null\n1577:      * will be returned for that partition.\n1578:      *\n1579:      * @param timestampsToSearch the mapping from partition to the timestamp to look up.\n1580:      *\n1581:      * @return a mapping from partition to the timestamp and offset of the first message with timestamp greater\n1582:      *         than or equal to the target timestamp. If the timestamp and offset for a specific partition cannot be found within\n1583:      *         the default timeout, and no corresponding message exists, the entry in the returned map will be {@code null}\n1584:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.errors.AuthenticationException if authentication fails. See the exception for more details\n1585:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.errors.AuthorizationException if not authorized to the topic(s). See the exception for more details\n1586:      * @throws IllegalArgumentException if the target timestamp is negative\n1587:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.errors.TimeoutException if the offset metadata could not be fetched before\n1588:      *         the amount of time allocated by {@code default.api.timeout.ms} expires.\n1589:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.errors.UnsupportedVersionException if the broker does not support looking up\n1590:      *         the offsets by timestamp\n1591:      */\n1592:     @Override\n1593:     public Map<TopicPartition, OffsetAndTimestamp> offsetsForTimes(Map<TopicPartition, Long> timestampsToSearch) {\n1594:         return delegate.offsetsForTimes(timestampsToSearch);\n1595:     }\n1596: \n1597:     /**\n1598:      * Look up the offsets for the given partitions by timestamp. The returned offset for each partition is the\n1599:      * earliest offset whose timestamp is greater than or equal to the given timestamp in the corresponding partition.\n1600:      *\n1601:      * This is a blocking call. The consumer does not have to be assigned the partitions.\n1602:      * If the message format version in a partition is before 0.10.0, i.e. the messages do not have timestamps, null\n1603:      * will be returned for that partition.\n1604:      *\n1605:      * @param timestampsToSearch the mapping from partition to the timestamp to look up.\n1606:      * @param timeout The maximum amount of time to await retrieval of the offsets\n1607:      *\n1608:      * @return a mapping from partition to the timestamp and offset of the first message with timestamp greater\n1609:      *         than or equal to the target timestamp. If the timestamp and offset for a specific partition cannot be found within\n1610:      *         timeout, and no corresponding message exists, the entry in the returned map will be {@code null}\n1611:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.errors.AuthenticationException if authentication fails. See the exception for more details\n1612:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.errors.AuthorizationException if not authorized to the topic(s). See the exception for more details\n1613:      * @throws IllegalArgumentException if the target timestamp is negative\n1614:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.errors.TimeoutException if the offset metadata could not be fetched before\n1615:      *         expiration of the passed timeout\n1616:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.errors.UnsupportedVersionException if the broker does not support looking up\n1617:      *         the offsets by timestamp\n1618:      */\n1619:     @Override\n1620:     public Map<TopicPartition, OffsetAndTimestamp> offsetsForTimes(Map<TopicPartition, Long> timestampsToSearch, Duration timeout) {\n1621:         return delegate.offsetsForTimes(timestampsToSearch, timeout);\n1622:     }\n1623: \n1624:     /**\n1625:      * Get the first offset for the given partitions.\n1626:      * <p>\n1627:      * This method does not change the current consumer position of the partitions.\n1628:      *\n1629:      * @see #seekToBeginning(Collection)\n1630:      *\n1631:      * @param partitions the partitions to get the earliest offsets.\n1632:      * @return The earliest available offsets for the given partitions\n1633:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.errors.AuthenticationException if authentication fails. See the exception for more details\n1634:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.errors.AuthorizationException if not authorized to the topic(s). See the exception for more details\n1635:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.errors.TimeoutException if the offset metadata could not be fetched before\n1636:      *         expiration of the configured {@code default.api.timeout.ms}\n1637:      */\n1638:     @Override\n1639:     public Map<TopicPartition, Long> beginningOffsets(Collection<TopicPartition> partitions) {\n1640:         return delegate.beginningOffsets(partitions);\n1641:     }\n1642: \n1643:     /**\n1644:      * Get the first offset for the given partitions.\n1645:      * <p>\n1646:      * This method does not change the current consumer position of the partitions.\n1647:      *\n1648:      * @see #seekToBeginning(Collection)\n1649:      *\n1650:      * @param partitions the partitions to get the earliest offsets\n1651:      * @param timeout The maximum amount of time to await retrieval of the beginning offsets\n1652:      *\n1653:      * @return The earliest available offsets for the given partitions, and it will return empty map if zero timeout is provided\n1654:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.errors.AuthenticationException if authentication fails. See the exception for more details\n1655:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.errors.AuthorizationException if not authorized to the topic(s). See the exception for more details\n1656:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.errors.TimeoutException if the offset metadata could not be fetched before\n1657:      *         expiration of the passed timeout\n1658:      */\n1659:     @Override\n1660:     public Map<TopicPartition, Long> beginningOffsets(Collection<TopicPartition> partitions, Duration timeout) {\n1661:         return delegate.beginningOffsets(partitions, timeout);\n1662:     }\n1663: \n1664:     /**\n1665:      * Get the end offsets for the given partitions. In the default {@code read_uncommitted} isolation level, the end\n1666:      * offset is the high watermark (that is, the offset of the last successfully replicated message plus one). For\n1667:      * {@code read_committed} consumers, the end offset is the last stable offset (LSO), which is the minimum of\n1668:      * the high watermark and the smallest offset of any open transaction. Finally, if the partition has never been\n1669:      * written to, the end offset is 0.\n1670:      *\n1671:      * <p>\n1672:      * This method does not change the current consumer position of the partitions.\n1673:      *\n1674:      * @see #seekToEnd(Collection)\n1675:      *\n1676:      * @param partitions the partitions to get the end offsets.\n1677:      * @return The end offsets for the given partitions.\n1678:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.errors.AuthenticationException if authentication fails. See the exception for more details\n1679:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.errors.AuthorizationException if not authorized to the topic(s). See the exception for more details\n1680:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.errors.TimeoutException if the offset metadata could not be fetched before\n1681:      *         the amount of time allocated by {@code default.api.timeout.ms} expires\n1682:      */\n1683:     @Override\n1684:     public Map<TopicPartition, Long> endOffsets(Collection<TopicPartition> partitions) {\n1685:         return delegate.endOffsets(partitions);\n1686:     }\n1687: \n1688:     /**\n1689:      * Get the end offsets for the given partitions. In the default {@code read_uncommitted} isolation level, the end\n1690:      * offset is the high watermark (that is, the offset of the last successfully replicated message plus one). For\n1691:      * {@code read_committed} consumers, the end offset is the last stable offset (LSO), which is the minimum of\n1692:      * the high watermark and the smallest offset of any open transaction. Finally, if the partition has never been\n1693:      * written to, the end offset is 0.\n1694:      *\n1695:      * <p>\n1696:      * This method does not change the current consumer position of the partitions.\n1697:      *\n1698:      * @see #seekToEnd(Collection)\n1699:      *\n1700:      * @param partitions the partitions to get the end offsets.\n1701:      * @param timeout The maximum amount of time to await retrieval of the end offsets\n1702:      *\n1703:      * @return The end offsets for the given partitions, and it will return empty map if zero timeout is provided\n1704:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.errors.AuthenticationException if authentication fails. See the exception for more details\n1705:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.errors.AuthorizationException if not authorized to the topic(s). See the exception for more details\n1706:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.errors.TimeoutException if the offsets could not be fetched before\n1707:      *         expiration of the passed timeout\n1708:      */\n1709:     @Override\n1710:     public Map<TopicPartition, Long> endOffsets(Collection<TopicPartition> partitions, Duration timeout) {\n1711:         return delegate.endOffsets(partitions, timeout);\n1712:     }\n1713: \n1714:     /**\n1715:      * Get the consumer's current lag on the partition. Returns an \"empty\" {@link OptionalLong} if the lag is not known,\n1716:      * for example if there is no position yet, or if the end offset is not known yet.\n1717:      *\n1718:      * <p>\n1719:      * This method uses locally cached metadata. If the log end offset is not known yet, it triggers a request to fetch\n1720:      * the log end offset, but returns immediately.\n1721:      *\n1722:      * @param topicPartition The partition to get the lag for.\n1723:      *\n1724:      * @return This {@code Consumer} instance's current lag for the given partition.\n1725:      *\n1726:      * @throws IllegalStateException if the {@code topicPartition} is not assigned\n1727:      */\n1728:     @Override\n1729:     public OptionalLong currentLag(TopicPartition topicPartition) {\n1730:         return delegate.currentLag(topicPartition);\n1731:     }\n1732: \n1733:     /**\n1734:      * Return the current group metadata associated with this consumer.\n1735:      *\n1736:      * @return consumer group metadata\n1737:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.errors.InvalidGroupIdException if consumer does not have a group\n1738:      */\n1739:     @Override\n1740:     public ConsumerGroupMetadata groupMetadata() {\n1741:         return delegate.groupMetadata();\n1742:     }\n1743: \n1744:     /**\n1745:      * Alert the consumer to trigger a new rebalance by rejoining the group. This is a nonblocking call that forces\n1746:      * the consumer to trigger a new rebalance on the next {@link #poll(Duration)} call. Note that this API does not\n1747:      * itself initiate the rebalance, so you must still call {@link #poll(Duration)}. If a rebalance is already in\n1748:      * progress this call will be a no-op. If you wish to force an additional rebalance you must complete the current\n1749:      * one by calling poll before retrying this API.\n1750:      * <p>\n1751:      * You do not need to call this during normal processing, as the consumer group will manage itself\n1752:      * automatically and rebalance when necessary. However there may be situations where the application wishes to\n1753:      * trigger a rebalance that would otherwise not occur. For example, if some condition external and invisible to\n1754:      * the Consumer and its group changes in a way that would affect the userdata encoded in the\n1755:      * {@link org.apache.kafka.clients.consumer.ConsumerPartitionAssignor.Subscription Subscription}, the Consumer\n1756:      * will not be notified and no rebalance will occur. This API can be used to force the group to rebalance so that\n1757:      * the assignor can perform a partition reassignment based on the latest userdata. If your assignor does not use\n1758:      * this userdata, or you do not use a custom\n1759:      * {@link org.apache.kafka.clients.consumer.ConsumerPartitionAssignor ConsumerPartitionAssignor}, you should not\n1760:      * use this API.\n1761:      *\n1762:      * @param reason The reason why the new rebalance is needed.\n1763:      *\n1764:      * @throws java.lang.IllegalStateException if the consumer does not use group subscription\n1765:      */\n1766:     @Override\n1767:     public void enforceRebalance(final String reason) {\n1768:         delegate.enforceRebalance(reason);\n1769:     }\n1770: \n1771:     /**\n1772:      * @see #enforceRebalance(String)\n1773:      */\n1774:     @Override\n1775:     public void enforceRebalance() {\n1776:         delegate.enforceRebalance();\n1777:     }\n1778: \n1779:     /**\n1780:      * Close the consumer with {@link CloseOptions.GroupMembershipOperation#DEFAULT default leave group behavior},\n1781:      * waiting for up to the default timeout of 30 seconds for any needed cleanup.\n1782:      * If auto-commit is enabled, this will commit the current offsets if possible within the default\n1783:      * timeout. See {@link #close(CloseOptions)} for details. Note that {@link #wakeup()}\n1784:      * cannot be used to interrupt close.\n1785:      * <p>\n1786:      * This close operation will attempt all shutdown steps even if one of them fails.\n1787:      * It logs all encountered errors, continues to execute the next steps, and finally throws the first error found.\n1788:      *\n1789:      * @throws WakeupException    if {@link #wakeup()} is called before or while this function is called\n1790:      * @throws InterruptException if the calling thread is interrupted before or while this function is called\n1791:      * @throws KafkaException     for any other error during close\n1792:      *                            (e.g., errors thrown from rebalance callbacks or commit callbacks from previous asynchronous commits)\n1793:      */\n1794:     @Override\n1795:     public void close() {\n1796:         delegate.close();\n1797:     }\n1798: \n1799:     /**\n1800:      * This method has been deprecated since Kafka 4.1 and should use {@link KafkaConsumer#close(CloseOptions)} instead.\n1801:      * <p>\n1802:      * Close the consumer with {@link CloseOptions.GroupMembershipOperation#DEFAULT default leave group behavior}\n1803:      * cleanly within the specified timeout. This method waits up to\n1804:      * {@code timeout} for the consumer to complete pending commits and maybe leave the group (if the member is dynamic).\n1805:      * If auto-commit is enabled, this will commit the current offsets if possible within the\n1806:      * timeout. If the consumer is unable to complete offset commits and to gracefully leave the group (if applicable)\n1807:      * before the timeout expires, the consumer is force closed. Note that {@link #wakeup()} cannot be\n1808:      * used to interrupt close.\n1809:      * <p>\n1810:      * The actual maximum wait time is bounded by the {@link ConsumerConfig#REQUEST_TIMEOUT_MS_CONFIG} setting, which\n1811:      * only applies to operations performed with the broker (coordinator-related requests and\n1812:      * fetch sessions). Even if a larger timeout is specified, the consumer will not wait longer than\n1813:      * {@link ConsumerConfig#REQUEST_TIMEOUT_MS_CONFIG} for these requests to complete during the close operation.\n1814:      * Note that the execution time of callbacks (such as {@link OffsetCommitCallback} and\n1815:      * {@link ConsumerRebalanceListener}) does not consume time from the close timeout.\n1816:      * <p>\n1817:      * This close operation will attempt all shutdown steps even if one of them fails.\n1818:      * It logs all encountered errors, continues to execute the next steps, and finally throws the first error found.\n1819:      *\n1820:      * @param timeout The maximum time to wait for consumer to close gracefully. The value must be\n1821:      *                non-negative. Specifying a timeout of zero means do not wait for pending requests to complete.\n1822:      * @throws IllegalArgumentException If the {@code timeout} is negative.\n1823:      * @throws WakeupException          if {@link #wakeup()} is called before or while this function is called\n1824:      * @throws InterruptException       if the calling thread is interrupted before or while this function is called\n1825:      * @throws KafkaException           for any other error during close\n1826:      *                                  (e.g., errors thrown from rebalance callbacks or commit callbacks from previous asynchronous commits)\n1827:      */\n1828:     @Deprecated(since = \"4.1\")\n1829:     @Override\n1830:     public void close(Duration timeout) {\n1831:         delegate.close(timeout);\n1832:     }\n1833: \n1834:     /**\n1835:      * Close the consumer cleanly. {@link CloseOptions} allows to specify a timeout and a\n1836:      * {@link CloseOptions.GroupMembershipOperation leave group behavior}.\n1837:      * If no timeout is specified, the default timeout of 30 seconds is used.\n1838:      * If no leave group behavior is specified, the {@link CloseOptions.GroupMembershipOperation#DEFAULT default\n1839:      * leave group behavior} is used.\n1840:      * <p>\n1841:      * This method waits up to the timeout for the consumer to complete pending commits and maybe leave the group,\n1842:      * depending on the specified leave group behavior.\n1843:      * If auto-commit is enabled, this will commit the current offsets if possible within the\n1844:      * timeout. If the consumer is unable to complete offset commits and to gracefully leave the group (if applicable)\n1845:      * before the timeout expires, the consumer is force closed. Note that {@link #wakeup()} cannot be\n1846:      * used to interrupt close.\n1847:      * <p>\n1848:      * The actual maximum wait time is bounded by the {@link ConsumerConfig#REQUEST_TIMEOUT_MS_CONFIG} setting, which\n1849:      * only applies to operations performed with the broker (coordinator-related requests and\n1850:      * fetch sessions). Even if a larger timeout is specified, the consumer will not wait longer than\n1851:      * {@link ConsumerConfig#REQUEST_TIMEOUT_MS_CONFIG} for these requests to complete during the close operation.\n1852:      * Note that the execution time of callbacks (such as {@link OffsetCommitCallback} and\n1853:      * {@link ConsumerRebalanceListener}) does not consume time from the close timeout.\n1854:      * <p>\n1855:      * This close operation will attempt all shutdown steps even if one of them fails.\n1856:      * It logs all encountered errors, continues to execute the next steps, and finally throws the first error found.\n1857:      *\n1858:      * @param option see {@link CloseOptions}; cannot be {@code null}\n1859:      * @throws IllegalArgumentException If the {@code option} timeout is negative\n1860:      * @throws WakeupException          if {@link #wakeup()} is called before or while this function is called\n1861:      * @throws InterruptException       if the calling thread is interrupted before or while this function is called\n1862:      * @throws KafkaException           for any other error during close\n1863:      *                                  (e.g., errors thrown from rebalance callbacks or commit callbacks from previous asynchronous commits)\n1864:      */\n1865:     @Override\n1866:     public void close(CloseOptions option) {\n1867:         delegate.close(option);\n1868:     }\n1869: \n1870:     /**\n1871:      * Wakeup the consumer. This method is thread-safe and is useful in particular to abort a long poll.\n1872:      * The thread which is blocking in an operation will throw {@link org.apache.kafka.common.errors.WakeupException}.\n1873:      * If no thread is blocking in a method which can throw {@link org.apache.kafka.common.errors.WakeupException}, the next call to such a method will raise it instead.\n1874:      */\n1875:     @Override\n1876:     public void wakeup() {\n1877:         delegate.wakeup();\n1878:     }\n1879: \n1880:     // Functions below are for testing only\n1881:     String clientId() {\n1882:         return delegate.clientId();\n1883:     }\n1884: \n1885:     Metrics metricsRegistry() {\n1886:         return delegate.metricsRegistry();\n1887:     }\n1888: \n1889:     KafkaConsumerMetrics kafkaConsumerMetrics() {\n1890:         return delegate.kafkaConsumerMetrics();\n1891:     }\n1892: \n1893:     boolean updateAssignmentMetadataIfNeeded(final Timer timer) {\n1894:         return delegate.updateAssignmentMetadataIfNeeded(timer);\n1895:     }\n1896: }\n1897: ",
    "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-benchmarks/kafka--e678b4b@47ac12bd6042edf23f1dbca3d789db945d62802d/-/blob/clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/clients/consumer/KafkaConsumer.java"
  }
}
13. Bash @ -

  "content": "{\"content\":\"1: /*\\n2:  * Licensed to the Apache Software Foundation (ASF) under one or more\\n3:  * contributor license agreements. See the NOTICE file distributed with\\n4:  * this work for additional information regarding copyright ownership.\\n5:  * The ASF licenses this file to You under the Apache License, Version 2.0\\n6:  * (the \\\"License\\\"); you may not use this file except in compliance with\\n7:  * the License. You may obtain a copy of the License at\\n8:  *\\n9:  *    http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\\n10:  *\\n11:  * Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\\n12:  * distributed under the License is distributed on an \\\"AS IS\\\" BASIS,\\n13:  * WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\\n14:  * See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\\n15:  * limitations under the License.\\n16:  */\\n17: package org.apache.kafka.clients.consumer;\\n18: \\n19: import org.apache.kafka.clients.KafkaClient;\\n20: import org.apache.kafka.clients.consumer.internals.ConsumerDelegate;\\n21: import org.apache.kafka.clients.consumer.internals.ConsumerDelegateCreator;\\n22: import org.apache.kafka.clients.consumer.internals.ConsumerMetadata;\\n23: import org.apache.kafka.clients.consumer.internals.SubscriptionState;\\n24: import org.apache.kafka.clients.consumer.internals.metrics.KafkaConsumerMetrics;\\n25: import org.apache.kafka.common.KafkaException;\\n26: import org.apache.kafka.common.Metric;\\n27: import org.apache.kafka.common.MetricName;\\n28: import org.apache.kafka.common.PartitionInfo;\\n29: import org.apache.kafka.common.TopicPartition;\\n30: import org.apache.kafka.common.Uuid;\\n31: import org.apache.kafka.common.errors.InterruptException;\\n32: import org.apache.kafka.common.errors.InvalidRegularExpression;\\n33: import org.apache.kafka.common.errors.WakeupException;\\n34: import org.apache.kafka.common.metrics.KafkaMetric;\\n35: import org.apache.kafka.common.metrics.Metrics;\\n36: import org.apache.kafka.common.serialization.Deserializer;\\n37: import org.apache.kafka.common.utils.LogContext;\\n38: import org.apache.kafka.common.utils.Time;\\n39: import org.apache.kafka.common.utils.Timer;\\n40: \\n41: import java.time.Duration;\\n42: import java.util.Collection;\\n43: import java.util.ConcurrentModificationException;\\n44: import java.util.List;\\n45: import java.util.Map;\\n46: import java.util.OptionalLong;\\n47: import java.util.Properties;\\n48: import java.util.Set;\\n49: import java.util.regex.Pattern;\\n50: \\n51: import static org.apache.kafka.common.utils.Utils.propsToMap;\\n52: \\n53: /**\\n54:  * A client that consumes records from a Kafka cluster.\\n55:  * <p>\\n56:  * This client transparently handles the failure of Kafka brokers, and transparently adapts as topic partitions\\n57:  * it fetches migrate within the cluster. This client also interacts with the broker to allow groups of\\n58:  * consumers to load balance consumption using <a href=\\\"#consumergroups\\\">consumer groups</a>.\\n59:  * <p>\\n60:  * The consumer maintains TCP connections to the necessary brokers to fetch data.\\n61:  * Failure to close the consumer after use will leak these connections.\\n62:  * The consumer is not thread-safe. See <a href=\\\"#multithreaded\\\">Multi-threaded Processing</a> for more details.\\n63:  *\\n64:  * <h3>Cross-Version Compatibility</h3>\\n65:  * This client can communicate with brokers that are version 0.10.0 or newer. Older or newer brokers may not support\\n66:  * certain features. For example, 0.10.0 brokers do not support offsetsForTimes, because this feature was added\\n67:  * in version 0.10.1. You will receive an {@link org.apache.kafka.common.errors.UnsupportedVersionException}\\n68:  * when invoking an API that is not available on the running broker version.\\n69:  * <p>\\n70:  *\\n71:  * <h3>Offsets and Consumer Position</h3>\\n72:  * Kafka maintains a numerical offset for each record in a partition. This offset acts as a unique identifier of\\n73:  * a record within that partition, and also denotes the position of the consumer in the partition. For example, a consumer\\n74:  * which is at position 5 has consumed records with offsets 0 through 4 and will next receive the record with offset 5.\\n75:  * Note that offsets are not guaranteed to be consecutive (such as compacted topic or when records have been produced\\n76:  * using transactions). For example, if the consumer did read a record with offset 4, but 5 is not an offset\\n77:  * with a record, its position might advance to 6 (or higher) directly. Similarly, if the consumer's position is 5,\\n78:  * but there is no record with offset 5, the consumer will return the record with the next higher offset.\\n79:  * There are actually two notions of position relevant to the user of the consumer:\\n80:  * <p>\\n81:  * The {@link #position(TopicPartition) position} of the consumer gives the offset of the next record that will be given\\n82:  * out. It will be one larger than the highest offset the consumer has seen in that partition. It automatically advances\\n83:  * every time the consumer receives messages in a call to {@link #poll(Duration)}.\\n84:  * <p>\\n85:  * The {@link #commitSync() committed position} is the last offset that has been stored securely. Should the\\n86:  * process fail and restart, this is the offset that the consumer will recover to. The consumer can either automatically commit\\n87:  * offsets periodically; or it can choose to control this committed position manually by calling one of the commit APIs\\n88:  * (e.g. {@link #commitSync() commitSync} and {@link #commitAsync(OffsetCommitCallback) commitAsync}).\\n89:  * <p>\\n90:  * This distinction gives the consumer control over when a record is considered consumed. It is discussed in further\\n91:  * detail below.\\n92:  *\\n93:  * <h3><a name=\\\"consumergroups\\\">Consumer Groups and Topic Subscriptions</a></h3>\\n94:  *\\n95:  * Kafka uses the concept of <i>consumer groups</i> to allow a pool of processes to divide the work of consuming and\\n96:  * processing records. These processes can either be running on the same machine or they can be\\n97:  * distributed over many machines to provide scalability and fault tolerance for processing. All consumer instances\\n98:  * sharing the same {@code group.id} will be part of the same consumer group.\\n99:  * <p>\\n100:  * Each consumer in a group can dynamically set the list of topics it wants to subscribe to through one of the\\n101: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-benchmarks/kafka--e678b4b@47ac12bd6042edf23f1dbca3d789db945d62802d/-/blob/clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/clients/consumer/KafkaConsumer.java%3FL1-100\"}",
  "structuredContent": {
    "content": "1: /*\n2:  * Licensed to the Apache Software Foundation (ASF) under one or more\n3:  * contributor license agreements. See the NOTICE file distributed with\n4:  * this work for additional information regarding copyright ownership.\n5:  * The ASF licenses this file to You under the Apache License, Version 2.0\n6:  * (the \"License\"); you may not use this file except in compliance with\n7:  * the License. You may obtain a copy of the License at\n8:  *\n9:  *    http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\n10:  *\n11:  * Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\n12:  * distributed under the License is distributed on an \"AS IS\" BASIS,\n13:  * WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\n14:  * See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\n15:  * limitations under the License.\n16:  */\n17: package org.apache.kafka.clients.consumer;\n18: \n19: import org.apache.kafka.clients.KafkaClient;\n20: import org.apache.kafka.clients.consumer.internals.ConsumerDelegate;\n21: import org.apache.kafka.clients.consumer.internals.ConsumerDelegateCreator;\n22: import org.apache.kafka.clients.consumer.internals.ConsumerMetadata;\n23: import org.apache.kafka.clients.consumer.internals.SubscriptionState;\n24: import org.apache.kafka.clients.consumer.internals.metrics.KafkaConsumerMetrics;\n25: import org.apache.kafka.common.KafkaException;\n26: import org.apache.kafka.common.Metric;\n27: import org.apache.kafka.common.MetricName;\n28: import org.apache.kafka.common.PartitionInfo;\n29: import org.apache.kafka.common.TopicPartition;\n30: import org.apache.kafka.common.Uuid;\n31: import org.apache.kafka.common.errors.InterruptException;\n32: import org.apache.kafka.common.errors.InvalidRegularExpression;\n33: import org.apache.kafka.common.errors.WakeupException;\n34: import org.apache.kafka.common.metrics.KafkaMetric;\n35: import org.apache.kafka.common.metrics.Metrics;\n36: import org.apache.kafka.common.serialization.Deserializer;\n37: import org.apache.kafka.common.utils.LogContext;\n38: import org.apache.kafka.common.utils.Time;\n39: import org.apache.kafka.common.utils.Timer;\n40: \n41: import java.time.Duration;\n42: import java.util.Collection;\n43: import java.util.ConcurrentModificationException;\n44: import java.util.List;\n45: import java.util.Map;\n46: import java.util.OptionalLong;\n47: import java.util.Properties;\n48: import java.util.Set;\n49: import java.util.regex.Pattern;\n50: \n51: import static org.apache.kafka.common.utils.Utils.propsToMap;\n52: \n53: /**\n54:  * A client that consumes records from a Kafka cluster.\n55:  * <p>\n56:  * This client transparently handles the failure of Kafka brokers, and transparently adapts as topic partitions\n57:  * it fetches migrate within the cluster. This client also interacts with the broker to allow groups of\n58:  * consumers to load balance consumption using <a href=\"#consumergroups\">consumer groups</a>.\n59:  * <p>\n60:  * The consumer maintains TCP connections to the necessary brokers to fetch data.\n61:  * Failure to close the consumer after use will leak these connections.\n62:  * The consumer is not thread-safe. See <a href=\"#multithreaded\">Multi-threaded Processing</a> for more details.\n63:  *\n64:  * <h3>Cross-Version Compatibility</h3>\n65:  * This client can communicate with brokers that are version 0.10.0 or newer. Older or newer brokers may not support\n66:  * certain features. For example, 0.10.0 brokers do not support offsetsForTimes, because this feature was added\n67:  * in version 0.10.1. You will receive an {@link org.apache.kafka.common.errors.UnsupportedVersionException}\n68:  * when invoking an API that is not available on the running broker version.\n69:  * <p>\n70:  *\n71:  * <h3>Offsets and Consumer Position</h3>\n72:  * Kafka maintains a numerical offset for each record in a partition. This offset acts as a unique identifier of\n73:  * a record within that partition, and also denotes the position of the consumer in the partition. For example, a consumer\n74:  * which is at position 5 has consumed records with offsets 0 through 4 and will next receive the record with offset 5.\n75:  * Note that offsets are not guaranteed to be consecutive (such as compacted topic or when records have been produced\n76:  * using transactions). For example, if the consumer did read a record with offset 4, but 5 is not an offset\n77:  * with a record, its position might advance to 6 (or higher) directly. Similarly, if the consumer's position is 5,\n78:  * but there is no record with offset 5, the consumer will return the record with the next higher offset.\n79:  * There are actually two notions of position relevant to the user of the consumer:\n80:  * <p>\n81:  * The {@link #position(TopicPartition) position} of the consumer gives the offset of the next record that will be given\n82:  * out. It will be one larger than the highest offset the consumer has seen in that partition. It automatically advances\n83:  * every time the consumer receives messages in a call to {@link #poll(Duration)}.\n84:  * <p>\n85:  * The {@link #commitSync() committed position} is the last offset that has been stored securely. Should the\n86:  * process fail and restart, this is the offset that the consumer will recover to. The consumer can either automatically commit\n87:  * offsets periodically; or it can choose to control this committed position manually by calling one of the commit APIs\n88:  * (e.g. {@link #commitSync() commitSync} and {@link #commitAsync(OffsetCommitCallback) commitAsync}).\n89:  * <p>\n90:  * This distinction gives the consumer control over when a record is considered consumed. It is discussed in further\n91:  * detail below.\n92:  *\n93:  * <h3><a name=\"consumergroups\">Consumer Groups and Topic Subscriptions</a></h3>\n94:  *\n95:  * Kafka uses the concept of <i>consumer groups</i> to allow a pool of processes to divide the work of consuming and\n96:  * processing records. These processes can either be running on the same machine or they can be\n97:  * distributed over many machines to provide scalability and fault tolerance for processing. All consumer instances\n98:  * sharing the same {@code group.id} will be part of the same consumer group.\n99:  * <p>\n100:  * Each consumer in a group can dynamically set the list of topics it wants to subscribe to through one of the\n101: ",
    "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-benchmarks/kafka--e678b4b@47ac12bd6042edf23f1dbca3d789db945d62802d/-/blob/clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/clients/consumer/KafkaConsumer.java%3FL1-100"
  }
}

  "content": [
    {
      "text": "Perfect! I've now read the entire KafkaConsumer.java file. Let me compile a comprehensive analysis of the information requested.\n\n## KafkaConsumer.java - Complete Analysis\n\n### 1. CLASS-LEVEL JAVADOC: Main Behavioral Semantics\n\n**File Location:** `/clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/clients/consumer/KafkaConsumer.java`\n\nThe KafkaConsumer is a client that consumes records from a Kafka cluster with the following key behavioral characteristics:\n\n- **Failure Handling:** Transparently handles failure of Kafka brokers and adapts as topic partitions migrate within the cluster\n- **Group Coordination:** Interacts with brokers to allow groups of consumers to load balance consumption using consumer groups\n- **Connection Management:** Maintains TCP connections to necessary brokers; connections leak if consumer is not closed\n- **Thread Safety:** NOT thread-safe. Multi-threaded access must be synchronized. `wakeup()` is the only exception\n- **Broker Compatibility:** Can communicate with brokers version 0.10.0 or newer; may throw UnsupportedVersionException for unsupported features\n- **Offset Management:** Maintains numerical offsets for records; supports both automatic and manual offset commits\n- **Consumer Groups:** Allows multiple consumer instances sharing the same group.id to divide work via dynamic rebalancing\n- **Isolation Levels:** Supports read_uncommitted (default) and read_committed modes for transactional messages\n\n---\n\n### 2. CONSTRUCTOR VARIANTS\n\n```java\n// Public Constructors\n\n// 1. With configuration map only\npublic KafkaConsumer(Map<String, Object> configs)\n\n// 2. With Properties object\npublic KafkaConsumer(Properties properties)\n\n// 3. With Properties and deserializers\npublic KafkaConsumer(Properties properties,\n                     Deserializer<K> keyDeserializer,\n                     Deserializer<V> valueDeserializer)\n\n// 4. With configuration map and deserializers\npublic KafkaConsumer(Map<String, Object> configs,\n                     Deserializer<K> keyDeserializer,\n                     Deserializer<V> valueDeserializer)\n\n// Package-private constructors (for testing/framework use):\n\n// 5. With ConsumerConfig and deserializers\nKafkaConsumer(ConsumerConfig config,\n              Deserializer<K> keyDeserializer,\n              Deserializer<V> valueDeserializer)\n\n// 6. Full constructor with all dependencies (testing)\nKafkaConsumer(LogContext logContext,\n              Time time,\n              ConsumerConfig config,\n              Deserializer<K> keyDeserializer,\n              Deserializer<V> valueDeserializer,\n              KafkaClient client,\n              SubscriptionState subscriptions,\n              ConsumerMetadata metadata,\n              List<ConsumerPartitionAssignor> assignors)\n```\n\n**Key Constructor Notes:**\n- All public constructors must be followed by `close()` to avoid resource leaks\n- Deserializers passed directly won't have their `configure()` method called by the consumer\n- Configuration strings are documented at `ConsumerConfig`\n\n---\n\n### 3. PUBLIC METHODS - COMPLETE SIGNATURES\n\n#### **Subscription and Assignment Methods**\n\n```java\nSet<TopicPartition> assignment()\nSet<String> subscription()\n\nvoid subscribe(Collection<String> topics, ConsumerRebalanceListener listener)\nvoid subscribe(Collection<String> topics)\nvoid subscribe(Pattern pattern, ConsumerRebalanceListener listener)\nvoid subscribe(Pattern pattern)\nvoid subscribe(SubscriptionPattern pattern, ConsumerRebalanceListener listener)\nvoid subscribe(SubscriptionPattern pattern)\n\nvoid unsubscribe()\nvoid assign(Collection<TopicPartition> partitions)\n```\n\n#### **Polling and Fetching**\n\n```java\nConsumerRecords<K, V> poll(final Duration timeout)\n```\n\n#### **Offset Commit Methods (Synchronous)**\n\n```java\nvoid commitSync()\nvoid commitSync(Duration timeout)\nvoid commitSync(final Map<TopicPartition, OffsetAndMetadata> offsets)\nvoid commitSync(final Map<TopicPartition, OffsetAndMetadata> offsets, final Duration timeout)\n```\n\n#### **Offset Commit Methods (Asynchronous)**\n\n```java\nvoid commitAsync()\nvoid commitAsync(OffsetCommitCallback callback)\nvoid commitAsync(final Map<TopicPartition, OffsetAndMetadata> offsets, OffsetCommitCallback callback)\n```\n\n#### **Seeking and Position Management**\n\n```java\nvoid seek(TopicPartition partition, long offset)\nvoid seek(TopicPartition partition, OffsetAndMetadata offsetAndMetadata)\nvoid seekToBeginning(Collection<TopicPartition> partitions)\nvoid seekToEnd(Collection<TopicPartition> partitions)\n\nlong position(TopicPartition partition)\nlong position(TopicPartition partition, final Duration timeout)\n```\n\n#### **Offset Lookup Methods**\n\n```java\nMap<TopicPartition, OffsetAndMetadata> committed(final Set<TopicPartition> partitions)\nMap<TopicPartition, OffsetAndMetadata> committed(final Set<TopicPartition> partitions, final Duration timeout)\n\nMap<TopicPartition, OffsetAndTimestamp> offsetsForTimes(Map<TopicPartition, Long> timestampsToSearch)\nMap<TopicPartition, OffsetAndTimestamp> offsetsForTimes(Map<TopicPartition, Long> timestampsToSearch, Duration timeout)\n\nMap<TopicPartition, Long> beginningOffsets(Collection<TopicPartition> partitions)\nMap<TopicPartition, Long> beginningOffsets(Collection<TopicPartition> partitions, Duration timeout)\n\nMap<TopicPartition, Long> endOffsets(Collection<TopicPartition> partitions)\nMap<TopicPartition, Long> endOffsets(Collection<TopicPartition> partitions, Duration timeout)\n```\n\n#### **Metadata and Topic Information**\n\n```java\nList<PartitionInfo> partitionsFor(String topic)\nList<PartitionInfo> partitionsFor(String topic, Duration timeout)\nMap<String, List<PartitionInfo>> listTopics()\nMap<String, List<PartitionInfo>> listTopics(Duration timeout)\n```\n\n#### **Pause and Resume**\n\n```java\nvoid pause(Collection<TopicPartition> partitions)\nvoid resume(Collection<TopicPartition> partitions)\nSet<TopicPartition> paused()\n```\n\n#### **Metrics and Monitoring**\n\n```java\nMap<MetricName, ? extends Metric> metrics()\nUuid clientInstanceId(Duration timeout)\nOptionalLong currentLag(TopicPartition topicPartition)\n```\n\n#### **Telemetry**\n\n```java\nvoid registerMetricForSubscription(KafkaMetric metric)\nvoid unregisterMetricFromSubscription(KafkaMetric metric)\n```\n\n#### **Group Management**\n\n```java\nConsumerGroupMetadata groupMetadata()\nvoid enforceRebalance(final String reason)\nvoid enforceRebalance()\n```\n\n#### **Lifecycle Methods**\n\n```java\nvoid close()\nvoid close(Duration timeout)                    // @Deprecated since 4.1\nvoid close(CloseOptions option)\nvoid wakeup()\n```\n\n---\n\n### 4. KEY CONFIGURATION PROPERTIES AFFECTING BEHAVIOR\n\n#### **Subscription & Assignment**\n\n| Property | Default | Impact |\n|----------|---------|--------|\n| `group.id` | null | Consumer group identifier for group coordination and offset management |\n| `partition.assignment.strategy` | RangeAssignor | Determines how partitions are distributed among consumer group members |\n| `GROUP_PROTOCOL_CONFIG` | - | Determines protocol: \"classic\" or \"consumer\" (affects SubscriptionPattern support) |\n\n#### **Offset Management**\n\n| Property | Default | Impact |\n|----------|---------|--------|\n| `enable.auto.commit` | true | Automatically commits offsets at intervals |\n| `auto.commit.interval.ms` | 5000 | Frequency of automatic offset commits |\n| `auto.offset.reset` | \"latest\" | Behavior when offset is out of range: \"earliest\", \"latest\", or \"none\" |\n| `isolation.level` | \"read_uncommitted\" | \"read_committed\" for transactional message consumption |\n\n#### **Polling & Fetching**\n\n| Property | Default | Impact |\n|----------|---------|--------|\n| `max.poll.interval.ms` | 300000 | Max time between polls before consumer leaves group |\n| `max.poll.records` | 500 | Max records returned per poll() call |\n| `fetch.min.bytes` | 1 | Min bytes server waits to accumulate before responding |\n| `fetch.max.wait.ms` | 500 | Max server wait time for fetch request |\n| `session.timeout.ms` | 45000 | Time before consumer is considered dead if heartbeats not sent |\n| `heartbeat.interval.ms` | 10000 | How often to send heartbeats to broker |\n\n#### **Connection & Request**\n\n| Property | Default | Impact |\n|----------|---------|--------|\n| `bootstrap.servers` | - | Initial broker addresses |\n| `default.api.timeout.ms` | 60000 | Default timeout for most operations |\n| `request.timeout.ms` | 30000 | Timeout for broker requests during close |\n| `reconnect.backoff.ms` | 50 | Backoff for reconnection attempts |\n\n#### **Deserialization**\n\n| Property | Default | Impact |\n|----------|---------|--------|\n| `key.deserializer` | - | Class implementing Deserializer for keys |\n| `value.deserializer` | - | Class implementing Deserializer for values |\n\n#### **Metadata & Rebalancing**\n\n| Property | Default | Impact |\n|----------|---------|--------|\n| `metadata.max.age.ms` | 300000 | How often to refresh metadata for pattern subscription |\n| `client.id` | - | Client identifier for logging/tracking |\n\n#### **Metrics & Telemetry**\n\n| Property | Default | Impact |\n|----------|---------|--------|\n| `metrics.num.samples` | 2 | Number of samples to keep for metrics |\n| `metrics.recording.level` | \"INFO\" | Metrics recording level |\n| `enable.metrics.push` | false | Enable client telemetry push to broker |\n\n---\n\n### 5. BEHAVIORAL SEMANTICS SUMMARY\n\n**Consumer Lifecycle:**\n1. Create instance with configuration\n2. Subscribe to topics via `subscribe()` or manually assign via `assign()`\n3. Poll repeatedly with `poll(Duration)` to fetch records\n4. Optionally commit offsets via `commitSync()` or `commitAsync()`\n5. Close with `close()` to release resources\n\n**Offset Tracking:**\n- **Position**: Offset of next record to fetch (auto-advances on poll)\n- **Committed Position**: Last offset stored durably; used on consumer restart\n- Manual control via `seek()`, `seekToBeginning()`, `seekToEnd()`\n\n**Rebalancing:**\n- Triggered by topic/partition changes, consumer group changes\n- Only occurs during `poll()` calls\n- Callbacks invoked via `ConsumerRebalanceListener` if provided\n\n**Error Handling:**\n- Synchronous methods block until completion or timeout\n- Asynchronous commits invoke callbacks with results\n- `wakeup()` can interrupt blocking operations from another thread\n- `InterruptException` supported but `wakeup()` preferred\n\nThis completes the comprehensive exploration of KafkaConsumer.java.",
      "type": "text"
    }
  ],

A list of ALL public methods with their signatures\n2. Constructor variants\n3. The main behavioral semantics documented in class-level javadoc\n4. Key configuration properties that affect behavior\n\nBe thorough and extract complete method signatures with parameters and return types. Return results as structured markdown.",
