Output
"{\"content\":\"1000:      * Commit the specified offsets for the specified list of topics and partitions.\\n1001:      * <p>\\n1002:      * This commits offsets to Kafka. The offsets committed using this API will be used on the first fetch after every\\n1003:      * rebalance and also on startup. As such, if you need to store offsets in anything other than Kafka, this API\\n1004:      * should not be used. The committed offset should be the next message your application will consume,\\n1005:      * i.e. {@code nextRecordToBeProcessed.offset()} (or {@link ConsumerRecords#nextOffsets()}).\\n1006:      * You should also add the leader epoch as commit metadata, which can be obtained from\\n1007:      * {@link ConsumerRecord#leaderEpoch()} or {@link ConsumerRecords#nextOffsets()}.\\n1008:      * If automatic group management with {@link #subscribe(Collection)} is used,\\n1009:      * then the committed offsets must belong to the currently auto-assigned partitions.\\n1010:      * <p>\\n1011:      * This is a synchronous commit and will block until either the commit succeeds or an unrecoverable error is\\n1012:      * encountered (in which case it is thrown to the caller), or the timeout specified by {@code default.api.timeout.ms} expires\\n1013:      * (in which case a {@link org.apache.kafka.common.errors.TimeoutException} is thrown to the caller).\\n1014:      * <p>\\n1015:      * Note that asynchronous offset commits sent previously with the {@link #commitAsync(OffsetCommitCallback)}\\n1016:      * (or similar) are guaranteed to have their callbacks invoked prior to completion of this method,\\n1017:      * but only when the consumer is using the consumer group protocol.\\n1018:      *\\n1019:      * @param offsets A map of offsets by partition with associated metadata. This map will be copied internally, so it\\n1020:      *                is safe to mutate the map after returning.\\n1021:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.clients.consumer.CommitFailedException if the commit failed and cannot be retried.\\n1022:      *             This can only occur if you are using automatic group management with {@link #subscribe(Collection)},\\n1023:      *             or if there is an active group with the same <code>group.id</code> which is using group management. In such cases,\\n1024:      *             when you are trying to commit to partitions that are no longer assigned to this consumer because the\\n1025:      *             consumer is for example no longer part of the group this exception would be thrown.\\n1026:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.errors.RebalanceInProgressException if the consumer instance is in the middle of a rebalance\\n1027:      *            so it is not yet determined which partitions would be assigned to the consumer. In such cases you can first\\n1028:      *            complete the rebalance by calling {@link #poll(Duration)} and commit can be reconsidered afterwards.\\n1029:      *            NOTE when you reconsider committing after the rebalance, the assigned partitions may have changed,\\n1030:      *            and also for those partitions that are still assigned their fetch positions may have changed too\\n1031:      *            if more records are returned from the {@link #poll(Duration)} call, so when you retry committing\\n1032:      *            you should consider updating the passed in {@code offset} parameter.\\n1033:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.errors.WakeupException if {@link #wakeup()} is called before or while this\\n1034:      *             function is called\\n1035:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.errors.InterruptException if the calling thread is interrupted before or while\\n1036:      *             this function is called\\n1037:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.errors.AuthenticationException if authentication fails. See the exception for more details\\n1038:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.errors.AuthorizationException if not authorized to the topic or to the\\n1039:      *             configured groupId. See the exception for more details\\n1040:      * @throws java.lang.IllegalArgumentException if the committed offset is negative\\n1041:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.KafkaException for any other unrecoverable errors (e.g. if offset metadata\\n1042:      *             is too large or if the topic does not exist).\\n1043:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.errors.TimeoutException if the timeout expires before successful completion\\n1044:      *            of the offset commit\\n1045:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.errors.FencedInstanceIdException if this consumer is using the classic group protocol\\n1046:      *            and this instance gets fenced by broker.\\n1047:      */\\n1048:     @Override\\n1049:     public void commitSync(final Map<TopicPartition, OffsetAndMetadata> offsets) {\\n1050:         delegate.commitSync(offsets);\\n1051:     }\\n1052: \\n1053:     /**\\n1054:      * Commit the specified offsets for the specified list of topics and partitions.\\n1055:      * <p>\\n1056:      * This commits offsets to Kafka. The offsets committed using this API will be used on the first fetch after every\\n1057:      * rebalance and also on startup. As such, if you need to store offsets in anything other than Kafka, this API\\n1058:      * should not be used. The committed offset should be the next message your application will consume,\\n1059:      * i.e. {@code nextRecordToBeProcessed.offset()} (or {@link ConsumerRecords#nextOffsets()}).\\n1060:      * You should also add the leader epoch as commit metadata, which can be obtained from\\n1061:      * {@link ConsumerRecord#leaderEpoch()} or {@link ConsumerRecords#nextOffsets()}.\\n1062:      * If automatic group management with {@link #subscribe(Collection)} is used,\\n1063:      * then the committed offsets must belong to the currently auto-assigned partitions.\\n1064:      * <p>\\n1065:      * This is a synchronous commit and will block until either the commit succeeds, an unrecoverable error is\\n1066:      * encountered (in which case it is thrown to the caller), or the timeout expires.\\n1067:      * <p>\\n1068:      * Note that asynchronous offset commits sent previously with the {@link #commitAsync(OffsetCommitCallback)}\\n1069:      * (or similar) are guaranteed to have their callbacks invoked prior to completion of this method,\\n1070:      * but only when the consumer is using the consumer group protocol.\\n1071:      *\\n1072:      * @param offsets A map of offsets by partition with associated metadata. This map will be copied internally, so it\\n1073:      *                is safe to mutate the map after returning.\\n1074:      * @param timeout The maximum amount of time to await completion of the offset commit\\n1075:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.clients.consumer.CommitFailedException if the commit failed and cannot be retried.\\n1076:      *             This can only occur if you are using automatic group management with {@link #subscribe(Collection)},\\n1077:      *             or if there is an active group with the same <code>group.id</code> which is using group management. In such cases,\\n1078:      *             when you are trying to commit to partitions that are no longer assigned to this consumer because the\\n1079:      *             consumer is for example no longer part of the group this exception would be thrown.\\n1080:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.errors.RebalanceInProgressException if the consumer instance is in the middle of a rebalance\\n1081:      *            so it is not yet determined which partitions would be assigned to the consumer. In such cases you can first\\n1082:      *            complete the rebalance by calling {@link #poll(Duration)} and commit can be reconsidered afterwards.\\n1083:      *            NOTE when you reconsider committing after the rebalance, the assigned partitions may have changed,\\n1084:      *            and also for those partitions that are still assigned their fetch positions may have changed too\\n1085:      *            if more records are returned from the {@link #poll(Duration)} call, so when you retry committing\\n1086:      *            you should consider updating the passed in {@code offset} parameter.\\n1087:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.errors.WakeupException if {@link #wakeup()} is called before or while this\\n1088:      *             function is called\\n1089:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.errors.InterruptException if the calling thread is interrupted before or while\\n1090:      *             this function is called\\n1091:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.errors.AuthenticationException if authentication fails. See the exception for more details\\n1092:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.errors.AuthorizationException if not authorized to the topic or to the\\n1093:      *             configured groupId. See the exception for more details\\n1094:      * @throws java.lang.IllegalArgumentException if the committed offset is negative\\n1095:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.KafkaException for any other unrecoverable errors (e.g. if offset metadata\\n1096:      *             is too large or if the topic does not exist).\\n1097:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.errors.TimeoutException if the timeout expires before successful completion\\n1098:      *            of the offset commit\\n1099:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.errors.FencedInstanceIdException if this consumer is using the classic group protocol\\n1100:      *            and this instance gets fenced by broker.\\n1101:      */\\n1102:     @Override\\n1103:     public void commitSync(final Map<TopicPartition, OffsetAndMetadata> offsets, final Duration timeout) {\\n1104:         delegate.commitSync(offsets, timeout);\\n1105:     }\\n1106: \\n1107:     /**\\n1108:      * Commit offsets returned on the last {@link #poll(Duration)} for all the subscribed list of topics and partition.\\n1109:      * Same as {@link #commitAsync(OffsetCommitCallback) commitAsync(null)}\\n1110:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.errors.FencedInstanceIdException if this consumer is using the classic group protocol\\n1111:      *            and this instance gets fenced by broker.\\n1112:      */\\n1113:     @Override\\n1114:     public void commitAsync() {\\n1115:         delegate.commitAsync();\\n1116:     }\\n1117: \\n1118:     /**\\n1119:      * Commit offsets returned on the last {@link #poll(Duration) poll()} for the subscribed list of topics and partitions.\\n1120:      * <p>\\n1121:      * This commits offsets only to Kafka. The offsets committed using this API will be used on the first fetch after\\n1122:      * every rebalance and also on startup. As such, if you need to store offsets in anything other than Kafka, this API\\n1123:      * should not be used.\\n1124:      * <p>\\n1125:      * This is an asynchronous call and will not block. Any errors encountered are either passed to the callback\\n1126:      * (if provided) or discarded.\\n1127:      * <p>\\n1128:      * Offsets committed through multiple calls to this API are guaranteed to be sent in the same order as\\n1129:      * the invocations. Corresponding commit callbacks are also invoked in the same order. Additionally note that\\n1130:      * offsets committed through this API are guaranteed to complete before a subsequent call to {@link #commitSync()}\\n1131:      * (and variants) returns.\\n1132:      *\\n1133:      * @param callback Callback to invoke when the commit completes\\n1134:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.errors.FencedInstanceIdException if this consumer is using the classic group protocol\\n1135:      *             and this instance gets fenced by broker.\\n1136:      */\\n1137:     @Override\\n1138:     public void commitAsync(OffsetCommitCallback callback) {\\n1139:         delegate.commitAsync(callback);\\n1140:     }\\n1141: \\n1142:     /**\\n1143:      * Commit the specified offsets for the specified list of topics and partitions to Kafka.\\n1144:      * <p>\\n1145:      * This commits offsets to Kafka. The offsets committed using this API will be used on the first fetch after every\\n1146:      * rebalance and also on startup. As such, if you need to store offsets in anything other than Kafka, this API\\n1147:      * should not be used. The committed offset should be the next message your application will consume,\\n1148:      * i.e. {@code nextRecordToBeProcessed.offset()} (or {@link ConsumerRecords#nextOffsets()}).\\n1149:      * You should also add the leader epoch as commit metadata, which can be obtained from\\n1150:      * {@link ConsumerRecord#leaderEpoch()} or {@link ConsumerRecords#nextOffsets()}.\\n1151:      * If automatic group management with {@link #subscribe(Collection)} is used,\\n1152:      * then the committed offsets must belong to the currently auto-assigned partitions.\\n1153:      * <p>\\n1154:      * This is an asynchronous call and will not block. Any errors encountered are either passed to the callback\\n1155:      * (if provided) or discarded.\\n1156:      * <p>\\n1157:      * Offsets committed through multiple calls to this API are guaranteed to be sent in the same order as\\n1158:      * the invocations. Corresponding commit callbacks are also invoked in the same order. Additionally note that\\n1159:      * offsets committed through this API are guaranteed to complete before a subsequent call to {@link #commitSync()}\\n1160:      * (and variants) returns.\\n1161:      *\\n1162:      * @param offsets A map of offsets by partition with associated metadata. This map will be copied internally, so it\\n1163:      *                is safe to mutate the map after returning.\\n1164:      * @param callback Callback to invoke when the commit completes\\n1165:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.errors.FencedInstanceIdException if this consumer is using the classic group protocol\\n1166:      *             and this instance gets fenced by broker.\\n1167:      */\\n1168:     @Override\\n1169:     public void commitAsync(final Map<TopicPartition, OffsetAndMetadata> offsets, OffsetCommitCallback callback) {\\n1170:         delegate.commitAsync(offsets, callback);\\n1171:     }\\n1172: \\n1173:     /**\\n1174:      * Overrides the fetch offsets that the consumer will use on the next {@link #poll(Duration) poll(timeout)}. If this API\\n1175:      * is invoked for the same partition more than once, the latest offset will be used on the next poll(). Note that\\n1176:      * you may lose data if this API is arbitrarily used in the middle of consumption, to reset the fetch offsets\\n1177:      * <p>\\n1178:      * The next Consumer Record which will be retrieved when poll() is invoked will have the offset specified, given that\\n1179:      * a record with that offset exists (i.e. it is a valid offset).\\n1180:      * <p>\\n1181:      * {@link #seekToBeginning(Collection)} will go to the first offset in the topic.\\n1182:      * seek(0) is equivalent to seekToBeginning for a TopicPartition with beginning offset 0,\\n1183:      * assuming that there is a record at offset 0 still available.\\n1184:      * {@link #seekToEnd(Collection)} is equivalent to seeking to the last offset of the partition, but behavior depends on\\n1185:      * {@code isolation.level}, so see {@link #seekToEnd(Collection)} documentation for more details.\\n1186:      * <p>\\n1187:      * Seeking to the offset smaller than the log start offset or larger than the log end offset\\n1188:      * means an invalid offset is reached.\\n1189:      * Invalid offset behaviour is controlled by the {@code auto.offset.reset} property.\\n1190:      * If this is set to \\\"earliest\\\", the next poll will return records from the starting offset.\\n1191:      * If it is set to \\\"latest\\\", it will seek to the last offset (similar to seekToEnd()).\\n1192:      * If it is set to \\\"none\\\", an {@code OffsetOutOfRangeException} will be thrown.\\n1193:      * <p>\\n1194:      * Note that, the seek offset won't change to the in-flight fetch request, it will take effect in next fetch request.\\n1195:      * So, the consumer might wait for {@code fetch.max.wait.ms} before starting to fetch the records from desired offset.\\n1196:      *\\n1197:      * @param partition the TopicPartition on which the seek will be performed.\\n1198:      * @param offset the next offset returned by poll().\\n1199:      * @throws IllegalArgumentException if the provided offset is negative\\n1200:      * @throws IllegalStateException if the provided TopicPartition is not assigned to this consumer\\n1201: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-benchmarks/kafka--e678b4b@47ac12bd6042edf23f1dbca3d789db945d62802d/-/blob/clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/clients/consumer/KafkaConsumer.java%3FL1000-1200\"}"

{
  "endLine": 1400,
  "path": "clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/clients/consumer/KafkaConsumer.java",

Output
"{\"content\":\"1200:      * @throws IllegalStateException if the provided TopicPartition is not assigned to this consumer\\n1201:      */\\n1202:     @Override\\n1203:     public void seek(TopicPartition partition, long offset) {\\n1204:         delegate.seek(partition, offset);\\n1205:     }\\n1206: \\n1207:     /**\\n1208:      * Overrides the fetch offsets that the consumer will use on the next {@link #poll(Duration) poll(timeout)}. If this API\\n1209:      * is invoked for the same partition more than once, the latest offset will be used on the next poll(). Note that\\n1210:      * you may lose data if this API is arbitrarily used in the middle of consumption, to reset the fetch offsets. This\\n1211:      * method allows for setting the leaderEpoch along with the desired offset.\\n1212:      *\\n1213:      * @throws IllegalArgumentException if the provided offset is negative\\n1214:      * @throws IllegalStateException if the provided TopicPartition is not assigned to this consumer\\n1215:      */\\n1216:     @Override\\n1217:     public void seek(TopicPartition partition, OffsetAndMetadata offsetAndMetadata) {\\n1218:         delegate.seek(partition, offsetAndMetadata);\\n1219:     }\\n1220: \\n1221:     /**\\n1222:      * Seek to the first offset for each of the given partitions. This function evaluates lazily, seeking to the\\n1223:      * first offset in all partitions only when {@link #poll(Duration)} or {@link #position(TopicPartition)} are called.\\n1224:      * If no partitions are provided, seek to the first offset for all of the currently assigned partitions.\\n1225:      *\\n1226:      * @throws IllegalArgumentException if {@code partitions} is {@code null}\\n1227:      * @throws IllegalStateException if any of the provided partitions are not currently assigned to this consumer\\n1228:      */\\n1229:     @Override\\n1230:     public void seekToBeginning(Collection<TopicPartition> partitions) {\\n1231:         delegate.seekToBeginning(partitions);\\n1232:     }\\n1233: \\n1234:     /**\\n1235:      * Seek to the last offset for each of the given partitions. This function evaluates lazily, seeking to the\\n1236:      * final offset in all partitions only when {@link #poll(Duration)} or {@link #position(TopicPartition)} are called.\\n1237:      * If no partitions are provided, seek to the final offset for all of the currently assigned partitions.\\n1238:      * <p>\\n1239:      * If {@code isolation.level=read_committed}, the end offset will be the Last Stable Offset, i.e., the offset\\n1240:      * of the first message with an open transaction.\\n1241:      *\\n1242:      * @throws IllegalArgumentException if {@code partitions} is {@code null}\\n1243:      * @throws IllegalStateException if any of the provided partitions are not currently assigned to this consumer\\n1244:      */\\n1245:     @Override\\n1246:     public void seekToEnd(Collection<TopicPartition> partitions) {\\n1247:         delegate.seekToEnd(partitions);\\n1248:     }\\n1249: \\n1250:     /**\\n1251:      * Get the offset of the <i>next record</i> that will be fetched (if a record with that offset exists).\\n1252:      * This method may issue a remote call to the server if there is no current position for the given partition.\\n1253:      * <p>\\n1254:      * This call will block until either the position could be determined or an unrecoverable error is\\n1255:      * encountered (in which case it is thrown to the caller), or the timeout specified by {@code default.api.timeout.ms} expires\\n1256:      * (in which case a {@link org.apache.kafka.common.errors.TimeoutException} is thrown to the caller).\\n1257:      *\\n1258:      * @param partition The partition to get the position for\\n1259:      * @return The current position of the consumer (that is, the offset of the next record to be fetched)\\n1260:      * @throws IllegalStateException if the provided TopicPartition is not assigned to this consumer\\n1261:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.clients.consumer.InvalidOffsetException if no offset is currently defined for\\n1262:      *             the partition\\n1263:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.errors.WakeupException if {@link #wakeup()} is called before or while this\\n1264:      *             function is called\\n1265:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.errors.InterruptException if the calling thread is interrupted before or while\\n1266:      *             this function is called\\n1267:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.errors.AuthenticationException if authentication fails. See the exception for more details\\n1268:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.errors.AuthorizationException if not authorized to the topic or to the\\n1269:      *             configured groupId. See the exception for more details\\n1270:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.errors.UnsupportedVersionException if the consumer attempts to fetch stable offsets\\n1271:      *             when the broker doesn't support this feature\\n1272:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.KafkaException for any other unrecoverable errors\\n1273:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.errors.TimeoutException if the position cannot be determined before the\\n1274:      *             timeout specified by {@code default.api.timeout.ms} expires\\n1275:      */\\n1276:     @Override\\n1277:     public long position(TopicPartition partition) {\\n1278:         return delegate.position(partition);\\n1279:     }\\n1280: \\n1281:     /**\\n1282:      * Get the offset of the <i>next record</i> that will be fetched (if a record with that offset exists).\\n1283:      * This method may issue a remote call to the server if there is no current position\\n1284:      * for the given partition.\\n1285:      * <p>\\n1286:      * This call will block until the position can be determined, an unrecoverable error is\\n1287:      * encountered (in which case it is thrown to the caller), or the timeout expires.\\n1288:      *\\n1289:      * @param partition The partition to get the position for\\n1290:      * @param timeout The maximum amount of time to await determination of the current position\\n1291:      * @return The current position of the consumer (that is, the offset of the next record to be fetched)\\n1292:      * @throws IllegalStateException if the provided TopicPartition is not assigned to this consumer\\n1293:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.clients.consumer.InvalidOffsetException if no offset is currently defined for\\n1294:      *             the partition\\n1295:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.errors.WakeupException if {@link #wakeup()} is called before or while this\\n1296:      *             function is called\\n1297:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.errors.InterruptException if the calling thread is interrupted before or while\\n1298:      *             this function is called\\n1299:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.errors.TimeoutException if the position cannot be determined before the\\n1300:      *             passed timeout expires\\n1301:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.errors.AuthenticationException if authentication fails. See the exception for more details\\n1302:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.errors.AuthorizationException if not authorized to the topic or to the\\n1303:      *             configured groupId. See the exception for more details\\n1304:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.KafkaException for any other unrecoverable errors\\n1305:      */\\n1306:     @Override\\n1307:     public long position(TopicPartition partition, final Duration timeout) {\\n1308:         return delegate.position(partition, timeout);\\n1309:     }\\n1310: \\n1311:     /**\\n1312:      * Get the last committed offsets for the given partitions (whether the commit happened by this process or\\n1313:      * another). The returned offsets will be used as the position for the consumer in the event of a failure.\\n1314:      * <p>\\n1315:      * If any of the partitions requested do not exist, the result map will contain null as the value for that partition.\\n1316:      * <p>\\n1317:      * This call will do a remote call to get the latest committed offsets from the server, and will block until the\\n1318:      * committed offsets are gotten successfully, an unrecoverable error is encountered (in which case it is thrown to\\n1319:      * the caller), or the timeout specified by {@code default.api.timeout.ms} expires (in which case a\\n1320:      * {@link org.apache.kafka.common.errors.TimeoutException} is thrown to the caller).\\n1321:      *\\n1322:      * @param partitions The partitions to check\\n1323:      * @return The latest committed offsets for the given partitions; {@code null} will be returned for the\\n1324:      *         partition if there is no such message.\\n1325:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.errors.WakeupException if {@link #wakeup()} is called before or while this\\n1326:      *             function is called\\n1327:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.errors.InterruptException if the calling thread is interrupted before or while\\n1328:      *             this function is called\\n1329:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.errors.AuthenticationException if authentication fails. See the exception for more details\\n1330:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.errors.AuthorizationException if not authorized to the topic or to the\\n1331:      *             configured groupId. See the exception for more details\\n1332:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.errors.UnsupportedVersionException if the consumer attempts to fetch stable offsets\\n1333:      *             when the broker doesn't support this feature\\n1334:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.KafkaException for any other unrecoverable errors\\n1335:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.errors.TimeoutException if the committed offset cannot be found before\\n1336:      *             the timeout specified by {@code default.api.timeout.ms} expires.\\n1337:      */\\n1338:     @Override\\n1339:     public Map<TopicPartition, OffsetAndMetadata> committed(final Set<TopicPartition> partitions) {\\n1340:         return delegate.committed(partitions);\\n1341:     }\\n1342: \\n1343:     /**\\n1344:      * Get the last committed offsets for the given partitions (whether the commit happened by this process or\\n1345:      * another). The returned offsets will be used as the position for the consumer in the event of a failure.\\n1346:      * <p>\\n1347:      * If any of the partitions requested do not exist, the result map will contain null as the value for that partition.\\n1348:      * <p>\\n1349:      * This call will block to do a remote call to get the latest committed offsets from the server.\\n1350:      *\\n1351:      * @param partitions The partitions to check\\n1352:      * @param timeout  The maximum amount of time to await the latest committed offsets\\n1353:      * @return The latest committed offsets for the given partitions; {@code null} will be returned for the\\n1354:      *         partition if there is no such message.\\n1355:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.errors.WakeupException if {@link #wakeup()} is called before or while this\\n1356:      *             function is called\\n1357:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.errors.InterruptException if the calling thread is interrupted before or while\\n1358:      *             this function is called\\n1359:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.errors.AuthenticationException if authentication fails. See the exception for more details\\n1360:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.errors.AuthorizationException if not authorized to the topic or to the\\n1361:      *             configured groupId. See the exception for more details\\n1362:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.KafkaException for any other unrecoverable errors\\n1363:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.errors.TimeoutException if the committed offset cannot be found before\\n1364:      *             expiration of the timeout\\n1365:      */\\n1366:     @Override\\n1367:     public Map<TopicPartition, OffsetAndMetadata> committed(final Set<TopicPartition> partitions, final Duration timeout) {\\n1368:         return delegate.committed(partitions, timeout);\\n1369:     }\\n1370: \\n1371:     /**\\n1372:      * Determines the client's unique client instance ID used for telemetry. This ID is unique to\\n1373:      * this specific client instance and will not change after it is initially generated.\\n1374:      * The ID is useful for correlating client operations with telemetry sent to the broker and\\n1375:      * to its eventual monitoring destinations.\\n1376:      * <p>\\n1377:      * If telemetry is enabled, this will first require a connection to the cluster to generate\\n1378:      * the unique client instance ID. This method waits up to {@code timeout} for the consumer\\n1379:      * client to complete the request.\\n1380:      * <p>\\n1381:      * Client telemetry is controlled by the {@link ConsumerConfig#ENABLE_METRICS_PUSH_CONFIG}\\n1382:      * configuration option.\\n1383:      *\\n1384:      * @param timeout The maximum time to wait for consumer client to determine its client instance ID.\\n1385:      *                The value must be non-negative. Specifying a timeout of zero means do not\\n1386:      *                wait for the initial request to complete if it hasn't already.\\n1387:      * @throws InterruptException If the thread is interrupted while blocked.\\n1388:      * @throws KafkaException If an unexpected error occurs while trying to determine the client\\n1389:      *                        instance ID, though this error does not necessarily imply the\\n1390:      *                        consumer client is otherwise unusable.\\n1391:      * @throws IllegalArgumentException If the {@code timeout} is negative.\\n1392:      * @throws IllegalStateException If telemetry is not enabled ie, config `{@code enable.metrics.push}`\\n1393:      *                               is set to `{@code false}`.\\n1394:      * @return The client's assigned instance id used for metrics collection.\\n1395:      */\\n1396:     @Override\\n1397:     public Uuid clientInstanceId(Duration timeout) {\\n1398:         return delegate.clientInstanceId(timeout);\\n1399:     }\\n1400: \\n1401: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-benchmarks/kafka--e678b4b@47ac12bd6042edf23f1dbca3d789db945d62802d/-/blob/clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/clients/consumer/KafkaConsumer.java%3FL1200-1400\"}"

"{\"content\":\"1400: \\n1401:   /**\\n1402:      * Get the metrics kept by the consumer\\n1403:      */\\n1404:     @Override\\n1405:     public Map<MetricName, ? extends Metric> metrics() {\\n1406:         return delegate.metrics();\\n1407:     }\\n1408: \\n1409:     /**\\n1410:      * Get metadata about the partitions for a given topic. This method will issue a remote call to the server if it\\n1411:      * does not already have any metadata about the given topic.\\n1412:      *\\n1413:      * @param topic The topic to get partition metadata for\\n1414:      *\\n1415:      * @return The list of partitions, which will be empty when the given topic is not found\\n1416:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.errors.WakeupException if {@link #wakeup()} is called before or while this\\n1417:      *             function is called\\n1418:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.errors.InterruptException if the calling thread is interrupted before or while\\n1419:      *             this function is called\\n1420:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.errors.AuthenticationException if authentication fails. See the exception for more details\\n1421:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.errors.AuthorizationException if not authorized to the specified topic. See the exception for more details\\n1422:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.KafkaException for any other unrecoverable errors\\n1423:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.errors.TimeoutException if the offset metadata could not be fetched before\\n1424:      *         the amount of time allocated by {@code default.api.timeout.ms} expires.\\n1425:      */\\n1426:     @Override\\n1427:     public List<PartitionInfo> partitionsFor(String topic) {\\n1428:         return delegate.partitionsFor(topic);\\n1429:     }\\n1430: \\n1431:     /**\\n1432:      * Get metadata about the partitions for a given topic. This method will issue a remote call to the server if it\\n1433:      * does not already have any metadata about the given topic.\\n1434:      *\\n1435:      * @param topic The topic to get partition metadata for\\n1436:      * @param timeout The maximum of time to await topic metadata\\n1437:      *\\n1438:      * @return The list of partitions, which will be empty when the given topic is not found\\n1439:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.errors.WakeupException if {@link #wakeup()} is called before or while this\\n1440:      *             function is called\\n1441:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.errors.InterruptException if the calling thread is interrupted before or while\\n1442:      *             this function is called\\n1443:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.errors.AuthenticationException if authentication fails. See the exception for more details\\n1444:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.errors.AuthorizationException if not authorized to the specified topic. See\\n1445:      *             the exception for more details\\n1446:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.errors.TimeoutException if topic metadata cannot be fetched before expiration\\n1447:      *             of the passed timeout\\n1448:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.KafkaException for any other unrecoverable errors\\n1449:      */\\n1450:     @Override\\n1451:     public List<PartitionInfo> partitionsFor(String topic, Duration timeout) {\\n1452:         return delegate.partitionsFor(topic, timeout);\\n1453:     }\\n1454: \\n1455:     /**\\n1456:      * Get metadata about partitions for all topics that the user is authorized to view. This method will issue a\\n1457:      * remote call to the server.\\n1458: \\n1459:      * @return The map of topics and its partitions\\n1460:      *\\n1461:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.errors.WakeupException if {@link #wakeup()} is called before or while this\\n1462:      *             function is called\\n1463:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.errors.InterruptException if the calling thread is interrupted before or while\\n1464:      *             this function is called\\n1465:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.KafkaException for any other unrecoverable errors\\n1466:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.errors.TimeoutException if the offset metadata could not be fetched before\\n1467:      *         the amount of time allocated by {@code default.api.timeout.ms} expires.\\n1468:      */\\n1469:     @Override\\n1470:     public Map<String, List<PartitionInfo>> listTopics() {\\n1471:         return delegate.listTopics();\\n1472:     }\\n1473: \\n1474:     /**\\n1475:      * Get metadata about partitions for all topics that the user is authorized to view. This method will issue a\\n1476:      * remote call to the server.\\n1477:      *\\n1478:      * @param timeout The maximum time this operation will block to fetch topic metadata\\n1479:      *\\n1480:      * @return The map of topics and its partitions\\n1481:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.errors.WakeupException if {@link #wakeup()} is called before or while this\\n1482:      *             function is called\\n1483:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.errors.InterruptException if the calling thread is interrupted before or while\\n1484:      *             this function is called\\n1485:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.errors.TimeoutException if the topic metadata could not be fetched before\\n1486:      *             expiration of the passed timeout\\n1487:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.KafkaException for any other unrecoverable errors\\n1488:      */\\n1489:     @Override\\n1490:     public Map<String, List<PartitionInfo>> listTopics(Duration timeout) {\\n1491:         return delegate.listTopics(timeout);\\n1492:     }\\n1493: \\n1494:     /**\\n1495:      * Suspend fetching from the requested partitions. Future calls to {@link #poll(Duration)} will not return\\n1496:      * any records from these partitions until they have been resumed using {@link #resume(Collection)}.\\n1497:      * Note that this method does not affect partition subscription. In particular, it does not cause a group\\n1498:      * rebalance when automatic assignment is used.\\n1499:      *\\n1500:      * Note: Rebalance will not preserve the pause/resume state.\\n1501:      * @param partitions The partitions which should be paused\\n1502:      * @throws IllegalStateException if any of the provided partitions are not currently assigned to this consumer\\n1503:      */\\n1504:     @Override\\n1505:     public void pause(Collection<TopicPartition> partitions) {\\n1506:         delegate.pause(partitions);\\n1507:     }\\n1508: \\n1509:     /**\\n1510:      * Resume specified partitions which have been paused with {@link #pause(Collection)}. New calls to\\n1511:      * {@link #poll(Duration)} will return records from these partitions if there are any to be fetched.\\n1512:      * If the partitions were not previously paused, this method is a no-op.\\n1513:      * @param partitions The partitions which should be resumed\\n1514:      * @throws IllegalStateException if any of the provided partitions are not currently assigned to this consumer\\n1515:      */\\n1516:     @Override\\n1517:     public void resume(Collection<TopicPartition> partitions) {\\n1518:         delegate.resume(partitions);\\n1519:     }\\n1520: \\n1521:     /**\\n1522:      * Add the provided application metric for subscription.\\n1523:      * This metric will be added to this client's metrics\\n1524:      * that are available for subscription and sent as\\n1525:      * telemetry data to the broker.\\n1526:      * The provided metric must map to an OTLP metric data point\\n1527:      * type in the OpenTelemetry v1 metrics protobuf message types.\\n1528:      * Specifically, the metric should be one of the following:\\n1529:      * <ul>\\n1530:      *  <li>\\n1531:      *     `Sum`: Monotonic total count meter (Counter). Suitable for metrics like total number of X, e.g., total bytes sent.\\n1532:      *  </li>\\n1533:      *  <li>\\n1534:      *     `Gauge`: Non-monotonic current value meter (UpDownCounter). Suitable for metrics like current value of Y, e.g., current queue count.\\n1535:      *  </li>\\n1536:      * </ul>\\n1537:      * Metrics not matching these types are silently ignored.\\n1538:      * Executing this method for a previously registered metric is a benign operation and results in updating that metrics entry.\\n1539:      *\\n1540:      * @param metric The application metric to register\\n1541:      */\\n1542:     @Override\\n1543:     public void registerMetricForSubscription(KafkaMetric metric) {\\n1544:         delegate.registerMetricForSubscription(metric);\\n1545:     }\\n1546: \\n1547:     /**\\n1548:      * Remove the provided application metric for subscription.\\n1549:      * This metric is removed from this client's metrics\\n1550:      * and will not be available for subscription any longer.\\n1551:      * Executing this method with a metric that has not been registered is a\\n1552:      * benign operation and does not result in any action taken (no-op).\\n1553:      *\\n1554:      * @param metric The application metric to remove\\n1555:      */\\n1556:     @Override\\n1557:     public void unregisterMetricFromSubscription(KafkaMetric metric) {\\n1558:         delegate.unregisterMetricFromSubscription(metric);\\n1559:     }\\n1560: \\n1561:     /**\\n1562:      * Get the set of partitions that were previously paused by a call to {@link #pause(Collection)}.\\n1563:      *\\n1564:      * @return The set of paused partitions\\n1565:      */\\n1566:     @Override\\n1567:     public Set<TopicPartition> paused() {\\n1568:         return delegate.paused();\\n1569:     }\\n1570: \\n1571:     /**\\n1572:      * Look up the offsets for the given partitions by timestamp. The returned offset for each partition is the\\n1573:      * earliest offset whose timestamp is greater than or equal to the given timestamp in the corresponding partition.\\n1574:      *\\n1575:      * This is a blocking call. The consumer does not have to be assigned the partitions.\\n1576:      * If the message format version in a partition is before 0.10.0, i.e. the messages do not have timestamps, null\\n1577:      * will be returned for that partition.\\n1578:      *\\n1579:      * @param timestampsToSearch the mapping from partition to the timestamp to look up.\\n1580:      *\\n1581:      * @return a mapping from partition to the timestamp and offset of the first message with timestamp greater\\n1582:      *         than or equal to the target timestamp. If the timestamp and offset for a specific partition cannot be found within\\n1583:      *         the default timeout, and no corresponding message exists, the entry in the returned map will be {@code null}\\n1584:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.errors.AuthenticationException if authentication fails. See the exception for more details\\n1585:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.errors.AuthorizationException if not authorized to the topic(s). See the exception for more details\\n1586:      * @throws IllegalArgumentException if the target timestamp is negative\\n1587:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.errors.TimeoutException if the offset metadata could not be fetched before\\n1588:      *         the amount of time allocated by {@code default.api.timeout.ms} expires.\\n1589:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.errors.UnsupportedVersionException if the broker does not support looking up\\n1590:      *         the offsets by timestamp\\n1591:      */\\n1592:     @Override\\n1593:     public Map<TopicPartition, OffsetAndTimestamp> offsetsForTimes(Map<TopicPartition, Long> timestampsToSearch) {\\n1594:         return delegate.offsetsForTimes(timestampsToSearch);\\n1595:     }\\n1596: \\n1597:     /**\\n1598:      * Look up the offsets for the given partitions by timestamp. The returned offset for each partition is the\\n1599:      * earliest offset whose timestamp is greater than or equal to the given timestamp in the corresponding partition.\\n1600:      *\\n1601: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-benchmarks/kafka--e678b4b@47ac12bd6042edf23f1dbca3d789db945d62802d/-/blob/clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/clients/consumer/KafkaConsumer.java%3FL1400-1600\"}"

"{\"content\":\"1600:      *\\n1601:      * This is a blocking call. The consumer does not have to be assigned the partitions.\\n1602:      * If the message format version in a partition is before 0.10.0, i.e. the messages do not have timestamps, null\\n1603:      * will be returned for that partition.\\n1604:      *\\n1605:      * @param timestampsToSearch the mapping from partition to the timestamp to look up.\\n1606:      * @param timeout The maximum amount of time to await retrieval of the offsets\\n1607:      *\\n1608:      * @return a mapping from partition to the timestamp and offset of the first message with timestamp greater\\n1609:      *         than or equal to the target timestamp. If the timestamp and offset for a specific partition cannot be found within\\n1610:      *         timeout, and no corresponding message exists, the entry in the returned map will be {@code null}\\n1611:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.errors.AuthenticationException if authentication fails. See the exception for more details\\n1612:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.errors.AuthorizationException if not authorized to the topic(s). See the exception for more details\\n1613:      * @throws IllegalArgumentException if the target timestamp is negative\\n1614:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.errors.TimeoutException if the offset metadata could not be fetched before\\n1615:      *         expiration of the passed timeout\\n1616:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.errors.UnsupportedVersionException if the broker does not support looking up\\n1617:      *         the offsets by timestamp\\n1618:      */\\n1619:     @Override\\n1620:     public Map<TopicPartition, OffsetAndTimestamp> offsetsForTimes(Map<TopicPartition, Long> timestampsToSearch, Duration timeout) {\\n1621:         return delegate.offsetsForTimes(timestampsToSearch, timeout);\\n1622:     }\\n1623: \\n1624:     /**\\n1625:      * Get the first offset for the given partitions.\\n1626:      * <p>\\n1627:      * This method does not change the current consumer position of the partitions.\\n1628:      *\\n1629:      * @see #seekToBeginning(Collection)\\n1630:      *\\n1631:      * @param partitions the partitions to get the earliest offsets.\\n1632:      * @return The earliest available offsets for the given partitions\\n1633:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.errors.AuthenticationException if authentication fails. See the exception for more details\\n1634:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.errors.AuthorizationException if not authorized to the topic(s). See the exception for more details\\n1635:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.errors.TimeoutException if the offset metadata could not be fetched before\\n1636:      *         expiration of the configured {@code default.api.timeout.ms}\\n1637:      */\\n1638:     @Override\\n1639:     public Map<TopicPartition, Long> beginningOffsets(Collection<TopicPartition> partitions) {\\n1640:         return delegate.beginningOffsets(partitions);\\n1641:     }\\n1642: \\n1643:     /**\\n1644:      * Get the first offset for the given partitions.\\n1645:      * <p>\\n1646:      * This method does not change the current consumer position of the partitions.\\n1647:      *\\n1648:      * @see #seekToBeginning(Collection)\\n1649:      *\\n1650:      * @param partitions the partitions to get the earliest offsets\\n1651:      * @param timeout The maximum amount of time to await retrieval of the beginning offsets\\n1652:      *\\n1653:      * @return The earliest available offsets for the given partitions, and it will return empty map if zero timeout is provided\\n1654:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.errors.AuthenticationException if authentication fails. See the exception for more details\\n1655:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.errors.AuthorizationException if not authorized to the topic(s). See the exception for more details\\n1656:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.errors.TimeoutException if the offset metadata could not be fetched before\\n1657:      *         expiration of the passed timeout\\n1658:      */\\n1659:     @Override\\n1660:     public Map<TopicPartition, Long> beginningOffsets(Collection<TopicPartition> partitions, Duration timeout) {\\n1661:         return delegate.beginningOffsets(partitions, timeout);\\n1662:     }\\n1663: \\n1664:     /**\\n1665:      * Get the end offsets for the given partitions. In the default {@code read_uncommitted} isolation level, the end\\n1666:      * offset is the high watermark (that is, the offset of the last successfully replicated message plus one). For\\n1667:      * {@code read_committed} consumers, the end offset is the last stable offset (LSO), which is the minimum of\\n1668:      * the high watermark and the smallest offset of any open transaction. Finally, if the partition has never been\\n1669:      * written to, the end offset is 0.\\n1670:      *\\n1671:      * <p>\\n1672:      * This method does not change the current consumer position of the partitions.\\n1673:      *\\n1674:      * @see #seekToEnd(Collection)\\n1675:      *\\n1676:      * @param partitions the partitions to get the end offsets.\\n1677:      * @return The end offsets for the given partitions.\\n1678:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.errors.AuthenticationException if authentication fails. See the exception for more details\\n1679:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.errors.AuthorizationException if not authorized to the topic(s). See the exception for more details\\n1680:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.errors.TimeoutException if the offset metadata could not be fetched before\\n1681:      *         the amount of time allocated by {@code default.api.timeout.ms} expires\\n1682:      */\\n1683:     @Override\\n1684:     public Map<TopicPartition, Long> endOffsets(Collection<TopicPartition> partitions) {\\n1685:         return delegate.endOffsets(partitions);\\n1686:     }\\n1687: \\n1688:     /**\\n1689:      * Get the end offsets for the given partitions. In the default {@code read_uncommitted} isolation level, the end\\n1690:      * offset is the high watermark (that is, the offset of the last successfully replicated message plus one). For\\n1691:      * {@code read_committed} consumers, the end offset is the last stable offset (LSO), which is the minimum of\\n1692:      * the high watermark and the smallest offset of any open transaction. Finally, if the partition has never been\\n1693:      * written to, the end offset is 0.\\n1694:      *\\n1695:      * <p>\\n1696:      * This method does not change the current consumer position of the partitions.\\n1697:      *\\n1698:      * @see #seekToEnd(Collection)\\n1699:      *\\n1700:      * @param partitions the partitions to get the end offsets.\\n1701:      * @param timeout The maximum amount of time to await retrieval of the end offsets\\n1702:      *\\n1703:      * @return The end offsets for the given partitions, and it will return empty map if zero timeout is provided\\n1704:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.errors.AuthenticationException if authentication fails. See the exception for more details\\n1705:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.errors.AuthorizationException if not authorized to the topic(s). See the exception for more details\\n1706:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.errors.TimeoutException if the offsets could not be fetched before\\n1707:      *         expiration of the passed timeout\\n1708:      */\\n1709:     @Override\\n1710:     public Map<TopicPartition, Long> endOffsets(Collection<TopicPartition> partitions, Duration timeout) {\\n1711:         return delegate.endOffsets(partitions, timeout);\\n1712:     }\\n1713: \\n1714:     /**\\n1715:      * Get the consumer's current lag on the partition. Returns an \\\"empty\\\" {@link OptionalLong} if the lag is not known,\\n1716:      * for example if there is no position yet, or if the end offset is not known yet.\\n1717:      *\\n1718:      * <p>\\n1719:      * This method uses locally cached metadata. If the log end offset is not known yet, it triggers a request to fetch\\n1720:      * the log end offset, but returns immediately.\\n1721:      *\\n1722:      * @param topicPartition The partition to get the lag for.\\n1723:      *\\n1724:      * @return This {@code Consumer} instance's current lag for the given partition.\\n1725:      *\\n1726:      * @throws IllegalStateException if the {@code topicPartition} is not assigned\\n1727:      */\\n1728:     @Override\\n1729:     public OptionalLong currentLag(TopicPartition topicPartition) {\\n1730:         return delegate.currentLag(topicPartition);\\n1731:     }\\n1732: \\n1733:     /**\\n1734:      * Return the current group metadata associated with this consumer.\\n1735:      *\\n1736:      * @return consumer group metadata\\n1737:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.errors.InvalidGroupIdException if consumer does not have a group\\n1738:      */\\n1739:     @Override\\n1740:     public ConsumerGroupMetadata groupMetadata() {\\n1741:         return delegate.groupMetadata();\\n1742:     }\\n1743: \\n1744:     /**\\n1745:      * Alert the consumer to trigger a new rebalance by rejoining the group. This is a nonblocking call that forces\\n1746:      * the consumer to trigger a new rebalance on the next {@link #poll(Duration)} call. Note that this API does not\\n1747:      * itself initiate the rebalance, so you must still call {@link #poll(Duration)}. If a rebalance is already in\\n1748:      * progress this call will be a no-op. If you wish to force an additional rebalance you must complete the current\\n1749:      * one by calling poll before retrying this API.\\n1750:      * <p>\\n1751:      * You do not need to call this during normal processing, as the consumer group will manage itself\\n1752:      * automatically and rebalance when necessary. However there may be situations where the application wishes to\\n1753:      * trigger a rebalance that would otherwise not occur. For example, if some condition external and invisible to\\n1754:      * the Consumer and its group changes in a way that would affect the userdata encoded in the\\n1755:      * {@link org.apache.kafka.clients.consumer.ConsumerPartitionAssignor.Subscription Subscription}, the Consumer\\n1756:      * will not be notified and no rebalance will occur. This API can be used to force the group to rebalance so that\\n1757:      * the assignor can perform a partition reassignment based on the latest userdata. If your assignor does not use\\n1758:      * this userdata, or you do not use a custom\\n1759:      * {@link org.apache.kafka.clients.consumer.ConsumerPartitionAssignor ConsumerPartitionAssignor}, you should not\\n1760:      * use this API.\\n1761:      *\\n1762:      * @param reason The reason why the new rebalance is needed.\\n1763:      *\\n1764:      * @throws java.lang.IllegalStateException if the consumer does not use group subscription\\n1765:      */\\n1766:     @Override\\n1767:     public void enforceRebalance(final String reason) {\\n1768:         delegate.enforceRebalance(reason);\\n1769:     }\\n1770: \\n1771:     /**\\n1772:      * @see #enforceRebalance(String)\\n1773:      */\\n1774:     @Override\\n1775:     public void enforceRebalance() {\\n1776:         delegate.enforceRebalance();\\n1777:     }\\n1778: \\n1779:     /**\\n1780:      * Close the consumer with {@link CloseOptions.GroupMembershipOperation#DEFAULT default leave group behavior},\\n1781:      * waiting for up to the default timeout of 30 seconds for any needed cleanup.\\n1782:      * If auto-commit is enabled, this will commit the current offsets if possible within the default\\n1783:      * timeout. See {@link #close(CloseOptions)} for details. Note that {@link #wakeup()}\\n1784:      * cannot be used to interrupt close.\\n1785:      * <p>\\n1786:      * This close operation will attempt all shutdown steps even if one of them fails.\\n1787:      * It logs all encountered errors, continues to execute the next steps, and finally throws the first error found.\\n1788:      *\\n1789:      * @throws WakeupException    if {@link #wakeup()} is called before or while this function is called\\n1790:      * @throws InterruptException if the calling thread is interrupted before or while this function is called\\n1791:      * @throws KafkaException     for any other error during close\\n1792:      *                            (e.g., errors thrown from rebalance callbacks or commit callbacks from previous asynchronous commits)\\n1793:      */\\n1794:     @Override\\n1795:     public void close() {\\n1796:         delegate.close();\\n1797:     }\\n1798: \\n1799:     /**\\n1800:      * This method has been deprecated since Kafka 4.1 and should use {@link KafkaConsumer#close(CloseOptions)} instead.\\n1801: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-benchmarks/kafka--e678b4b@47ac12bd6042edf23f1dbca3d789db945d62802d/-/blob/clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/clients/consumer/KafkaConsumer.java%3FL1600-1800\"}"

{
  "endLine": 1900,
  "path": "clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/clients/consumer/KafkaConsumer.java",
  "repo": "github.com/sg-benchmarks/kafka--e678b4b",

}
Output
"{\"content\":\"1800:      * This method has been deprecated since Kafka 4.1 and should use {@link KafkaConsumer#close(CloseOptions)} instead.\\n1801:      * <p>\\n1802:      * Close the consumer with {@link CloseOptions.GroupMembershipOperation#DEFAULT default leave group behavior}\\n1803:      * cleanly within the specified timeout. This method waits up to\\n1804:      * {@code timeout} for the consumer to complete pending commits and maybe leave the group (if the member is dynamic).\\n1805:      * If auto-commit is enabled, this will commit the current offsets if possible within the\\n1806:      * timeout. If the consumer is unable to complete offset commits and to gracefully leave the group (if applicable)\\n1807:      * before the timeout expires, the consumer is force closed. Note that {@link #wakeup()} cannot be\\n1808:      * used to interrupt close.\\n1809:      * <p>\\n1810:      * The actual maximum wait time is bounded by the {@link ConsumerConfig#REQUEST_TIMEOUT_MS_CONFIG} setting, which\\n1811:      * only applies to operations performed with the broker (coordinator-related requests and\\n1812:      * fetch sessions). Even if a larger timeout is specified, the consumer will not wait longer than\\n1813:      * {@link ConsumerConfig#REQUEST_TIMEOUT_MS_CONFIG} for these requests to complete during the close operation.\\n1814:      * Note that the execution time of callbacks (such as {@link OffsetCommitCallback} and\\n1815:      * {@link ConsumerRebalanceListener}) does not consume time from the close timeout.\\n1816:      * <p>\\n1817:      * This close operation will attempt all shutdown steps even if one of them fails.\\n1818:      * It logs all encountered errors, continues to execute the next steps, and finally throws the first error found.\\n1819:      *\\n1820:      * @param timeout The maximum time to wait for consumer to close gracefully. The value must be\\n1821:      *                non-negative. Specifying a timeout of zero means do not wait for pending requests to complete.\\n1822:      * @throws IllegalArgumentException If the {@code timeout} is negative.\\n1823:      * @throws WakeupException          if {@link #wakeup()} is called before or while this function is called\\n1824:      * @throws InterruptException       if the calling thread is interrupted before or while this function is called\\n1825:      * @throws KafkaException           for any other error during close\\n1826:      *                                  (e.g., errors thrown from rebalance callbacks or commit callbacks from previous asynchronous commits)\\n1827:      */\\n1828:     @Deprecated(since = \\\"4.1\\\")\\n1829:     @Override\\n1830:     public void close(Duration timeout) {\\n1831:         delegate.close(timeout);\\n1832:     }\\n1833: \\n1834:     /**\\n1835:      * Close the consumer cleanly. {@link CloseOptions} allows to specify a timeout and a\\n1836:      * {@link CloseOptions.GroupMembershipOperation leave group behavior}.\\n1837:      * If no timeout is specified, the default timeout of 30 seconds is used.\\n1838:      * If no leave group behavior is specified, the {@link CloseOptions.GroupMembershipOperation#DEFAULT default\\n1839:      * leave group behavior} is used.\\n1840:      * <p>\\n1841:      * This method waits up to the timeout for the consumer to complete pending commits and maybe leave the group,\\n1842:      * depending on the specified leave group behavior.\\n1843:      * If auto-commit is enabled, this will commit the current offsets if possible within the\\n1844:      * timeout. If the consumer is unable to complete offset commits and to gracefully leave the group (if applicable)\\n1845:      * before the timeout expires, the consumer is force closed. Note that {@link #wakeup()} cannot be\\n1846:      * used to interrupt close.\\n1847:      * <p>\\n1848:      * The actual maximum wait time is bounded by the {@link ConsumerConfig#REQUEST_TIMEOUT_MS_CONFIG} setting, which\\n1849:      * only applies to operations performed with the broker (coordinator-related requests and\\n1850:      * fetch sessions). Even if a larger timeout is specified, the consumer will not wait longer than\\n1851:      * {@link ConsumerConfig#REQUEST_TIMEOUT_MS_CONFIG} for these requests to complete during the close operation.\\n1852:      * Note that the execution time of callbacks (such as {@link OffsetCommitCallback} and\\n1853:      * {@link ConsumerRebalanceListener}) does not consume time from the close timeout.\\n1854:      * <p>\\n1855:      * This close operation will attempt all shutdown steps even if one of them fails.\\n1856:      * It logs all encountered errors, continues to execute the next steps, and finally throws the first error found.\\n1857:      *\\n1858:      * @param option see {@link CloseOptions}; cannot be {@code null}\\n1859:      * @throws IllegalArgumentException If the {@code option} timeout is negative\\n1860:      * @throws WakeupException          if {@link #wakeup()} is called before or while this function is called\\n1861:      * @throws InterruptException       if the calling thread is interrupted before or while this function is called\\n1862:      * @throws KafkaException           for any other error during close\\n1863:      *                                  (e.g., errors thrown from rebalance callbacks or commit callbacks from previous asynchronous commits)\\n1864:      */\\n1865:     @Override\\n1866:     public void close(CloseOptions option) {\\n1867:         delegate.close(option);\\n1868:     }\\n1869: \\n1870:     /**\\n1871:      * Wakeup the consumer. This method is thread-safe and is useful in particular to abort a long poll.\\n1872:      * The thread which is blocking in an operation will throw {@link org.apache.kafka.common.errors.WakeupException}.\\n1873:      * If no thread is blocking in a method which can throw {@link org.apache.kafka.common.errors.WakeupException}, the next call to such a method will raise it instead.\\n1874:      */\\n1875:     @Override\\n1876:     public void wakeup() {\\n1877:         delegate.wakeup();\\n1878:     }\\n1879: \\n1880:     // Functions below are for testing only\\n1881:     String clientId() {\\n1882:         return delegate.clientId();\\n1883:     }\\n1884: \\n1885:     Metrics metricsRegistry() {\\n1886:         return delegate.metricsRegistry();\\n1887:     }\\n1888: \\n1889:     KafkaConsumerMetrics kafkaConsumerMetrics() {\\n1890:         return delegate.kafkaConsumerMetrics();\\n1891:     }\\n1892: \\n1893:     boolean updateAssignmentMetadataIfNeeded(final Timer timer) {\\n1894:         return delegate.updateAssignmentMetadataIfNeeded(timer);\\n1895:     }\\n1896: }\\n1897: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-benchmarks/kafka--e678b4b@47ac12bd6042edf23f1dbca3d789db945d62802d/-/blob/clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/clients/consumer/KafkaConsumer.java%3FL1800-1900\"}"

Long> endOffsets(Collection<TopicPartition> partitions);\\n248: \\n249:     /**\\n250:      * @see KafkaConsumer#endOffsets(Collection, Duration)\\n251:      */\\n252:     Map<TopicPartition, Long> endOffsets(Collection<TopicPartition> partitions, Duration timeout);\\n253: \\n254:     /**\\n255:      * @see KafkaConsumer#currentLag(TopicPartition)\\n256:      */\\n257:     OptionalLong currentLag(TopicPartition topicPartition);\\n258: \\n259:     /**\\n260:      * @see KafkaConsumer#groupMetadata()\\n261:      */\\n262:     ConsumerGroupMetadata groupMetadata();\\n263: \\n264:     /**\\n265:      * @see KafkaConsumer#enforceRebalance()\\n266:      */\\n267:     void enforceRebalance();\\n268: \\n269:     /**\\n270:      * @see KafkaConsumer#enforceRebalance(String)\\n271:      */\\n272:     void enforceRebalance(final String reason);\\n273: \\n274:     /**\\n275:      * @see KafkaConsumer#close()\\n276:      */\\n277:     void close();\\n278: \\n279:     /**\\n280:      * @see KafkaConsumer#close(Duration)\\n281:      */\\n282:     @Deprecated\\n283:     void close(Duration timeout);\\n284: \\n285:     /**\\n286:      * @see KafkaConsumer#close(CloseOptions)\\n287:      */\\n288:     void close(final CloseOptions option);\\n289: \\n290:     /**\\n291:      * @see KafkaConsumer#wakeup()\\n292:      */\\n293:     void wakeup();\\n294: }\\n295: org.apache.kafka.common.MetricName;\n21: import org.apache.kafka.common.PartitionInfo;\n22: import org.apache.kafka.common.TopicPartition;\n23: import org.apache.kafka.common.Uuid;\n24: import java.util.Map;\n31: import java.util.OptionalLong;\n32: import java.util.Set;\n33: import java.util.regex.Pattern;\n34: \n35: /**\n36:  * @see KafkaConsumer\n37:  * @see MockConsumer\n38:  */\n39: public interface Consumer<K, V> extends Closeable {\n40: \n41:     /**\n42:      * @see KafkaConsumer#assignment()\n43:      */\n44:     Set<TopicPartition> assignment();\n45: \n46:     /**\n47: subscribe(Collection<String> topics);\n55: \n56:     /**\n57:      * @see KafkaConsumer#subscribe(Collection, ConsumerRebalanceListener)\n58:      */\n59:     void subscribe(Collection<String> topics, ConsumerRebalanceListener callback);\n60: \n61:     /**\n62:      * @see KafkaConsumer#assign(Collection)\n63:      */\n64:     void assign(Collection<TopicPartition> partitions);\n65: \n66:     /**\n67:     * @see KafkaConsumer#subscribe(Pattern, ConsumerRebalanceListener)\n68:     */\n69:     void subscribe(Pattern pattern, ConsumerRebalanceListener callback);\n70: \n71:     /**\n72:     * @see KafkaConsumer#subscribe(Pattern)\n73:     */\n74:     void subscribe(Pattern pattern);\n75: \n76:     /**\n77:      * @see KafkaConsumer#subscribe(SubscriptionPattern, ConsumerRebalanceListener)\n78:      */\n79:     void subscribe(SubscriptionPattern pattern, ConsumerRebalanceListener callback);\n80: \n81:     /**\n82:      * @see KafkaConsumer#subscribe(SubscriptionPattern)\n83:      */\n84:     void subscribe(SubscriptionPattern pattern);\n85: \n86:     /**\n87:      * @see KafkaConsumer#unsubscribe()\n88:      */\n89:     void unsubscribe();\n90: \n91:     /**\n92:      * @see KafkaConsumer#poll(Duration)\n93:      */\n94:     ConsumerRecords<K, V> poll(Duration timeout);\n95: \n96:     /**\n97:      * @see KafkaConsumer#commitSync()\n98:      */\n99:     void commitSync();\n100: \n101:     /**\n102:      * @see KafkaConsumer#commitSync(Duration)\n103:      */\n104:     void commitSync(Duration timeout);\n105: \n106:     /**\n107:      * @see KafkaConsumer#commitSync(Map)\n108:      */\n109:     void commitSync(Map<TopicPartition, OffsetAndMetadata> offsets);\n110: \n111:     /**\n112:      * @see KafkaConsumer#commitSync(Map, Duration)\n113:      */\n114:     void commitSync(final Map<TopicPartition, OffsetAndMetadata> offsets, final Duration timeout);\n115:     /**\n116:      * @see KafkaConsumer#commitAsync()\n117:      */\n118:     void commitAsync();\n119: \n120:     /**\n121:      * @see KafkaConsumer#commitAsync(OffsetCommitCallback)\n122:      */\n123:     void commitAsync(OffsetCommitCallback callback);\n124: \n125:     /**\n126:      * @see KafkaConsumer#commitAsync(Map, OffsetCommitCallback)\n127:      */\n128:     void commitAsync(Map<TopicPartition, OffsetAndMetadata> offsets, OffsetCommitCallback callback);\n129: \n130:     /**\n131:      * @see KafkaConsumer#registerMetricForSubscription(KafkaMetric)\n132:      */\n133:     void registerMetricForSubscription(KafkaMetric metric);\n134: \n135:     /**\n136:      * @see KafkaConsumer#unregisterMetricFromSubscription(KafkaMetric)\n137:      */\n138:     void unregisterMetricFromSubscription(KafkaMetric metric);\n139:     /**\n140:      * @see KafkaConsumer#seek(TopicPartition, long)\n141:      */\n142:     void seek(TopicPartition partition, long offset);\n143: \n144:     /**\n145:      * @see KafkaConsumer#seek(TopicPartition, OffsetAndMetadata)\n146:      */\n147:     void seek(TopicPartition partition, OffsetAndMetadata offsetAndMetadata);\n148: \n149:     /**\n150:      * @see KafkaConsumer#seekToBeginning(Collection)\n151:      */\n152:     void seekToBeginning(Collection<TopicPartition> partitions);\n153: \n154: KafkaConsumer#position(TopicPartition)\n161:      */\n162:     long position(TopicPartition partition);\n163:     \n164:     /**\n165:      * @see KafkaConsumer#position(TopicPartition, Duration)\n166:      */\n167:     long position(TopicPartition partition, final Duration timeout);\n168: \n169:     /**\n170:      * @see KafkaConsumer#committed(Set)\n171:      */\n172:     Map<TopicPartition, OffsetAndMetadata> committed(Set<TopicPartition> partitions);\n173: \n174:     /**\n175:      * @see KafkaConsumer#committed(Set, Duration)\n176:      */\n177:     Map<TopicPartition, OffsetAndMetadata> committed(Set<TopicPartition> partitions, final Duration timeout);\n178: \n179:     /**\n180:      * See {@link KafkaConsumer#clientInstanceId(Duration)}}\n181: \n189:     /**\n190:      * @see KafkaConsumer#partitionsFor(String)\n191:      */\n192:     List<PartitionInfo> partitionsFor(String topic);\n193: \n194:     /**\n195:      * @see KafkaConsumer#partitionsFor(String, Duration)\n196:      */\n197:     List<PartitionInfo> partitionsFor(String topic, Duration timeout);\n198: \n199:     /**\n200:      * @see
