Output
{
  "content": "{\"content\":\"1: /*\\n2:  * Licensed to the Apache Software Foundation (ASF) under one or more\\n3:  * contributor license agreements. See the NOTICE file distributed with\\n4:  * this work for additional information regarding copyright ownership.\\n5:  * The ASF licenses this file to You under the Apache License, Version 2.0\\n6:  * (the \\\"License\\\"); you may not use this file except in compliance with\\n7:  * the License. You may obtain a copy of the License at\\n8:  *\\n9:  *    http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\\n10:  *\\n11:  * Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\\n12:  * distributed under the License is distributed on an \\\"AS IS\\\" BASIS,\\n13:  * WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\\n14:  * See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\\n15:  * limitations under the License.\\n16:  */\\n17: package org.apache.kafka.clients.consumer;\\n18: \\n19: import org.apache.kafka.common.TopicPartition;\\n20: \\n21: import java.util.Collection;\\n22: \\n23: /**\\n24:  * A callback interface that the user can implement to trigger custom actions when the set of partitions assigned to the\\n25:  * consumer changes.\\n26:  * <p>\\n27:  * This is applicable when the consumer is having Kafka auto-manage group membership. If the consumer directly assigns partitions,\\n28:  * those partitions will never be reassigned and this callback is not applicable.\\n29:  * <p>\\n30:  * When Kafka is managing the group membership, a partition re-assignment will be triggered any time the members of the group change or the subscription\\n31:  * of the members changes. This can occur when processes die, new process instances are added or old instances come back to life after failure.\\n32:  * Partition re-assignments can also be triggered by changes affecting the subscribed topics (e.g. when the number of partitions is\\n33:  * administratively adjusted).\\n34:  * <p>\\n35:  * There are many uses for this functionality. One common use is saving offsets in a custom store. By saving offsets in\\n36:  * the {@link #onPartitionsRevoked(Collection)} call we can ensure that any time partition assignment changes\\n37:  * the offset gets saved.\\n38:  * <p>\\n39:  * Another use is flushing out any kind of cache of intermediate results the consumer may be keeping. For example,\\n40:  * consider a case where the consumer is subscribed to a topic containing user page views, and the goal is to count the\\n41:  * number of page views per user for each five minute window. Let's say the topic is partitioned by the user id so that\\n42:  * all events for a particular user go to a single consumer instance. The consumer can keep in memory a running\\n43:  * tally of actions per user and only flush these out to a remote data store when its cache gets too big. However if a\\n44:  * partition is reassigned it may want to automatically trigger a flush of this cache, before the new owner takes over\\n45:  * consumption.\\n46:  * <p>\\n47:  * This callback will only execute in the user thread as part of the {@link Consumer#poll(java.time.Duration) poll(long)} call\\n48:  * whenever partition assignment changes.\\n49:  * <p>\\n50:  * Under normal conditions, if a partition is reassigned from one consumer to another, then the old consumer will\\n51:  * always invoke {@link #onPartitionsRevoked(Collection) onPartitionsRevoked} for that partition prior to the new consumer\\n52:  * invoking {@link #onPartitionsAssigned(Collection) onPartitionsAssigned} for the same partition. So if offsets or other state is saved in the\\n53:  * {@link #onPartitionsRevoked(Collection) onPartitionsRevoked} call by one consumer member, it will always be accessible by the time the\\n54:  * other consumer member taking over that partition and triggering its {@link #onPartitionsAssigned(Collection) onPartitionsAssigned} callback to load the state.\\n55:  * <p>\\n56:  * You can think of revocation as a graceful way to give up ownership of a partition. In some cases, the consumer may not have an opportunity to do so.\\n57:  * For example, if the session times out, then the partitions may be reassigned before we have a chance to revoke them gracefully.\\n58:  * For this case, we have a third callback {@link #onPartitionsLost(Collection)}. The difference between this function and\\n59:  * {@link #onPartitionsRevoked(Collection)} is that upon invocation of {@link #onPartitionsLost(Collection)}, the partitions\\n60:  * may already be owned by some other members in the group and therefore users would not be able to commit its consumed offsets for example.\\n61:  * Users could implement these two functions differently (by default,\\n62:  * {@link #onPartitionsLost(Collection)} will be calling {@link #onPartitionsRevoked(Collection)} directly); for example, in the\\n63:  * {@link #onPartitionsLost(Collection)} we should not need to store the offsets since we know these partitions are no longer owned by the consumer\\n64:  * at that time.\\n65:  * <p>\\n66:  * During a rebalance event, the {@link #onPartitionsAssigned(Collection) onPartitionsAssigned} function will always be triggered exactly once when\\n67:  * the rebalance completes. That is, even if there is no newly assigned partitions for a consumer member, its {@link #onPartitionsAssigned(Collection) onPartitionsAssigned}\\n68:  * will still be triggered with an empty collection of partitions. As a result this function can be used also to notify when a rebalance event has happened.\\n69:  * With eager rebalancing, {@link #onPartitionsRevoked(Collection)} will always be called at the start of a rebalance. On the other hand, {@link #onPartitionsLost(Collection)}\\n70:  * will only be called when there were non-empty partitions that were lost.\\n71:  * With cooperative rebalancing, {@link #onPartitionsRevoked(Collection)} and {@link #onPartitionsLost(Collection)}\\n72:  * will only be triggered when there are non-empty partitions revoked or lost from this consumer member during a rebalance event.\\n73:  * <p>\\n74:  * It is possible\\n75:  * for a {@link org.apache.kafka.common.errors.WakeupException} or {@link org.apache.kafka.common.errors.InterruptException}\\n76:  * to be raised from one of these nested invocations. In this case, the exception will be propagated to the current\\n77:  * invocation of {@link KafkaConsumer#poll(java.time.Duration)} in which this callback is being executed. This means it is not\\n78:  * necessary to catch these exceptions and re-attempt to wakeup or interrupt the consumer thread.\\n79:  * Also if the callback function implementation itself throws an exception, this exception will be propagated to the current\\n80:  * invocation of {@link KafkaConsumer#poll(java.time.Duration)} as well.\\n81:  * <p>\\n82:  * Note that callbacks only serve as notification of an assignment change.\\n83:  * They cannot be used to express acceptance of the change.\\n84:  * Hence throwing an exception from a callback does not affect the assignment in any way,\\n85:  * as it will be propagated all the way up to the {@link KafkaConsumer#poll(java.time.Duration)} call.\\n86:  * If user captures the exception in the caller, the callback is still assumed successful and no further retries will be attempted.\\n87:  * <p>\\n88:  *\\n89:  * Here is pseudo-code for a callback implementation for saving offsets:\\n90:  * <pre>\\n91:  * {@code\\n92:  *   public class SaveOffsetsOnRebalance implements ConsumerRebalanceListener {\\n93:  *       private Consumer<?,?> consumer;\\n94:  *\\n95:  *       public SaveOffsetsOnRebalance(Consumer<?,?> consumer) {\\n96:  *           this.consumer = consumer;\\n97:  *       }\\n98:  *\\n99:  *       public void onPartitionsRevoked(Collection<TopicPartition> partitions) {\\n100:  *           // save the offsets in an external store using some custom code not described here\\n101:  *           for(TopicPartition partition: partitions)\\n102:  *              saveOffsetInExternalStore(consumer.position(partition));\\n103:  *       }\\n104:  *\\n105:  *       public void onPartitionsLost(Collection<TopicPartition> partitions) {\\n106:  *           // do not need to save the offsets since these partitions are probably owned by other consumers already\\n107:  *       }\\n108:  *\\n109:  *       public void onPartitionsAssigned(Collection<TopicPartition> partitions) {\\n110:  *           // read the offsets from an external store using some custom code not described here\\n111:  *           for(TopicPartition partition: partitions)\\n112:  *              consumer.seek(partition, readOffsetFromExternalStore(partition));\\n113:  *       }\\n114:  *   }\\n115:  * }\\n116:  * </pre>\\n117:  */\\n118: public interface ConsumerRebalanceListener {\\n119: \\n120:     /**\\n121:      * A callback method the user can implement to provide handling of offset commits to a customized store.\\n122:      * This method will be called during a rebalance operation when the consumer has to give up some partitions.\\n123:      * The consumer may need to give up some partitions (thus this callback executed) under the following scenarios:\\n124:      * <ul>\\n125:      *     <li>If the consumer assignment changes</li>\\n126:      *     <li>If the consumer is being closed ({@link KafkaConsumer#close(CloseOptions option)})</li>\\n127:      *     <li>If the consumer is unsubscribing ({@link KafkaConsumer#unsubscribe()})</li>\\n128:      * </ul>\\n129:      * It is recommended that offsets should be committed in this callback to either Kafka or a\\n130:      * custom offset store to prevent duplicate data.\\n131:      * <p>\\n132:      * This callback is always called before re-assigning the partitions.\\n133:      * If the consumer is using the {@link GroupProtocol#CLASSIC} rebalance protocol:\\n134:      * <ul>\\n135:      *     <li>\\n136:      *         In eager rebalancing, onPartitionsRevoked will be called with the full set of assigned partitions as a parameter (all partitions are revoked).\\n137:      *         It will be called even if there are no partitions to revoke.\\n138:      *     </li>\\n139:      *     <li>\\n140:      *         In cooperative rebalancing, onPartitionsRevoked will be called with the set of partitions to revoke,\\n141:      *         iff the set is non-empty.\\n142:      *     </li>\\n143:      * </ul>\\n144:      * If the consumer is using the {@link GroupProtocol#CONSUMER} rebalance protocol, this callback will be called\\n145:      * with the set of partitions to revoke iff the set is non-empty\\n146:      * (same behavior as the {@link GroupProtocol#CLASSIC} rebalance protocol with Cooperative mode).\\n147:      * <p>\\n148:      * For examples on usage of this API, see Usage Examples section of {@link KafkaConsumer KafkaConsumer}.\\n149:      * <p>\\n150:      * It is common for the revocation callback to use the consumer instance in order to commit offsets. It is possible\\n151:      * for a {@link org.apache.kafka.common.errors.WakeupException} or {@link org.apache.kafka.common.errors.InterruptException}\\n152:      * to be raised from one of these nested invocations. In this case, the exception will be propagated to the current\\n153:      * invocation of {@link KafkaConsumer#poll(java.time.Duration)} in which this callback is being executed. This means it is not\\n154:      * necessary to catch these exceptions and re-attempt to wakeup or interrupt the consumer thread.\\n155:      *\\n156:      * @param partitions The list of partitions that were assigned to the consumer and now need to be revoked. This will\\n157:      *                  include the full assignment under the Classic/Eager protocol, given that it revokes all partitions.\\n158:      *                   It will only include the subset to revoke under the Classic/Cooperative and Consumer protocols.\\n159:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.errors.WakeupException If raised from a nested call to {@link KafkaConsumer}\\n160:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.errors.InterruptException If raised from a nested call to {@link KafkaConsumer}\\n161:      */\\n162:     void onPartitionsRevoked(Collection<TopicPartition> partitions);\\n163: \\n164:     /**\\n165:      * A callback method the user can implement to provide handling of customized offsets on completion of a successful\\n166:      * partition re-assignment. This method will be called after the partition re-assignment completes (even if no new\\n167:      * partitions were assigned to the consumer), and before the consumer starts fetching data,\\n168:      * and only as the result of a {@link Consumer#poll(java.time.Duration) poll(long)} call.\\n169:      * <p>\\n170:      * It is guaranteed that under normal conditions all the processes in a consumer group will execute their\\n171:      * {@link #onPartitionsRevoked(Collection)} callback before any instance executes this onPartitionsAssigned callback.\\n172:      * During exceptional scenarios, partitions may be migrated\\n173:      * without the old owner being notified (i.e. their {@link #onPartitionsRevoked(Collection)} callback not triggered),\\n174:      * and later when the old owner consumer realized this event, the {@link #onPartitionsLost(Collection)} callback\\n175:      * will be triggered by the consumer then.\\n176:      * <p>\\n177:      * It is common for the assignment callback to use the consumer instance in order to query offsets. It is possible\\n178:      * for a {@link org.apache.kafka.common.errors.WakeupException} or {@link org.apache.kafka.common.errors.InterruptException}\\n179:      * to be raised from one of these nested invocations. In this case, the exception will be propagated to the current\\n180:      * invocation of {@link KafkaConsumer#poll(java.time.Duration)} in which this callback is being executed. This means it is not\\n181:      * necessary to catch these exceptions and re-attempt to wakeup or interrupt the consumer thread.\\n182:      *\\n183:      * @param partitions Partitions that have been added to the assignment as a result of the rebalance.\\n184:      *                   Note that partitions that were already owned by this consumer and remain assigned are not\\n185:      *                   included in this list under the Classic/Cooperative or Consumer protocols. THe full assignment\\n186:      *                   will be received under the Classic/Eager protocol.\\n187:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.errors.WakeupException    If raised from a nested call to {@link KafkaConsumer}\\n188:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.errors.InterruptException If raised from a nested call to {@link KafkaConsumer}\\n189:      */\\n190:     void onPartitionsAssigned(Collection<TopicPartition> partitions);\\n191: \\n192:     /**\\n193:      * A callback method you can implement to provide handling of cleaning up resources for partitions that have already\\n194:      * been reassigned to other consumers. This method will not be called during normal execution as the owned partitions would\\n195:      * first be revoked by calling the {@link ConsumerRebalanceListener#onPartitionsRevoked}, before being reassigned\\n196:      * to other consumers during a rebalance event. However, during exceptional scenarios when the consumer realized that it\\n197:      * does not own this partition any longer, i.e. not revoked via a normal rebalance event, then this method would be invoked.\\n198:      * <p>\\n199:      * For example, this function is called if a consumer's session timeout has expired, or if a fatal error has been\\n200:      * received indicating the consumer is no longer part of the group.\\n201:      * <p>\\n202:      * By default it will just trigger {@link ConsumerRebalanceListener#onPartitionsRevoked}; for users who want to distinguish\\n203:      * the handling logic of revoked partitions v.s. lost partitions, they can override the default implementation.\\n204:      * <p>\\n205:      * It is possible\\n206:      * for a {@link org.apache.kafka.common.errors.WakeupException} or {@link org.apache.kafka.common.errors.InterruptException}\\n207:      * to be raised from one of these nested invocations. In this case, the exception will be propagated to the current\\n208:      * invocation of {@link KafkaConsumer#poll(java.time.Duration)} in which this callback is being executed. This means it is not\\n209:      * necessary to catch these exceptions and re-attempt to wakeup or interrupt the consumer thread.\\n210:      *\\n211:      * @param partitions The list of partitions that were assigned to the consumer and now have been reassigned\\n212:      *                   to other consumers. With both, the Classic and Consumer protocols, this will always include\\n213:      *                   all partitions that were previously assigned to the consumer.\\n214:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.errors.WakeupException    If raised from a nested call to {@link KafkaConsumer}\\n215:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.errors.InterruptException If raised from a nested call to {@link KafkaConsumer}\\n216:      */\\n217:     default void onPartitionsLost(Collection<TopicPartition> partitions) {\\n218:         onPartitionsRevoked(partitions);\\n219:     }\\n220: }\\n221: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-benchmarks/kafka--e678b4b@47ac12bd6042edf23f1dbca3d789db945d62802d/-/blob/clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/clients/consumer/ConsumerRebalanceListener.java\"}",
  "structuredContent": {
    "content": "1: /*\n2:  * Licensed to the Apache Software Foundation (ASF) under one or more\n3:  * contributor license agreements. See the NOTICE file distributed with\n4:  * this work for additional information regarding copyright ownership.\n5:  * The ASF licenses this file to You under the Apache License, Version 2.0\n6:  * (the \"License\"); you may not use this file except in compliance with\n7:  * the License. You may obtain a copy of the License at\n8:  *\n9:  *    http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\n10:  *\n11:  * Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\n12:  * distributed under the License is distributed on an \"AS IS\" BASIS,\n13:  * WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\n14:  * See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\n15:  * limitations under the License.\n16:  */\n17: package org.apache.kafka.clients.consumer;\n18: \n19: import org.apache.kafka.common.TopicPartition;\n20: \n21: import java.util.Collection;\n22: \n23: /**\n24:  * A callback interface that the user can implement to trigger custom actions when the set of partitions assigned to the\n25:  * consumer changes.\n26:  * <p>\n27:  * This is applicable when the consumer is having Kafka auto-manage group membership. If the consumer directly assigns partitions,\n28:  * those partitions will never be reassigned and this callback is not applicable.\n29:  * <p>\n30:  * When Kafka is managing the group membership, a partition re-assignment will be triggered any time the members of the group change or the subscription\n31:  * of the members changes. This can occur when processes die, new process instances are added or old instances come back to life after failure.\n32:  * Partition re-assignments can also be triggered by changes affecting the subscribed topics (e.g. when the number of partitions is\n33:  * administratively adjusted).\n34:  * <p>\n35:  * There are many uses for this functionality. One common use is saving offsets in a custom store. By saving offsets in\n36:  * the {@link #onPartitionsRevoked(Collection)} call we can ensure that any time partition assignment changes\n37:  * the offset gets saved.\n38:  * <p>\n39:  * Another use is flushing out any kind of cache of intermediate results the consumer may be keeping. For example,\n40:  * consider a case where the consumer is subscribed to a topic containing user page views, and the goal is to count the\n41:  * number of page views per user for each five minute window. Let's say the topic is partitioned by the user id so that\n42:  * all events for a particular user go to a single consumer instance. The consumer can keep in memory a running\n43:  * tally of actions per user and only flush these out to a remote data store when its cache gets too big. However if a\n44:  * partition is reassigned it may want to automatically trigger a flush of this cache, before the new owner takes over\n45:  * consumption.\n46:  * <p>\n47:  * This callback will only execute in the user thread as part of the {@link Consumer#poll(java.time.Duration) poll(long)} call\n48:  * whenever partition assignment changes.\n49:  * <p>\n50:  * Under normal conditions, if a partition is reassigned from one consumer to another, then the old consumer will\n51:  * always invoke {@link #onPartitionsRevoked(Collection) onPartitionsRevoked} for that partition prior to the new consumer\n52:  * invoking {@link #onPartitionsAssigned(Collection) onPartitionsAssigned} for the same partition. So if offsets or other state is saved in the\n53:  * {@link #onPartitionsRevoked(Collection) onPartitionsRevoked} call by one consumer member, it will always be accessible by the time the\n54:  * other consumer member taking over that partition and triggering its {@link #onPartitionsAssigned(Collection) onPartitionsAssigned} callback to load the state.\n55:  * <p>\n56:  * You can think of revocation as a graceful way to give up ownership of a partition. In some cases, the consumer may not have an opportunity to do so.\n57:  * For example, if the session times out, then the partitions may be reassigned before we have a chance to revoke them gracefully.\n58:  * For this case, we have a third callback {@link #onPartitionsLost(Collection)}. The difference between this function and\n59:  * {@link #onPartitionsRevoked(Collection)} is that upon invocation of {@link #onPartitionsLost(Collection)}, the partitions\n60:  * may already be owned by some other members in the group and therefore users would not be able to commit its consumed offsets for example.\n61:  * Users could implement these two functions differently (by default,\n62:  * {@link #onPartitionsLost(Collection)} will be calling {@link #onPartitionsRevoked(Collection)} directly); for example, in the\n63:  * {@link #onPartitionsLost(Collection)} we should not need to store the offsets since we know these partitions are no longer owned by the consumer\n64:  * at that time.\n65:  * <p>\n66:  * During a rebalance event, the {@link #onPartitionsAssigned(Collection) onPartitionsAssigned} function will always be triggered exactly once when\n67:  * the rebalance completes. That is, even if there is no newly assigned partitions for a consumer member, its {@link #onPartitionsAssigned(Collection) onPartitionsAssigned}\n68:  * will still be triggered with an empty collection of partitions. As a result this function can be used also to notify when a rebalance event has happened.\n69:  * With eager rebalancing, {@link #onPartitionsRevoked(Collection)} will always be called at the start of a rebalance. On the other hand, {@link #onPartitionsLost(Collection)}\n70:  * will only be called when there were non-empty partitions that were lost.\n71:  * With cooperative rebalancing, {@link #onPartitionsRevoked(Collection)} and {@link #onPartitionsLost(Collection)}\n72:  * will only be triggered when there are non-empty partitions revoked or lost from this consumer member during a rebalance event.\n73:  * <p>\n74:  * It is possible\n75:  * for a {@link org.apache.kafka.common.errors.WakeupException} or {@link org.apache.kafka.common.errors.InterruptException}\n76:  * to be raised from one of these nested invocations. In this case, the exception will be propagated to the current\n77:  * invocation of {@link KafkaConsumer#poll(java.time.Duration)} in which this callback is being executed. This means it is not\n78:  * necessary to catch these exceptions and re-attempt to wakeup or interrupt the consumer thread.\n79:  * Also if the callback function implementation itself throws an exception, this exception will be propagated to the current\n80:  * invocation of {@link KafkaConsumer#poll(java.time.Duration)} as well.\n81:  * <p>\n82:  * Note that callbacks only serve as notification of an assignment change.\n83:  * They cannot be used to express acceptance of the change.\n84:  * Hence throwing an exception from a callback does not affect the assignment in any way,\n85:  * as it will be propagated all the way up to the {@link KafkaConsumer#poll(java.time.Duration)} call.\n86:  * If user captures the exception in the caller, the callback is still assumed successful and no further retries will be attempted.\n87:  * <p>\n88:  *\n89:  * Here is pseudo-code for a callback implementation for saving offsets:\n90:  * <pre>\n91:  * {@code\n92:  *   public class SaveOffsetsOnRebalance implements ConsumerRebalanceListener {\n93:  *       private Consumer<?,?> consumer;\n94:  *\n95:  *       public SaveOffsetsOnRebalance(Consumer<?,?> consumer) {\n96:  *           this.consumer = consumer;\n97:  *       }\n98:  *\n99:  *       public void onPartitionsRevoked(Collection<TopicPartition> partitions) {\n100:  *           // save the offsets in an external store using some custom code not described here\n101:  *           for(TopicPartition partition: partitions)\n102:  *              saveOffsetInExternalStore(consumer.position(partition));\n103:  *       }\n104:  *\n105:  *       public void onPartitionsLost(Collection<TopicPartition> partitions) {\n106:  *           // do not need to save the offsets since these partitions are probably owned by other consumers already\n107:  *       }\n108:  *\n109:  *       public void onPartitionsAssigned(Collection<TopicPartition> partitions) {\n110:  *           // read the offsets from an external store using some custom code not described here\n111:  *           for(TopicPartition partition: partitions)\n112:  *              consumer.seek(partition, readOffsetFromExternalStore(partition));\n113:  *       }\n114:  *   }\n115:  * }\n116:  * </pre>\n117:  */\n118: public interface ConsumerRebalanceListener {\n119: \n120:     /**\n121:      * A callback method the user can implement to provide handling of offset commits to a customized store.\n122:      * This method will be called during a rebalance operation when the consumer has to give up some partitions.\n123:      * The consumer may need to give up some partitions (thus this callback executed) under the following scenarios:\n124:      * <ul>\n125:      *     <li>If the consumer assignment changes</li>\n126:      *     <li>If the consumer is being closed ({@link KafkaConsumer#close(CloseOptions option)})</li>\n127:      *     <li>If the consumer is unsubscribing ({@link KafkaConsumer#unsubscribe()})</li>\n128:      * </ul>\n129:      * It is recommended that offsets should be committed in this callback to either Kafka or a\n130:      * custom offset store to prevent duplicate data.\n131:      * <p>\n132:      * This callback is always called before re-assigning the partitions.\n133:      * If the consumer is using the {@link GroupProtocol#CLASSIC} rebalance protocol:\n134:      * <ul>\n135:      *     <li>\n136:      *         In eager rebalancing, onPartitionsRevoked will be called with the full set of assigned partitions as a parameter (all partitions are revoked).\n137:      *         It will be called even if there are no partitions to revoke.\n138:      *     </li>\n139:      *     <li>\n140:      *         In cooperative rebalancing, onPartitionsRevoked will be called with the set of partitions to revoke,\n141:      *         iff the set is non-empty.\n142:      *     </li>\n143:      * </ul>\n144:      * If the consumer is using the {@link GroupProtocol#CONSUMER} rebalance protocol, this callback will be called\n145:      * with the set of partitions to revoke iff the set is non-empty\n146:      * (same behavior as the {@link GroupProtocol#CLASSIC} rebalance protocol with Cooperative mode).\n147:      * <p>\n148:      * For examples on usage of this API, see Usage Examples section of {@link KafkaConsumer KafkaConsumer}.\n149:      * <p>\n150:      * It is common for the revocation callback to use the consumer instance in order to commit offsets. It is possible\n151:      * for a {@link org.apache.kafka.common.errors.WakeupException} or {@link org.apache.kafka.common.errors.InterruptException}\n152:      * to be raised from one of these nested invocations. In this case, the exception will be propagated to the current\n153:      * invocation of {@link KafkaConsumer#poll(java.time.Duration)} in which this callback is being executed. This means it is not\n154:      * necessary to catch these exceptions and re-attempt to wakeup or interrupt the consumer thread.\n155:      *\n156:      * @param partitions The list of partitions that were assigned to the consumer and now need to be revoked. This will\n157:      *                  include the full assignment under the Classic/Eager protocol, given that it revokes all partitions.\n158:      *                   It will only include the subset to revoke under the Classic/Cooperative and Consumer protocols.\n159:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.errors.WakeupException If raised from a nested call to {@link KafkaConsumer}\n160:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.errors.InterruptException If raised from a nested call to {@link KafkaConsumer}\n161:      */\n162:     void onPartitionsRevoked(Collection<TopicPartition> partitions);\n163: \n164:     /**\n165:      * A callback method the user can implement to provide handling of customized offsets on completion of a successful\n166:      * partition re-assignment. This method will be called after the partition re-assignment completes (even if no new\n167:      * partitions were assigned to the consumer), and before the consumer starts fetching data,\n168:      * and only as the result of a {@link Consumer#poll(java.time.Duration) poll(long)} call.\n169:      * <p>\n170:      * It is guaranteed that under normal conditions all the processes in a consumer group will execute their\n171:      * {@link #onPartitionsRevoked(Collection)} callback before any instance executes this onPartitionsAssigned callback.\n172:      * During exceptional scenarios, partitions may be migrated\n173:      * without the old owner being notified (i.e. their {@link #onPartitionsRevoked(Collection)} callback not triggered),\n174:      * and later when the old owner consumer realized this event, the {@link #onPartitionsLost(Collection)} callback\n175:      * will be triggered by the consumer then.\n176:      * <p>\n177:      * It is common for the assignment callback to use the consumer instance in order to query offsets. It is possible\n178:      * for a {@link org.apache.kafka.common.errors.WakeupException} or {@link org.apache.kafka.common.errors.InterruptException}\n179:      * to be raised from one of these nested invocations. In this case, the exception will be propagated to the current\n180:      * invocation of {@link KafkaConsumer#poll(java.time.Duration)} in which this callback is being executed. This means it is not\n181:      * necessary to catch these exceptions and re-attempt to wakeup or interrupt the consumer thread.\n182:      *\n183:      * @param partitions Partitions that have been added to the assignment as a result of the rebalance.\n184:      *                   Note that partitions that were already owned by this consumer and remain assigned are not\n185:      *                   included in this list under the Classic/Cooperative or Consumer protocols. THe full assignment\n186:      *                   will be received under the Classic/Eager protocol.\n187:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.errors.WakeupException    If raised from a nested call to {@link KafkaConsumer}\n188:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.errors.InterruptException If raised from a nested call to {@link KafkaConsumer}\n189:      */\n190:     void onPartitionsAssigned(Collection<TopicPartition> partitions);\n191: \n192:     /**\n193:      * A callback method you can implement to provide handling of cleaning up resources for partitions that have already\n194:      * been reassigned to other consumers. This method will not be called during normal execution as the owned partitions would\n195:      * first be revoked by calling the {@link ConsumerRebalanceListener#onPartitionsRevoked}, before being reassigned\n196:      * to other consumers during a rebalance event. However, during exceptional scenarios when the consumer realized that it\n197:      * does not own this partition any longer, i.e. not revoked via a normal rebalance event, then this method would be invoked.\n198:      * <p>\n199:      * For example, this function is called if a consumer's session timeout has expired, or if a fatal error has been\n200:      * received indicating the consumer is no longer part of the group.\n201:      * <p>\n202:      * By default it will just trigger {@link ConsumerRebalanceListener#onPartitionsRevoked}; for users who want to distinguish\n203:      * the handling logic of revoked partitions v.s. lost partitions, they can override the default implementation.\n204:      * <p>\n205:      * It is possible\n206:      * for a {@link org.apache.kafka.common.errors.WakeupException} or {@link org.apache.kafka.common.errors.InterruptException}\n207:      * to be raised from one of these nested invocations. In this case, the exception will be propagated to the current\n208:      * invocation of {@link KafkaConsumer#poll(java.time.Duration)} in which this callback is being executed. This means it is not\n209:      * necessary to catch these exceptions and re-attempt to wakeup or interrupt the consumer thread.\n210:      *\n211:      * @param partitions The list of partitions that were assigned to the consumer and now have been reassigned\n212:      *                   to other consumers. With both, the Classic and Consumer protocols, this will always include\n213:      *                   all partitions that were previously assigned to the consumer.\n214:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.errors.WakeupException    If raised from a nested call to {@link KafkaConsumer}\n215:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.errors.InterruptException If raised from a nested call to {@link KafkaConsumer}\n216:      */\n217:     default void onPartitionsLost(Collection<TopicPartition> partitions) {\n218:         onPartitionsRevoked(partitions);\n219:     }\n220: }\n221: ",
    "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-benchmarks/kafka--e678b4b@47ac12bd6042edf23f1dbca3d789db945d62802d/-/blob/clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/clients/consumer/ConsumerRebalanceListener.java"
  }
}

Input
{
  "description": "Find and analyze consumer test cases",
  "prompt": "Find and read test files related to KafkaConsumer in the github.com/sg-benchmarks/kafka--e678b4b repository. Look for:\n\n1. Test file names that contain \"Consumer\" and \"Test\" (e.g., *ConsumerTest.java, *ConsumerIntegrationTest.java)\n2. For the most relevant test files, extract:\n   - Test methods that demonstrate poll() behavior and blocking\n   - Test methods for offset commit strategies (sync vs async)\n   - Test methods for rebalancing and ConsumerRebalanceListener callbacks\n   - Test methods for exception handling and error scenarios\n   - Test methods for seek operations and position management\n\nReturn key insights about behavioral semantics that tests demonstrate, with specific test names and what they verify. Focus on edge cases and non-obvious behaviors.",
  "subagent_type": "Explore"
}
Output
{
  "agentId": "a1249697592c68f3b",
  "content": [
    {
      "text": "Perfect! Now let me compile the comprehensive summary based on all the test files I've found and read. Let me create a final summary of my findings:\n\nBased on my thorough exploration of the KafkaConsumer test files in the `github.com/sg-benchmarks/kafka--e678b4b` repository, here is a comprehensive analysis of test files and the behavioral semantics they demonstrate:\n\n## Test Files Found\n\nThe main test files related to KafkaConsumer are located in:\n- `/workspace/clients/src/test/java/org/apache/kafka/clients/consumer/KafkaConsumerTest.java` (3919 lines)\n- `/workspace/clients/src/test/java/org/apache/kafka/clients/consumer/internals/AsyncKafkaConsumerTest.java`\n- `/workspace/clients/src/test/java/org/apache/kafka/clients/consumer/internals/CommitRequestManagerTest.java`\n- `/workspace/clients/src/test/java/org/apache/kafka/clients/consumer/internals/ConsumerCoordinatorTest.java`\n- `/workspace/clients/src/test/java/org/apache/kafka/clients/consumer/internals/OffsetFetcherTest.java`\n- `/workspace/clients/src/test/java/org/apache/kafka/clients/consumer/internals/EagerConsumerCoordinatorTest.java`\n- `/workspace/clients/src/test/java/org/apache/kafka/clients/consumer/internals/CooperativeConsumerCoordinatorTest.java`\n\n## Key Test Methods and Behavioral Insights\n\n### 1. Poll() Behavior and Blocking\n\n**Test: `testPollReturnsRecords(GroupProtocol groupProtocol)` - Line 438**\n- Verifies that `poll(Duration.ZERO)` with available records returns all records and updates next offsets\n- Key behavior: Returns 5 records with correct partition set and nextOffsets metadata\n\n**Test: `testPollThrowsInterruptExceptionIfInterrupted(GroupProtocol groupProtocol)` - Line 1437**\n- Demonstrates that `Thread.interrupt()` before poll() throws `InterruptException`\n- Important edge case: Thread interrupt state is preserved and can affect subsequent operations\n- Test ensures proper cleanup with `Thread.interrupted()` to avoid polluting subsequent tests\n\n**Test: `testPollWithNoSubscription(GroupProtocol groupProtocol)` - Line 1905**\n- Calling poll() without subscription or assignment throws `IllegalStateException`\n- Also applies to empty subscriptions and empty user assignments (lines 1912-1924)\n\n**Test: `testWakeupWithFetchDataAvailable(GroupProtocol groupProtocol)` - Line 1396**\n- Non-obvious behavior: After `wakeup()` throws `WakeupException`, position is NOT advanced\n- Next poll() after wakeup() returns the buffered data correctly\n- Critical semantic: Wakeup interrupts the current poll without advancing offset state\n\n### 2. Offset Commit Strategies (Sync vs Async)\n\n**Test: `testCommitSync()` - CommitRequestManagerTest.java:296**\n- Synchronous commit sends request and waits for coordinator response\n- Verifies `unsentOffsetCommitRequests()` has exactly 1 request\n- Metadata is updated with epoch information via `updateLastSeenEpochIfNewer()`\n- Future completes with exact offsets that were committed\n\n**Test: `testCommitSyncWithEmptyOffsets()` - CommitRequestManagerTest.java:322**\n- Empty offset maps complete immediately without sending requests\n- No coordinator interaction needed\n\n**Test: `testCommitAsync()` - CommitRequestManagerTest.java:338**\n- Async commits also send request immediately (non-obvious: not truly async in sending)\n- Future completes when response received\n- Semantic difference from sync: Returns immediately after queueing, not after response\n\n**Test: `testOffsetCommitAsyncFailedWithRetriableThrowsRetriableCommitException()` - CommitRequestManagerTest.java:1072**\n- Critical behavior: Async commits with retriable errors (e.g., `COORDINATOR_NOT_AVAILABLE`) are NOT retried\n- Returns `RetriableCommitFailedException` to caller\n- Edge case: Differs from sync behavior which may retry\n\n**Test: `testOffsetCommitSingleFailedAttemptPerRequestWhenPartitionErrors()` - CommitRequestManagerTest.java:1097**\n- Subtle behavior: Multiple partition-level errors in one response count as single attempt\n- Retriable exceptions get retried with backoff (2+ attempts observed)\n- Non-retriable errors are not retried\n\n**Test: `testAutoCommitSentBeforePositionUpdate(GroupProtocol groupProtocol)` - Line 1296**\n- Critical timing semantic: Auto-commits happen BEFORE position is updated to user\n- User hasn't consumed records yet when auto-commit fires at interval boundary\n- Ensures committed offset reflects actual consumed data, not lookahead\n\n**Test: `testCommitSyncAllConsumed()` - AsyncKafkaConsumerTest.java:757**\n- `commitSyncAllConsumed()` commits without explicit offset map\n- Uses tracked offsets from actual consumption\n\n**Test: `testMeasureCommitSyncDuration()` - Line 2344**\n- Validates timing metrics are recorded even on failure\n- Min duration is >= 999ms (in test with 1s mock time)\n\n### 3. Rebalancing and ConsumerRebalanceListener Callbacks\n\n**Test: `testRebalanceException(GroupProtocol groupProtocol)` - Line 2424**\n- Non-obvious behavior: Assignment is updated EVEN when listener callback throws exception\n- Close's revoke also throws, but assignment still clears\n- Semantic: Internal state always updates despite user callback exceptions\n\n**Test: `testOnPartitionsAssignExceptionThrownWhenNoPreviousThrownCallbacks()` - ConsumerCoordinatorTest.java:802**\n- `onPartitionsAssigned()` exception becomes `KafkaException` with original message preserved\n- Edge case: Exception message explicitly includes assignor name context\n\n**Test: `testOnPartitionsAssignExceptionShouldBeRenderedIfNotKafkaException()` - Line 851**\n- Non-KafkaException thrown by callback gets wrapped: message becomes \"User rebalance callback throws an error\"\n- Original exception appears as cause\n- Semantic: Framework normalizes all callback exceptions\n\n**Test: `testOnRevokeExceptionShouldBeRenderedIfNotKafkaException()` - Line 816**\n- Cooperative protocol DOES call `onPartitionsRevoked()`, eager protocol does NOT\n- Protocol-dependent callback behavior is critical semantic\n\n**Test: `testReturnRecordsDuringRebalance(GroupProtocol groupProtocol)` - Line 2461**\n- Records are returned to user even while rebalance is in progress\n- Position tracking works correctly across rebalance boundaries\n\n### 4. Exception Handling and Error Scenarios\n\n**Test: `testSecondPollWithDeserializationErrorThrowsRecordDeserializationException()` - Line 455**\n- First poll returns records up to (not including) bad record\n- Next poll throws `RecordDeserializationException` with:\n  - `offset()` = offset of bad record\n  - `topicPartition()` = partition\n  - Position == offset (important for retry logic)\n- Semantic: Position stops at bad record for potential recovery\n\n**Test: `testCommitSyncAuthenticationFailure(GroupProtocol groupProtocol)` - Line 2307**\n- `AuthenticationException` from commitSync propagates unchanged\n- Also applies to `committed()` call\n\n**Test: `testMeasureCommitSyncDurationOnFailure()` - Line 2326**\n- Metrics recorded even on failure (metric value >= 999ms)\n\n**Test: `testWakeupCommitted()` - AsyncKafkaConsumerTest.java:1020**\n- `committed()` operations also respect wakeup (throws `WakeupException`)\n\n**Test: `testCommittedExceptionThrown()` - AsyncKafkaConsumerTest.java:399**\n- Internal exceptions during committed lookup propagate as `KafkaException`\n\n### 5. Seek Operations and Position Management\n\n**Test: `testSeekNegative(GroupProtocol groupProtocol)` - Line 687**\n- Negative offsets throw `IllegalArgumentException`\n- Validation happens at seek time, not at poll\n\n**Test: `testSeekWithInFlightReset()` - OffsetFetcherTest.java:462**\n- Non-obvious behavior: If seek arrives while ListOffsets request in-flight, seek overrides pending reset\n- Response to ListOffsets is discarded\n- Final position is seek target (237), not ListOffsets response (5)\n- Semantic: User seeks always override auto-reset logic\n\n**Test: `testOffsetIsValidAfterSeek(GroupProtocol groupProtocol)` - Line 1160**\n- After `seek(tp0, 20L)` and poll, position is exactly 20L\n- Position is valid immediately after seek (doesn't require fetch)\n\n**Test: `testOffsetOfPausedPartitions(GroupProtocol groupProtocol)` - Line 1854**\n- `position()` works on paused partitions\n- Returns actual end offset even if partition is paused\n- Semantic: pause doesn't affect position tracking\n\n**Test: `testCommitsFetchedDuringAssign(GroupProtocol groupProtocol)` - Line 1177**\n- Committed offsets fetched fresh for each partition in assign call\n- Multiple `committed()` calls trigger coordinator requests\n- Each partition may have different committed offset\n\n### 6. Non-Obvious Behavioral Edge Cases\n\n**Wakeup Semantics:**\n- `testWakeupBeforeCallingPoll()` - Wakeup before poll throws immediately\n- `testWakeupAfterEmptyFetch()` - Wakeup can occur during fetch collection\n- `testWakeupAfterNonEmptyFetch()` - Wakeup interrupts data return\n- Key insight: Wakeup does NOT advance position\n\n**Poll State Validation:**\n- Empty assignment throws IllegalStateException (even with empty set)\n- Only subscription+assignment (non-empty) or manual assign allows polling\n\n**Callback Exception Propagation:**\n- Framework wraps non-KafkaException as `KafkaException`\n- Original cause preserved in wrapped exception\n- Assignment state updates despite exceptions\n\n**Coordinator Discovery:**\n- `FindCoordinatorResponse` requests happen before any offset operations\n- Coordinator election may fail (separate exception types)\n- Retry logic with backoff applied\n\n**Metadata Updates:**\n- Epoch information from responses updates consumer metadata\n- Important for conflict resolution with other consumers\n\n## Summary of Key Insights\n\n1. **Position doesn't advance on wakeup** - critical for exactly-once semantics\n2. **Auto-commit happens BEFORE user sees records** - timing sensitive\n3. **State updates despite callback exceptions** - resilient design\n4. **Seek overrides pending resets** - user intent takes precedence\n5. **Async commit doesn't retry on retriable errors** - different from sync behavior\n6. **Multiple partition errors = single attempt** - batching semantics\n7. **Deserialization error stops position** - allows recovery attempts\n8. **Protocol differences** - EAGER vs COOPERATIVE have different rebalance behaviors\n9. **Empty maps handled specially** - no coordinator interaction needed\n10. **Thread interruption state persists** - tests explicitly clean up\n\nThese behaviors are essential for implementing reliable Kafka consumers with proper offset management, fault tolerance, and rebalancing resilience.",
      "type": "text"
    }
  ],
  "prompt": "Find and read test files related to KafkaConsumer in the github.com/sg-benchmarks/kafka--e678b4b repository. Look for:\n\n1. Test file names that contain \"Consumer\" and \"Test\" (e.g., *ConsumerTest.java, *ConsumerIntegrationTest.java)\n2. For the most relevant test files, extract:\n   - Test methods that demonstrate poll() behavior and blocking\n   - Test methods for offset commit strategies (sync vs async)\n   - Test methods for rebalancing and ConsumerRebalanceListener callbacks\n   - Test methods for exception handling and error scenarios\n   - Test methods for seek operations and position management\n\nReturn key insights about behavioral semantics that tests demonstrate, with specific test names and what they verify. Focus on edge cases and non-obvious behaviors.",
  "status": "completed",
  "totalDurationMs": 100677,
  "totalTokens": 81492,
  "totalToolUseCount": 56,
  "usage": {

  }
}

props.setProperty(ConsumerConfig.GROUP_PROTOCOL_CONFIG, groupProtocol.name());\\n356:             props.setProperty(ConsumerConfig.BOOTSTRAP_SERVERS_CONFIG, \\\"localhost:9999\\\");\\n357: consumer.metrics().entrySet().iterator().next().getValue();\\n360:             consumer.unregisterMetricFromSubscription(existingMetric);\\n361:             Mockito.verify(clientTelemetryReporter, never()).metricRemoval(existingMetric);\\n362:         }\\n363:     }\\n364: \\n365:     @ParameterizedTest\\n366:     @EnumSource(GroupProtocol.class)\\n367:     public void testUnSubscribingNonExisingMetricsDoesntCauseError(GroupProtocol groupProtocol) {\\n368:         Properties props = new Properties();\\n369: registered but removed should not cause an error\\n375:         customMetrics.forEach((name, metric) -> assertDoesNotThrow(() -> consumer.unregisterMetricFromSubscription(metric)));\\n376: "{\"content\":\"437:     @SuppressWarnings(\\\"unchecked\\\")\\n438:     public void testPollReturnsRecords(GroupProtocol groupProtocol) {\\n439:         consumer = setUpConsumerWithRecordsToPoll(groupProtocol, tp0, 5);\\n440: \\n441:         ConsumerRecords<String, String> records = (ConsumerRecords<String, String>) consumer.poll(Duration.ZERO);\\n442: \\n443:         assertEquals(5, records.count());\\n444:         assertEquals(Set.of(tp0), records.partitions());\\n445:         assertEquals(5, records.records(tp0).size());\\n446:         assertEquals(1, records.nextOffsets().size());\\n447:         assertEquals(new OffsetAndMetadata(5), records.nextOffsets().get(tp0));\\n448:     }\\n449: \\n450:     // TODO: this test requires rebalance logic which is not yet implemented in the CONSUMER group protocol.\\n451:     //       Once it is implemented, this should use both group protocols.\\n452:     @ParameterizedTest\\n453:     @EnumSource(value = GroupProtocol.class, names = \\\"CLASSIC\\\")\\n454:     @SuppressWarnings(\\\"unchecked\\\")\\n455:     public void testSecondPollWithDeserializationErrorThrowsRecordDeserializationException(GroupProtocol groupProtocol) {\\n456:         int invalidRecordNumber = 4;\\n457:         int invalidRecordOffset = 3;\\n458:         StringDeserializer deserializer = mockErrorDeserializer(invalidRecordNumber);\\n459: \\n460:         consumer = setUpConsumerWithRecordsToPoll(groupProtocol, tp0, 5, deserializer);\\n461:         ConsumerRecords<String, String> records = (ConsumerRecords<String, String>) consumer.poll(Duration.ZERO);\\n462: \\n463:         assertEquals(invalidRecordNumber - 1, records.count());\\n464:         assertEquals(Set.of(tp0), records.partitions());\\n465:         assertEquals(invalidRecordNumber - 1, records.records(tp0).size());\\n466:         long lastOffset = records.records(tp0).get(records.records(tp0).size() - 1).offset();\\n467:         assertEquals(invalidRecordNumber - 2, lastOffset);\\n468:         assertEquals(1, records.nextOffsets().size());\\n469:         assertEquals(new OffsetAndMetadata(lastOffset + 1), records.nextOffsets().get(tp0));\\n470: \\n471:         RecordDeserializationException rde = assertThrows(RecordDeserializationException.class, () -> consumer.poll(Duration.ZERO));\\n472:         assertEquals(invalidRecordOffset, rde.offset());\\n473:         assertEquals(tp0, rde.topicPartition());\\n474:         assertEquals(consumer.position(tp0), rde.offset());\\n475:     }\\n476: \\n477:     /*\\n478:         Create a mock deserializer which throws a SerializationException on the Nth record's value deserialization\\n479:      */\\n480:     private StringDeserializer mockErrorDeserializer(int recordNumber) {\\n481:         int recordIndex = recordNumber - 1;\\n482:         return new StringDeserializer() {\\n483:             int i = 0;\\n484:             @Override\\n485:             public String deserialize(String topic, byte[] data) {\\n486:                 if (i == recordIndex) {\\n487:                     throw new SerializationException();\\n488:                 } else {\\n489:                     i++;\\n490:                     return super.deserialize(topic, data);\\n491:                 }\\n492:             }\\n493: \\n494:             @Override\\n495:             public String deserialize(String topic, Headers headers, ByteBuffer data) {\\n496:                 if (i == recordIndex) {\\n497:                     throw new SerializationException();\\n498:                 } else {\\n499:                     i++;\\n500:                     return super.deserialize(topic, headers, data);\\n501: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-benchmarks/kafka--e678b4b@47ac12bd6042edf23f1dbca3d789db945d62802d/-/blob/clients/src/test/java/org/apache/kafka/clients/consumer/KafkaConsumerTest.java%3FL437-500\"}"

"{\"content\":\"1295:     @EnumSource(value = GroupProtocol.class, names = \\\"CLASSIC\\\")\\n1296:     public void testAutoCommitSentBeforePositionUpdate(GroupProtocol groupProtocol) {\\n1297:        Node node = metadata.fetch().nodes().get(0);\\n1302: \\n1303:         consumer = newConsumer(groupProtocol, time, client, subscription, metadata, assignor, true, groupInstanceId);\\n1304:         consumer.subscribe(Set.of(topic), getConsumerRebalanceListener(consumer));\\n1305:         Node coordinator = prepareRebalance(client, node, assignor, List.of(tp0), null);\\n1306: \\n1307:         consumer.updateAssignmentMetadataIfNeeded(time.timer(Long.MAX_VALUE));\\n1308:         consumer.poll(Duration.ZERO);\\n1309: \\n1310:         // respond to the outstanding fetch so that we have data available on the next poll\\n1311:         client.respondFrom(fetchResponse(tp0, 0, 5), node);\\n1312:         client.poll(0, time.milliseconds());\\n1313: \\n1314:         time.sleep(autoCommitIntervalMs);\\n1315: \\n1316:         client.prepareResponseFrom(fetchResponse(tp0, 5, 0), node);\\n1317: \\n1318:         // no data has been returned to the user yet, so the committed offset should be 0\\n1319:         AtomicBoolean commitReceived = prepareOffsetCommitResponse(client, coordinator, tp0, 0);\\n1320: \\n1321:         consumer.poll(Duration.ZERO);\\n1322: \\n1323:         assertTrue(commitReceived.get());\\n1324:     }\\n1325: \\n1326:     // TODO: this test requires rebalance logic which is not yet implemented in the CONSUMER group protocol.\\n1327: void testRegexSubscription(GroupProtocol groupProtocol) {\\n1331:         String unmatchedTopic = \\\"unmatched\\\";\\n1332:         ConsumerMetadata metadata = createMetadata(subscription);\\n1333:  partitionCounts.put(unmatchedTopic, 1);\\n1338:         topicIds.put(unmatchedTopic, Uuid.randomUuid());\\n1339:         initMetadata(client, partitionCounts);\\n1340:         Node node = metadata.fetch().nodes().get(0);\\n1341: \\n1342:         consumer = newConsumer(groupProtocol, time, client, subscription, metadata, assignor, true, groupInstanceId);\\n1343:         prepareRebalance(client, node, Set.of(topic), assignor, List.of(tp0), null);\\n1344: \\n1345:         consumer.subscribe(Pattern.compile(topic), getConsumerRebalanceListener(consumer));\\n1346: \\n1347:         client.prepareMetadataUpdate(RequestTestUtils.metadataUpdateWithIds(1, partitionCounts, topicIds));\\n1348: \\n1349:         consumer.updateAssignmentMetadataIfNeeded(time.timer(Long.MAX_VALUE));\\n1350: \\n1351:       Node node = metadata.fetch().nodes().get(0);\\n2430: \\n2431:         KafkaConsumer<String, String> consumer = newConsumer(groupProtocol, time, client, subscription, metadata, assignor, true, node.host(), node.port());\\n2435: \\n2436:         client.prepareResponseFrom(FindCoordinatorResponse.prepareResponse(Errors.NONE, groupId, node), node);\\n2437:         client.prepareResponseFrom(joinGroupFollowerResponse(assignor, 1, memberId, leaderId, Errors.NONE), coordinator);\\n2438:         client.prepareResponseFrom(syncGroupResponse(List.of(tp0), Errors.NONE), coordinator);\\n2439: \\n2440:         // assign throws\\n2441:         KafkaException exc = assertThrows(KafkaException.class, () -> consumer.updateAssignmentMetadataIfNeeded(time.timer(Long.MAX_VALUE)));\\n2442:         assertEquals(partitionAssigned + singleTopicPartition, exc.getCause().getMessage());\\n2443: \\n2444:         // the assignment is still updated regardless of the exception\\n2445:         assertEquals(Set.of(tp0), subscription.assignedPartitions());\\n2446: \\n2447:         // close's revoke throws\\n2448:         exc = assertThrows(KafkaException.class, () -> consumer.close(CloseOptions.timeout(Duration.ofMillis(0))));\\n2449:         assertEquals(partitionRevoked + singleTopicPartition, exc.getCause().getCause().getMessage());\\n2450: \\n2451:         consumer.close(CloseOptions.timeout(Duration.ofMillis(0)));\\n2452: \\n2453:         // the assignment is still updated regardless of the exception\\n2454:         assertTrue(subscription.assignedPartitions().isEmpty());\\n2455:     }\\n2456: \\n2457:     // TODO: this test requires rebalance logic which is not yet implemented in the CONSUMER group protocol.\\n2458:     //       Once it is implemented, this should use both group protocols.\\n2459:     @ParameterizedTest\\n2460:     @EnumSource(value = GroupProtocol.class, names = \\\"CLASSIC\\\")\\n2461:     public void testReturnRecordsDuringRebalance(GroupProtocol groupProtocol) throws InterruptedException {\\n2462:         Time time = new MockTime(1L);\\n2463:         ConsumerMetadata metadata = createMetadata(subscription);\\n2464:         MockClient client = new MockClient(time, metadata);\\n2465:         ConsumerPartitionAssignor assignor = new CooperativeStickyAssignor();\\n2466:         KafkaConsumer<String, String> consumer = newConsumer(groupProtocol, time, client, subscription, metadata, assignor, true, groupInstanceId);\\n2467: \\n2468:         initMetadata(client, Utils.mkMap(Utils.mkEntry(topic, 1), Utils.mkEntry(topic2, 1), Utils.mkEntry(topic3, 1)));\\n2469: \\n2470:         consumer.subscribe(Arrays.asList(topic, topic2), getConsumerRebalanceListener(consumer));\\n2471: \\n2472:         Node node = metadata.fetch().nodes().get(0);\\n2473:         Node coordinator = prepareRebalance(client, node, assignor, Arrays.asList(tp0, t2p0), null);\\n2474: \\n2475:         // a poll with non-zero milliseconds would complete three round-trips (discover, join, sync)\\n2476:         TestUtils.waitForCondition(() -> {\\n2477:             consumer.poll(Duration.ofMillis(100L));\\n2478:             return consumer.assignment().equals(Set.of(tp0, t2p0));\\n2479:         }, \\\"Does not complete rebalance in time\\\");\\n2480: \\n2481:         assertEquals(Set.of(topic, topic2), consumer.subscription());\\n2482:         assertEquals(Set.of(tp0, t2p0), consumer.assignment());\\n2483: \\n2484:         // prepare a response of the outstanding fetch so that we have data available on the next poll\\n2485:         Map<TopicPartition, FetchInfo> fetches1 = new HashMap<>();\\n2486:         fetches1.put(tp0, new FetchInfo(0, 1));\\n2487:         fetches1.put(t2p0, new FetchInfo(0, 10));\\n2488:         client.respondFrom(fetchResponse(fetches1), node);\\n2489: \\n2490:         ConsumerRecords<String, String> records = consumer.poll(Duration.ZERO);\\n2491: \\n2492:         // verify that the fetch occurred as expected\\n2493:         assertEquals(11, records.count());\\n2494:         assertEquals(1L, consumer.position(tp0));\\n2495:         assertEquals(10L, consumer.position(t2p0));\\n2496:         assertEquals(2, records.nextOffsets().size());\\n2497:         assertEquals(new OffsetAndMetadata(1L), records.nextOffsets().get(tp0));\\n2498:         assertEquals(new OffsetAndMetadata(10L), records.nextOffsets().get(t2p0));\\n2499: \\n2500:         // prepare the next response of the prefetch\\n2501: "{\"content\":\"1395:     @EnumSource(value = GroupProtocol.class, names = \\\"CLASSIC\\\")\\n1396:     public void testWakeupWithFetchDataAvailable(GroupProtocol groupProtocol) throws Exception {\\n1397:         ConsumerMetadata metadata = createMetadata(subscription);\\n1398:         MockClient client = new MockClient(time, metadata);\\n1399: \\n1400:         initMetadata(client, Map.of(topic, 1));\\n1401:         Node node = metadata.fetch().nodes().get(0);\\n1402: \\n1403:         consumer = newConsumer(groupProtocol, time, client, subscription, metadata, assignor, true, groupInstanceId);\\n1404:         consumer.subscribe(Set.of(topic), getConsumerRebalanceListener(consumer));\\n1405:         prepareRebalance(client, node, assignor, List.of(tp0), null);\\n1406: \\n1407:         consumer.updateAssignmentMetadataIfNeeded(time.timer(Long.MAX_VALUE));\\n1408:         consumer.poll(Duration.ZERO);\\n1409: \\n1410:         // respond to the outstanding fetch so that we have data available on the next poll\\n1411:         client.respondFrom(fetchResponse(tp0, 0, 5), node);\\n1412:         client.poll(0, time.milliseconds());\\n1413: \\n1414:         consumer.wakeup();\\n1415: \\n1416:         assertThrows(WakeupException.class, () -> consumer.poll(Duration.ZERO));\\n1417: \\n1418:         // make sure the position hasn't been updated\\n1419:         assertEquals(0, consumer.position(tp0));\\n1420: \\n1421:         // the next poll should return the completed fetch\\n1422:         @SuppressWarnings(\\\"unchecked\\\")\\n1423:         ConsumerRecords<String, String> records = (ConsumerRecords<String, String>) consumer.poll(Duration.ZERO);\\n1424:         assertEquals(5, records.count());\\n1425:         assertEquals(1, records.nextOffsets().size());\\n1426:         assertEquals(new OffsetAndMetadata(5), records.nextOffsets().get(tp0));\\n1427:         // Increment time asynchronously to clear timeouts in closing the consumer\\n1428:         final ScheduledExecutorService exec = Executors.newSingleThreadScheduledExecutor();\\n1429:         exec.scheduleAtFixedRate(() -> time.sleep(sessionTimeoutMs), 0L, 10L, TimeUnit.MILLISECONDS);\\n1430:         consumer.close();\\n1431:         exec.shutdownNow();\\n1432:         exec.awaitTermination(5L, TimeUnit.SECONDS);\\n1433:     }\\n1434: \\n1435:     @ParameterizedTest\\n1436: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-benchmarks/kafka--e678b4b@47ac12bd6042edf23f1dbca3d789db945d62802d/-/blob/clients/src/test/java/org/apache/kafka/clients/consumer/KafkaConsumerTest.java%3FL1395-1435\"}"

"{\"content\":\"1071:     @Test\\n1072:     public void testOffsetCommitAsyncFailedWithRetriableThrowsRetriableCommitException() {\\n1073:         CommitRequestManager commitRequestManager = create(true, 100);\\n1074:         when(coordinatorRequestManager.coordinator()).thenReturn(Optional.of(mockedNode));\\n1075: \\n1076:         Map<TopicPartition, OffsetAndMetadata> offsets = Collections.singletonMap(new TopicPartition(\\\"topic\\\", 1),\\n1077:             new OffsetAndMetadata(0));\\n1078: \\n1079:         // Send async commit request that fails with retriable error (not expected to be retried).\\n1080:         Errors retriableError = Errors.COORDINATOR_NOT_AVAILABLE;\\n1081:         CompletableFuture<Map<TopicPartition, OffsetAndMetadata>> commitResult = commitRequestManager.commitAsync(offsets);\\n1082:         completeOffsetCommitRequestWithError(commitRequestManager, retriableError);\\n1083:         NetworkClientDelegate.PollResult res = commitRequestManager.poll(time.milliseconds());\\n1084:         assertEquals(0, res.unsentRequests.size());\\n1085:         assertTrue(commitResult.isDone());\\n1086:         assertTrue(commitResult.isCompletedExceptionally());\\n1087: \\n1088:         // We expect that the request should not have been retried on this async commit.\\n1089:         assertExceptionHandling(commitRequestManager, retriableError, false);\\n1090: \\n1091:         // Request should complete with a RetriableCommitException\\n1092:         assertFutureThrows(RetriableCommitFailedException.class, commitResult);\\n1093:     }\\n1094: \\n1095:     @ParameterizedTest\\n1096:     @MethodSource(\\\"offsetCommitExceptionSupplier\\\")\\n1097:     public void testOffsetCommitSingleFailedAttemptPerRequestWhenPartitionErrors(final Errors error) {\\n1098:         CommitRequestManager commitRequestManager = create(true, 100);\\n1099:         when(coordinatorRequestManager.coordinator()).thenReturn(Optional.of(mockedNode));\\n1100: \\n1101:         Map<TopicPartition, OffsetAndMetadata> offsets = new HashMap<>();\\n1102:         offsets.put(new TopicPartition(\\\"t1\\\", 0), new OffsetAndMetadata(1));\\n1103:         offsets.put(new TopicPartition(\\\"t1\\\", 1), new OffsetAndMetadata(2));\\n1104:         offsets.put(new TopicPartition(\\\"t1\\\", 2), new OffsetAndMetadata(3));\\n1105: \\n1106:         commitRequestManager.commitSync(offsets, time.milliseconds() + defaultApiTimeoutMs);\\n1107:         NetworkClientDelegate.PollResult res = commitRequestManager.poll(time.milliseconds());\\n1108:         assertEquals(1, res.unsentRequests.size());\\n1109: \\n1110:         res.unsentRequests.get(0).handler().onComplete(mockOffsetCommitResponse(\\\"topic\\\", (short) 1, error, offsets.size()));\\n1111:         CommitRequestManager.OffsetCommitRequestState commitRequest = commitRequestManager.pendingRequests.unsentOffsetCommits.peek();\\n1112:         if (error.exception() instanceof RetriableException) {\\n1113:             assertNotNull(commitRequest);\\n1114:             assertEquals(1, commitRequest.numAttempts, \\\"Only one failed attempt should be registered, even if the response contains multiple partition errors\\\");\\n1115:             time.sleep(retryBackoffMs);\\n1116:             res = commitRequestManager.poll(time.milliseconds());\\n1117:             res.unsentRequests.get(0).handler().onComplete(mockOffsetCommitResponse(\\\"topic\\\", (short) 1, error, offsets.size()));\\n1118:             assertEquals(2, commitRequest.numAttempts, \\\"Only one failed attempt should be registered, even if the response contains multiple partition errors\\\");\\n1119:         } else assertNull(commitRequest);\\n1120:     }\\n1121: \\n1122:     @Test\\n1123:     public void testEnsureBackoffRetryOnOffsetCommitRequestTimeout() {\\n1124:         CommitRequestManager commitRequestManager = create(true, 100);\\n1125:         when(coordinatorRequestManager.coordinator()).thenReturn(Optional.of(mockedNode));\\n1126: \\n1127:         Map<TopicPartition, OffsetAndMetadata> offsets = Collections.singletonMap(new TopicPartition(\\\"topic\\\", 1),\\n1128:             new OffsetAndMetadata(0));\\n1129: \\n1130:         long deadlineMs = time.milliseconds() + defaultApiTimeoutMs;\\n1131: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-benchmarks/kafka--e678b4b@47ac12bd6042edf23f1dbca3d789db945d62802d/-/blob/clients/src/test/java/org/apache/kafka/clients/consumer/internals/CommitRequestManagerTest.java%3FL1071-1130\"}"

"{\"content\":\"801:     @Test\\n802:     public void testOnPartitionsAssignExceptionThrownWhenNoPreviousThrownCallbacks() {\\n803:         MockRebalanceListener throwOnAssignListener = new MockRebalanceListener() {\\n804:             @Override\\n805:             public void onPartitionsAssigned(Collection<TopicPartition> partitions) {\\n806:                 super.onPartitionsAssigned(partitions);\\n807:                 throw new KafkaException(\\\"Kaboom on partition assign!\\\");\\n808:             }\\n809:         };\\n810: \\n811:         verifyOnCallbackExceptions(throwOnAssignListener,\\n812:             partitionAssignor.name(), \\\"Kaboom on partition assign!\\\", null);\\n813:     }\\n814: \\n815:     @Test\\n816:     public void testOnRevokeExceptionShouldBeRenderedIfNotKafkaException() {\\n817:         MockRebalanceListener throwOnRevokeListener = new MockRebalanceListener() {\\n818:             @Override\\n819:             public void onPartitionsRevoked(Collection<TopicPartition> partitions) {\\n820:                 super.onPartitionsRevoked(partitions);\\n821:                 throw new IllegalStateException(\\\"Illegal state on partition revoke!\\\");\\n822:             }\\n823:         };\\n824: \\n825:         if (protocol == COOPERATIVE) {\\n826:             verifyOnCallbackExceptions(throwOnRevokeListener,\\n827:                 throwOnAssignmentAssignor.name(),\\n828:                 \\\"User rebalance callback throws an error\\\", \\\"Illegal state on partition revoke!\\\");\\n829:         } else {\\n830:             // Eager protocol doesn't revoke partitions.\\n831:             verifyOnCallbackExceptions(throwOnRevokeListener,\\n832:                 throwOnAssignmentAssignor.name(), \\\"Kaboom for assignment!\\\", null);\\n833:         }\\n834:     }\\n835: \\n836:     @Test\\n837:     public void testOnAssignmentExceptionShouldBeRenderedIfNotKafkaException() {\\n838:         MockRebalanceListener throwOnAssignListener = new MockRebalanceListener() {\\n839:             @Override\\n840:             public void onPartitionsAssigned(Collection<TopicPartition> partitions) {\\n841:                 super.onPartitionsAssigned(partitions);\\n842:                 throw new KafkaException(\\\"Kaboom on partition assign!\\\");\\n843:             }\\n844:         };\\n845:         verifyOnCallbackExceptions(throwOnAssignListener,\\n846:             throwFatalErrorOnAssignmentAssignor.name(),\\n847:             \\\"User rebalance callback throws an error\\\", \\\"Illegal state for assignment!\\\");\\n848:     }\\n849: \\n850:     @Test\\n851:     public void testOnPartitionsAssignExceptionShouldBeRenderedIfNotKafkaException() {\\n852:         MockRebalanceListener throwOnAssignListener = new MockRebalanceListener() {\\n853:             @Override\\n854:             public void onPartitionsAssigned(Collection<TopicPartition> partitions) {\\n855:                 super.onPartitionsAssigned(partitions);\\n856:                 throw new IllegalStateException(\\\"Illegal state on partition assign!\\\");\\n857:             }\\n858:         };\\n859: \\n860:         verifyOnCallbackExceptions(throwOnAssignListener,\\n861:             partitionAssignor.name(), \\\"User rebalance callback throws an error\\\",\\n862:             \\\"Illegal state on partition assign!\\\");\\n863:     }\\n864: \\n865:     private void verifyOnCallbackExceptions(final MockRebalanceListener rebalanceListener,\\n866:                                             final String assignorName,\\n867:                                             final String exceptionMessage,\\n868:                                             final String causeMessage) {\\n869:         client.prepareResponse(groupCoordinatorResponse(node, Errors.NONE));\\n870:         coordinator.ensureCoordinatorReady(time.timer(Long.MAX_VALUE));\\n871: \\n872:         subscriptions.subscribe(singleton(topic1), Optional.of(rebalanceListener));\\n873:         ByteBuffer buffer = ConsumerProtocol.serializeAssignment(\\n874:             new ConsumerPartitionAssignor.Assignment(Collections.singletonList(t1p), ByteBuffer.wrap(new byte[0])));\\n875:         subscriptions.assignFromSubscribed(singleton(t2p));\\n876: \\n877:         if (exceptionMessage != null) {\\n878:             final Exception exception = assertThrows(KafkaException.class,\\n879:                 () -> coordinator.onJoinComplete(1, \\\"memberId\\\", assignorName, buffer));\\n880:             assertEquals(exceptionMessage, exception.getMessage());\\n881:             if (causeMessage != null) {\\n882:                 assertEquals(causeMessage, exception.getCause().getMessage());\\n883:             }\\n884:         }\\n885: \\n886:         // Eager doesn't trigger on partition revoke.\\n887:         assertEquals(protocol == COOPERATIVE ? 1 : 0, rebalanceListener.revokedCount);\\n888:         assertEquals(0, rebalanceListener.lostCount);\\n889:         assertEquals(1, rebalanceListener.assignedCount);\\n890:         assertTrue(assignorMap.containsKey(assignorName), \\\"Unknown assignor name: \\\" + assignorName);\\n891:         assertEquals(1, assignorMap.get(assignorName).numAssignment());\\n892:     }\\n893: \\n894:     @Test\\n895:     public void testUnsubscribeWithInvalidGeneration() {\\n896:         client.prepareResponse(groupCoordinatorResponse(node, Errors.NONE));\\n897:         coordinator.ensureCoordinatorReady(time.timer(Long.MAX_VALUE));\\n898: \\n899:         subscriptions.subscribe(singleton(topic1), Optional.of(rebalanceListener));\\n900:         subscriptions.assignFromSubscribed(Collections.singletonList(t1p));\\n901: "{\"content\":\"461:     @Test\\n462:     public void testSeekWithInFlightReset() {\\n463:         buildFetcher();\\n464:         assignFromUser(singleton(tp0));\\n465:         subscriptions.requestOffsetReset(tp0, AutoOffsetResetStrategy.LATEST);\\n466: \\n467:         // Send the ListOffsets request to reset the position\\n468:         offsetFetcher.resetPositionsIfNeeded();\\n469:         consumerClient.pollNoWakeup();\\n470:         assertFalse(subscriptions.hasValidPosition(tp0));\\n471:         assertTrue(client.hasInFlightRequests());\\n472: \\n473:         // Now we get a seek from the user\\n474:         subscriptions.seek(tp0, 237);\\n475: \\n476:         // The response returns and is discarded\\n477:         client.respond(listOffsetResponse(Errors.NONE, 1L, 5L));\\n478:         consumerClient.pollNoWakeup();\\n479: \\n480:         assertFalse(client.hasPendingResponses());\\n481:         assertFalse(client.hasInFlightRequests());\\n482:         assertEquals(237L, subscriptions.position(tp0).offset);\\n483:     }\\n484: \\n485:     private boolean listOffsetMatchesExpectedReset(\\n486:         TopicPartition tp,\\n487:         AutoOffsetResetStrategy strategy,\\n488:         AbstractRequest request\\n489:     ) {\\n490:         assertInstanceOf(ListOffsetsRequest.class, request);\\n491: \\n492:         ListOffsetsRequest req = (ListOffsetsRequest) request;\\n493:         assertEquals(singleton(tp.topic()), req.data().topics().stream()\\n494:             .map(ListOffsetsTopic::name).collect(Collectors.toSet()));\\n495: \\n496:         ListOffsetsTopic listTopic = req.data().topics().get(0);\\n497:         assertEquals(singleton(tp.partition()), listTopic.partitions().stream()\\n498:             .map(ListOffsetsPartition::partitionIndex).collect(Collectors.toSet()));\\n499: \\n500:         ListOffsetsPartition listPartition = listTopic.partitions().get(0);\\n501:         if (strategy == AutoOffsetResetStrategy.EARLIEST) {\\n502:             assertEquals(ListOffsetsRequest.EARLIEST_TIMESTAMP, listPartition.timestamp());\\n503:         } else if (strategy == AutoOffsetResetStrategy.LATEST) {\\n504:             assertEquals(ListOffsetsRequest.LATEST_TIMESTAMP, listPartition.timestamp());\\n505:         }\\n506:         return true;\\n507:     }\\n508: \\n509:     @Test\\n510:     public void testEarlierOffsetResetArrivesLate() {\\n511:         buildFetcher();\\n512:         assignFromUser(singleton(tp0));\\n513: \\n514:         subscriptions.requestOffsetReset(tp0, AutoOffsetResetStrategy.EARLIEST);\\n515:         offsetFetcher.resetPositionsIfNeeded();\\n516: \\n517:         client.prepareResponse(req -> {\\n518:             if (listOffsetMatchesExpectedReset(tp0, AutoOffsetResetStrategy.EARLIEST, req)) {\\n519:                 // Before the response is handled, we get a request to reset to the latest offset\\n520:                 subscriptions.requestOffsetReset(tp0, AutoOffsetResetStrategy.LATEST);\\n521: mock(ConsumerRebalanceListenerInvoker.class),\\n763:             subscriptions);\\n764:         completeTopicSubscriptionChangeEventSuccessfully();\\n765:         consumer.subscribe(singleton(\\\"topic\\\"), mock(ConsumerRebalanceListener.class));\\n766:         subscriptions.assignFromSubscribed(singleton(new TopicPartition(\\\"topic\\\", 0)));\\n767:         completeSeekUnvalidatedEventSuccessfully();\\n768:         subscriptions.seek(new TopicPartition(\\\"topic\\\", 0), 100);\\n769:         markOffsetsReadyForCommitEvent();\\n770:         consumer.commitSyncAllConsumed(time.timer(100));\\n771: \\n772:         ArgumentCaptor<SyncCommitEvent> eventCaptor = ArgumentCaptor.forClass(SyncCommitEvent.class);\\n773:         verify(applicationEventHandler).add(eventCaptor.capture());\\n774:         SyncCommitEvent capturedEvent = eventCaptor.getValue();\\n775:         assertFalse(capturedEvent.offsets().isPresent(), \\\"Expected empty optional offsets\\\");\\n776:     }\\n777: \\n778:     @Test\\n779:     public void testAutoCommitSyncDisabled() {\\n780:         SubscriptionState subscriptions = new SubscriptionState(new LogContext(), AutoOffsetResetStrategy.NONE);\\n781:         consumer = newConsumer(\\n782:             mock(FetchBuffer.class),\\n783:             mock(ConsumerInterceptors.class),\\n784:             mock(ConsumerRebalanceListenerInvoker.class),\\n785:             subscriptions);\\n786: never()).add(any(SyncCommitEvent.class));\\n794:     }\\n795: \\n796:     private void assertMockCommitCallbackInvoked(final Executable task, final MockCommitCallback callback) {\\n797:         assertDoesNotThrow(task);\\n798:         assertEquals(1, callback.invoked);\\n799:         assertNull(callback.exception);\\n800:     }\\n801: \\n802:     private static class MockCommitCallback implements OffsetCommitCallback {\\n803:         public int invoked = 0;\\n804:         public Exception exception = null;\\n805:         public String completionThread;\\n806: \\n807:         @Override\\n808:         public void onComplete(Map<TopicPartition, OffsetAndMetadata> offsets, Exception exception) {\\n809:             invoked++;\\n810:             this.completionThread = Thread.currentThread().getName();\\n811:             this.exception = exception;\\n812:         }\\n813:     }\\n814: \\n815:     @Test\\n816:     public void testAssign() {\\n817:         consumer = newConsumer();\\n818:         final TopicPartition tp = new TopicPartition(\\\"foo\\\", 3);\\n819:         completeAssignmentChangeEventSuccessfully();\\n820:         consumer.assign(singleton(tp));\\n821:
