assertThrows(IllegalStateException.class, () -> consumer.poll(Duration.ZERO));\\n1908:     }\\n1909: \\n1910:     @ParameterizedTest\\n1911:     @EnumSource(GroupProtocol.class)\\n1912:     public void testPollWithEmptySubscription(GroupProtocol groupProtocol) {\\n1913:         consumer = newConsumer(groupProtocol, groupId);\\n1914:         consumer.subscribe(Collections.emptyList());\\n1915:         assertThrows(IllegalStateException.class, () -> consumer.poll(Duration.ZERO));\\n1916:     }\\n1917: \\n1918:     @ParameterizedTest\\n1919:     @EnumSource(GroupProtocol.class)\\n1920:     public void testPollWithEmptyUserAssignment(GroupProtocol groupProtocol) {\\n1921:         consumer = newConsumer(groupProtocol, groupId);\\n1922:         consumer.assign(Collections.emptySet());\\n1923:         assertThrows(IllegalStateException.class, () -> consumer.poll(Duration.ZERO));\\n1924:     }\\n1925: \\n1926:     // TODO: this test references RPCs to be sent that are not part of the CONSUMER group protocol.\\n1927:     //       We are deferring any attempts at generalizing this test for both group protocols to the future.\\n1928:     @ParameterizedTest\\n1929:     @EnumSource(value = GroupProtocol.class, names = \\\"CLASSIC\\\")\\n1930:     public void testGracefulClose(GroupProtocol groupProtocol) throws Exception {\\n1931:         Map<TopicPartition, Errors> response = new HashMap<>();\\n1932:         response.put(tp0, Errors.NONE);\\n1933:         OffsetCommitResponse commitResponse = offsetCommitResponse(response);\\n1934:         LeaveGroupResponse leaveGroupResponse = new LeaveGroupResponse(new LeaveGroupResponseData().setErrorCode(Errors.NONE.code()));\\n1935:         FetchResponse closeResponse = FetchResponse.of(Errors.NONE, 0, INVALID_SESSION_ID, new LinkedHashMap<>(), List.of());\\n1936:         consumerCloseTest(groupProtocol, 5000, Arrays.asList(commitResponse, leaveGroupResponse, closeResponse), 0, false);\\n1937:     }\\n1938: \\n1939:     // TODO: this test references RPCs to be sent that are not part of the CONSUMER group protocol.\\n1940:     //       We are deferring any attempts at generalizing this test for both group protocols to the future.\\n1941:     @ParameterizedTest\\n1942:     @EnumSource(value = GroupProtocol.class, names = \\\"CLASSIC\\\")\\n1943:     public void testCloseTimeoutDueToNoResponseForCloseFetchRequest(GroupProtocol groupProtocol) throws Exception {\\n1944:         Map<TopicPartition, Errors> response = new HashMap<>();\\n1945:         response.put(tp0, Errors.NONE);\\n1946:         OffsetCommitResponse commitResponse = offsetCommitResponse(response);\\n1947:         LeaveGroupResponse leaveGroupResponse = new LeaveGroupResponse(new LeaveGroupResponseData().setErrorCode(Errors.NONE.code()));\\n1948:         final List<AbstractResponse> serverResponsesWithoutCloseResponse = Arrays.asList(commitResponse, leaveGroupResponse);\\n1949: \\n1950:         // to ensure timeout due to no response for fetcher close request, we will ensure that we have successful\\n1951:         // response from server for first two requests and the test is configured to wait for duration which is greater\\n1952:         // than configured timeout.\\n1953:         final int closeTimeoutMs = 5000;\\n1954:         final int waitForCloseCompletionMs = closeTimeoutMs + 1000;\\n1955:         consumerCloseTest(groupProtocol, closeTimeoutMs, serverResponsesWithoutCloseResponse, waitForCloseCompletionMs, false);\\n1956:     }\\n1957: \\n1958:     // TODO: this test requires rebalance logic which is not yet implemented in the CONSUMER group protocol.\\n1959:     //       Once it is implemented, this should use both group protocols.\\n1960:     @ParameterizedTest\\n1961:     @EnumSource(value = GroupProtocol.class, names = \\\"CLASSIC\\\")\\n1962:     public void testCloseTimeout(GroupProtocol groupProtocol) throws "{\"content\":\"1159:     @EnumSource(GroupProtocol.class)\\n1160:     public void testOffsetIsValidAfterSeek(GroupProtocol groupProtocol) {\\n1161:         SubscriptionState subscription = new SubscriptionState(new LogContext(), AutoOffsetResetStrategy.LATEST);\\n1162:         ConsumerMetadata metadata = createMetadata(subscription);\\n1163:         MockClient client = new MockClient(time, metadata);\\n1164: \\n1165:         initMetadata(client, Map.of(topic, 1));\\n1166: \\n1167:         consumer = newConsumer(groupProtocol, time, client, subscription, metadata, assignor,\\n1168:                 true, groupId, Optional.empty(), false);\\n1169:         consumer.assign(List.of(tp0));\\n1170:         consumer.seek(tp0, 20L);\\n1171:         consumer.poll(Duration.ZERO);\\n1172:         assertEquals(20L, subscription.validPosition(tp0).offset);\\n1173:     }\\n1174: \\n1175:     @ParameterizedTest\\n1176:     @EnumSource(GroupProtocol.class)\\n1177:     public void testCommitsFetchedDuringAssign(GroupProtocol groupProtocol) {\\n1178:         long offset1 = 10000;\\n1179:         long offset2 = 20000;\\n1180: \\n1181:         ConsumerMetadata metadata = createMetadata(subscription);\\n1182:         MockClient client = new MockClient(time, metadata);\\n1183: \\n1184:         initMetadata(client, Map.of(topic, 2));\\n1185:         Node node = metadata.fetch().nodes().get(0);\\n1186: \\n1187:         client.prepareResponseFrom(FindCoordinatorResponse.prepareResponse(Errors.NONE, groupId, node), node);\\n1188:         consumer = newConsumer(groupProtocol, time, client, subscription, metadata, assignor, true, groupInstanceId);\\n1189:         consumer.assign(List.of(tp0));\\n1190: \\n1191:         // lookup coordinator\\n1192:         Node coordinator = new Node(Integer.MAX_VALUE - node.id(), node.host(), node.port());\\n1193: \\n1194:         // fetch offset for one topic\\n1195:         client.prepareResponseFrom(offsetResponse(Map.of(tp0, offset1), Errors.NONE), coordinator);\\n1196:         assertEquals(offset1, consumer.committed(Set.of(tp0)).get(tp0).offset());\\n1197: \\n1198:         consumer.assign(Arrays.asList(tp0, tp1));\\n1199: \\n1200:         // fetch offset for two topics\\n1201:         Map<TopicPartition, Long> offsets = new HashMap<>();\\n1202:         offsets.put(tp0, offset1);\\n1203:         client.prepareResponseFrom(offsetResponse(offsets, Errors.NONE), coordinator);\\n1204:         assertEquals(offset1, consumer.committed(Set.of(tp0)).get(tp0).offset());\\n1205: \\n1206:         offsets.remove(tp0);\\n1207:         offsets.put(tp1, offset2);\\n1208:         client.prepareResponseFrom(offsetResponse(offsets, Errors.NONE), coordinator);\\n1209:         assertEquals(offset2, consumer.committed(Set.of(tp1)).get(tp1).offset());\\n1210:     }\\n1211: \\n1212:     @SuppressWarnings(\\\"resource\\\")\\n1213:     @ParameterizedTest\\n1214:     @EnumSource(GroupProtocol.class)\\n1215:     public void testFetchStableOffsetThrowInCommitted(GroupProtocol groupProtocol) {\\n1216:         assertThrows(UnsupportedVersionException.class, () -> setupThrowableConsumer(groupProtocol).committed(Set.of(tp0)));\\n1217:     }\\n1218: \\n1219:     @SuppressWarnings(\\\"resource\\\")\\n1220:     @ParameterizedTest\\n1221: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-benchmarks/kafka--e678b4b@47ac12bd6042edf23f1dbca3d789db945d62802d/-/blob/clients/src/test/java/org/apache/kafka/clients/consumer/KafkaConsumerTest.java%3FL1159-1220\"}"

"{\"content\":\"1853:     @EnumSource(GroupProtocol.class)\\n1854:     public void testOffsetOfPausedPartitions(GroupProtocol groupProtocol) {\\n1855:         ConsumerMetadata metadata = createMetadata(subscription);\\n1856:         MockClient client = new MockClient(time, metadata);\\n1857: \\n1858:         initMetadata(client, Map.of(topic, 2));\\n1859:         Node node = metadata.fetch().nodes().get(0);\\n1860: \\n1861:         ConsumerPartitionAssignor assignor = new RangeAssignor();\\n1862: \\n1863:         client.prepareResponseFrom(FindCoordinatorResponse.prepareResponse(Errors.NONE, groupId, node), node);\\n1864:         consumer = newConsumer(groupProtocol, time, client, subscription, metadata, assignor, true, groupInstanceId);\\n1865: \\n1866:         // lookup coordinator\\n1867:         Node coordinator = new Node(Integer.MAX_VALUE - node.id(), node.host(), node.port());\\n1868: \\n1869:         // manual assignment\\n1870:         Set<TopicPartition> partitions = Set.of(tp0, tp1);\\n1871:         consumer.assign(partitions);\\n1872:         // verify consumer's assignment\\n1873:         assertEquals(partitions, consumer.assignment());\\n1874: \\n1875:         consumer.pause(partitions);\\n1876:         consumer.seekToEnd(partitions);\\n1877: \\n1878:         // fetch and verify committed offset of two partitions\\n1879:         Map<TopicPartition, Long> offsets = new HashMap<>();\\n1880:         offsets.put(tp0, 0L);\\n1881:         offsets.put(tp1, 0L);\\n1882: \\n1883:         client.prepareResponseFrom(offsetResponse(offsets, Errors.NONE), coordinator);\\n1884:         assertEquals(0, consumer.committed(Set.of(tp0)).get(tp0).offset());\\n1885: \\n1886:         offsets.remove(tp0);\\n1887:         offsets.put(tp1, 0L);\\n1888:         client.prepareResponseFrom(offsetResponse(offsets, Errors.NONE), coordinator);\\n1889:         assertEquals(0, consumer.committed(Set.of(tp1)).get(tp1).offset());\\n1890: \\n1891:         // fetch and verify consumer's position in the two partitions\\n1892:         final Map<TopicPartition, Long> offsetResponse = new HashMap<>();\\n1893:         offsetResponse.put(tp0, 3L);\\n1894:         offsetResponse.put(tp1, 3L);\\n1895:         client.prepareResponse(listOffsetsResponse(offsetResponse));\\n1896:         assertEquals(3L, consumer.position(tp0));\\n1897:         assertEquals(3L, consumer.position(tp1));\\n1898: \\n1899:         client.requests().clear();\\n1900:         consumer.unsubscribe();\\n1901:     }\\n1902: \\n1903:     @ParameterizedTest\\n1904:     @EnumSource(GroupProtocol.class)\\n1905:     public void testPollWithNoSubscription(GroupProtocol groupProtocol) {\\n1906:   "content": "{\"content\":\"1: /*\\n2:  * Licensed to the Apache Software Foundation (ASF) under one or more\\n3:  * contributor license agreements. See the NOTICE file distributed with\\n4:  * this work for additional information regarding copyright ownership.\\n5:  * The ASF licenses this file to You under the Apache License, Version 2.0\\n6:  * (the \\\"License\\\"); you may not use this file except in compliance with\\n7:  * the License. You may obtain a copy of the License at\\n8:  *\\n9:  *    http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\\n10:  *\\n11:  * Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\\n12:  * distributed under the License is distributed on an \\\"AS IS\\\" BASIS,\\n13:  * WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\\n14:  * See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\\n15:  * limitations under the License.\\n16:  */\\n17: package org.apache.kafka.clients.consumer;\\n18: \\n19: import org.apache.kafka.common.TopicPartition;\\n20: import org.apache.kafka.common.utils.AbstractIterator;\\n21: \\n22: import java.util.ArrayList;\\n23: import java.util.Collections;\\n24: import java.util.Iterator;\\n25: import java.util.List;\\n26: import java.util.Map;\\n27: import java.util.Set;\\n28: \\n29: /**\\n30:  * A container that holds the list {@link ConsumerRecord} per partition for a\\n31:  * particular topic. There is one {@link ConsumerRecord} list for every topic\\n32:  * partition returned by a {@link Consumer#poll(java.time.Duration)} operation.\\n33:  */\\n34: public class ConsumerRecords<K, V> implements Iterable<ConsumerRecord<K, V>> {\\n35:     public static final ConsumerRecords<Object, Object> EMPTY = new ConsumerRecords<>(Map.of(), Map.of());\\n36: \\n37:     private final Map<TopicPartition, List<ConsumerRecord<K, V>>> records;\\n38:     private final Map<TopicPartition, OffsetAndMetadata> nextOffsets;\\n39: \\n40:     /**\\n41:      * @deprecated Since 4.0. Use {@link #ConsumerRecords(Map, Map)} instead.\\n42:      */\\n43:     @Deprecated\\n44:     public ConsumerRecords(Map<TopicPartition, List<ConsumerRecord<K, V>>> records) {\\n45:         this(records, Map.of());\\n46:     }\\n47: \\n48:     public ConsumerRecords(Map<TopicPartition, List<ConsumerRecord<K, V>>> records, final Map<TopicPartition, OffsetAndMetadata> nextOffsets) {\\n49:         this.records = records;\\n50:         this.nextOffsets = Map.copyOf(nextOffsets);\\n51:     }\\n52: \\n53:     /**\\n54:      * Get just the records for the given partition\\n55:      * \\n56:      * @param partition The partition to get records for\\n57:      */\\n58:     public List<ConsumerRecord<K, V>> records(TopicPartition partition) {\\n59:         List<ConsumerRecord<K, V>> recs = this.records.get(partition);\\n60:         if (recs == null)\\n61:             return Collections.emptyList();\\n62:         else\\n63:             return Collections.unmodifiableList(recs);\\n64:     }\\n65: \\n66:     /**\\n67:      * Get the next offsets and metadata corresponding to all topic partitions for which the position have been advanced in this poll call\\n68:      * @return the next offsets that the consumer will consume\\n69:      */\\n70:     public Map<TopicPartition, OffsetAndMetadata> nextOffsets() {\\n71:         return nextOffsets;\\n72:     }\\n73: \\n74:     /**\\n75:      * Get just the records for the given topic\\n76:      */\\n77:     public Iterable<ConsumerRecord<K, V>> records(String topic) {\\n78:         if (topic == null)\\n79:             throw new IllegalArgumentException(\\\"Topic must be non-null.\\\");\\n80:         List<List<ConsumerRecord<K, V>>> recs = new ArrayList<>();\\n81:         for (Map.Entry<TopicPartition, List<ConsumerRecord<K, V>>> entry : records.entrySet()) {\\n82:             if (entry.getKey().topic().equals(topic))\\n83:                 recs.add(entry.getValue());\\n84:         }\\n85:         return new ConcatenatedIterable<>(recs);\\n86:     }\\n87: \\n88:     /**\\n89:      * Get the partitions which have records contained in this record set.\\n90:      * @return the set of partitions with data in this record set (may be empty if no data was returned)\\n91:      */\\n92:     public Set<TopicPartition> partitions() {\\n93:         return Collections.unmodifiableSet(records.keySet());\\n94:     }\\n95: \\n96:     @Override\\n97:     public Iterator<ConsumerRecord<K, V>> iterator() {\\n98:         return new ConcatenatedIterable<>(records.values()).iterator();\\n99:     }\\n100: \\n101:     /**\\n102:      * The number of records for all topics\\n103:      */\\n104:     public int count() {\\n105:         int count = 0;\\n106:         for (List<ConsumerRecord<K, V>> recs: this.records.values())\\n107:             count += recs.size();\\n108:         return count;\\n109:     }\\n110: \\n111:     private static class ConcatenatedIterable<K, V> implements Iterable<ConsumerRecord<K, V>> {\\n112: \\n113:         private final Iterable<? extends Iterable<ConsumerRecord<K, V>>> iterables;\\n114: \\n115:         public ConcatenatedIterable(Iterable<? extends Iterable<ConsumerRecord<K, V>>> iterables) {\\n116:             this.iterables = iterables;\\n117:         }\\n118: \\n119:         @Override\\n120:         public Iterator<ConsumerRecord<K, V>> iterator() {\\n121:             return new AbstractIterator<>() {\\n122:                 final Iterator<? extends Iterable<ConsumerRecord<K, V>>> iters = iterables.iterator();\\n123:                 Iterator<ConsumerRecord<K, V>> current;\\n124: \\n125:                 protected ConsumerRecord<K, V> makeNext() {\\n126:                     while (current == null || !current.hasNext()) {\\n127:                         if (iters.hasNext())\\n128:                             current = iters.next().iterator();\\n129:                         else\\n130:                             return allDone();\\n131:                     }\\n132:                     return current.next();\\n133:                 }\\n134:             };\\n135:         }\\n136:     }\\n137: \\n138:     public boolean isEmpty() {\\n139:         return records.isEmpty();\\n140:     }\\n141: \\n142:     @SuppressWarnings(\\\"unchecked\\\")\\n143:     public static <K, V> ConsumerRecords<K, V> empty() {\\n144:         return (ConsumerRecords<K, V>) EMPTY;\\n145:     }\\n146: \\n147: }\\n148: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-benchmarks/kafka--e678b4b@47ac12bd6042edf23f1dbca3d789db945d62802d/-/blob/clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/clients/consumer/ConsumerRecords.java%3FL1-150\"}",

See the NOTICE file distributed with\n4:  * this work for additional information regarding copyright ownership.\n5:  * The ASF licenses this file to You under the Apache License, Version 2.0\n6:  * (the \"License\"); you may not use this file except in compliance with\n7:  * the License. You may obtain a copy of the License at\n8:  *\n9:  *    http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\n10:  *\n11:  * Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\n12:  * distributed under the License is distributed on an \"AS IS\" BASIS,\n13:  * WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\n14:  * See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\n15:  * limitations under the License.\n16:  */\n17: package org.apache.kafka.clients.consumer;\n18: \n19: import org.apache.kafka.common.TopicPartition;\n20: import org.apache.kafka.common.utils.AbstractIterator;\n21: \n22: import java.util.ArrayList;\n23: import java.util.Collections;\n24: import java.util.Iterator;\n25: import java.util.List;\n26: import java.util.Map;\n27: import java.util.Set;\n28: \n29: /**\n30:  * A container that holds the list {@link ConsumerRecord} per partition for a\n31:  * particular topic. There is one {@link ConsumerRecord} list for every topic\n32:  * partition returned by a {@link Consumer#poll(java.time.Duration)} operation.\n33:  */\n34: public class ConsumerRecords<K, V> implements Iterable<ConsumerRecord<K, V>> {\n35:     public static final ConsumerRecords<Object, Object> EMPTY = new ConsumerRecords<>(Map.of(), Map.of());\n36: \n37:     private final Map<TopicPartition, List<ConsumerRecord<K, V>>> records;\n38:     private final Map<TopicPartition, OffsetAndMetadata> nextOffsets;\n39: \n40:     /**\n41:      * @deprecated Since 4.0. Use {@link #ConsumerRecords(Map, Map)} instead.\n42:      */\n43:     @Deprecated\n44:     public ConsumerRecords(Map<TopicPartition, List<ConsumerRecord<K, V>>> records) {\n45:         this(records, Map.of());\n46:     }\n47: \n48:     public ConsumerRecords(Map<TopicPartition, List<ConsumerRecord<K, V>>> records, final Map<TopicPartition, OffsetAndMetadata> nextOffsets) {\n49:         this.records = records;\n50:         this.nextOffsets = Map.copyOf(nextOffsets);\n51:     }\n52: \n53:     /**\n54:      * Get just the records for the given partition\n55:      * \n56:      * @param partition The partition to get records for\n57:      */\n58:     public List<ConsumerRecord<K, V>> records(TopicPartition partition) {\n59:         List<ConsumerRecord<K, V>> recs = this.records.get(partition);\n60:         if (recs == null)\n61:             return Collections.emptyList();\n62:         else\n63:             return Collections.unmodifiableList(recs);\n64:     }\n65: \n66:     /**\n67:      * Get the next offsets and metadata corresponding to all topic partitions for which the position have been advanced in this poll call\n68:      * @return the next offsets that the consumer will consume\n69:      */\n70:     public Map<TopicPartition, OffsetAndMetadata> nextOffsets() {\n71:         return nextOffsets;\n72:     }\n73: \n74:     /**\n75:      * Get just the records for the given topic\n76:      */\n77:     public Iterable<ConsumerRecord<K, V>> records(String topic) {\n78:         if (topic == null)\n79:             throw new IllegalArgumentException(\"Topic must be non-null.\");\n80:         List<List<ConsumerRecord<K, V>>> recs = new ArrayList<>();\n81:         for (Map.Entry<TopicPartition, List<ConsumerRecord<K, V>>> entry : records.entrySet()) {\n82:             if (entry.getKey().topic().equals(topic))\n83:                 recs.add(entry.getValue());\n84:         }\n85:         return new ConcatenatedIterable<>(recs);\n86:     }\n87: \n88:     /**\n89:      * Get the partitions which have records contained in this record set.\n90:      * @return the set of partitions with data in this record set (may be empty if no data was returned)\n91:      */\n92:     public Set<TopicPartition> partitions() {\n93:         return Collections.unmodifiableSet(records.keySet());\n94:     }\n95: \n96:     @Override\n97:     public Iterator<ConsumerRecord<K, V>> iterator() {\n98:         return new ConcatenatedIterable<>(records.values()).iterator();\n99:     }\n100: \n101:     /**\n102:      * The number of records for all topics\n103:      */\n104:     public int count() {\n105:         int count = 0;\n106:         for (List<ConsumerRecord<K, V>> recs: this.records.values())\n107:             count += recs.size();\n108:         return count;\n109:     }\n110: \n111:     private static class ConcatenatedIterable<K, V> implements Iterable<ConsumerRecord<K, V>> {\n112: \n113:         private final Iterable<? extends Iterable<ConsumerRecord<K, V>>> iterables;\n114: \n115:         public ConcatenatedIterable(Iterable<? extends Iterable<ConsumerRecord<K, V>>> iterables) {\n116:             this.iterables = iterables;\n117:         }\n118: \n119:         @Override\n120:         public Iterator<ConsumerRecord<K, V>> iterator() {\n121:             return new AbstractIterator<>() {\n122:                 final Iterator<? extends Iterable<ConsumerRecord<K, V>>> iters = iterables.iterator();\n123:                 Iterator<ConsumerRecord<K, V>> current;\n124: \n125:                 protected ConsumerRecord<K, V> makeNext() {\n126:                     while (current == null || !current.hasNext()) {\n127:                         if (iters.hasNext())\n128:                             current = iters.next().iterator();\n129:                         else\n130:                             return allDone();\n131:                     }\n132:                     return current.next();\n133:                 }\n134:             };\n135:         }\n136:     }\n137: \n138:     public boolean isEmpty() {\n139:         return records.isEmpty();\n140:     }\n141: \n142:     @SuppressWarnings(\"unchecked\")\n143:     public static <K, V> ConsumerRecords<K, V> empty() {\n144:         return (ConsumerRecords<K, V>) EMPTY;\n145:     }\n146: \n147: }\n148: ",
    "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-benchmarks/kafka--e678b4b@47ac12bd6042edf23f1dbca3d789db945d62802d/-/blob/clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/clients/consumer/ConsumerRecords.java%3FL1-150"
  }
}

{
  "content": "{\"content\":\"1: /*\\n2:  * Licensed to the Apache Software Foundation (ASF) under one or more\\n3:  * contributor license agreements. See the NOTICE file distributed with\\n4:  * this work for additional information regarding copyright ownership.\\n5:  * The ASF licenses this file to You under the Apache License, Version 2.0\\n6:  * (the \\\"License\\\"); you may not use this file except in compliance with\\n7:  * the License. You may obtain a copy of the License at\\n8:  *\\n9:  *    http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\\n10:  *\\n11:  * Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\\n12:  * distributed under the License is distributed on an \\\"AS IS\\\" BASIS,\\n13:  * WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\\n14:  * See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\\n15:  * limitations under the License.\\n16:  */\\n17: package org.apache.kafka.clients.consumer;\\n18: \\n19: import org.apache.kafka.common.requests.OffsetFetchResponse;\\n20: \\n21: import java.io.Serializable;\\n22: import java.util.Objects;\\n23: import java.util.Optional;\\n24: \\n25: /**\\n26:  * The Kafka offset commit API allows users to provide additional metadata (in the form of a string)\\n27:  * when an offset is committed. This can be useful (for example) to store information about which\\n28:  * node made the commit, what time the commit was made, etc.\\n29:  */\\n30: public class OffsetAndMetadata implements Serializable {\\n31:     private static final long serialVersionUID = 2019555404968089681L;\\n32: \\n33:     private final long offset;\\n34:     private final String metadata;\\n35: \\n36:     // We use null to represent the absence of a leader epoch to simplify serialization.\\n37:     // I.e., older serializations of this class which do not have this field will automatically\\n38:     // initialize its value to null.\\n39:     private final Integer leaderEpoch;\\n40: \\n41:     /**\\n42:      * Construct a new OffsetAndMetadata object for committing through {@link KafkaConsumer}.\\n43:      *\\n44:      * @param offset The offset to be committed\\n45:      * @param leaderEpoch Optional leader epoch of the last consumed record\\n46:      * @param metadata Non-null metadata\\n47:      */\\n48:     public OffsetAndMetadata(long offset, Optional<Integer> leaderEpoch, String metadata) {\\n49:         if (offset < 0)\\n50:             throw new IllegalArgumentException(\\\"Invalid negative offset\\\");\\n51: \\n52:         this.offset = offset;\\n53:         this.leaderEpoch = leaderEpoch.orElse(null);\\n54: \\n55:         // The server converts null metadata to an empty string. So we store it as an empty string as well on the client\\n56:         // to be consistent.\\n57:         this.metadata = Objects.requireNonNullElse(metadata, OffsetFetchResponse.NO_METADATA);\\n58:     }\\n59: \\n60:     /**\\n61:      * Construct a new OffsetAndMetadata object for committing through {@link KafkaConsumer}.\\n62:      * @param offset The offset to be committed\\n63:      * @param metadata Non-null metadata\\n64:      */\\n65:     public OffsetAndMetadata(long offset, String metadata) {\\n66:         this(offset, Optional.empty(), metadata);\\n67:     }\\n68: \\n69:     /**\\n70:      * Construct a new OffsetAndMetadata object for committing through {@link KafkaConsumer}. The metadata\\n71:      * associated with the commit will be empty.\\n72:      * @param offset The offset to be committed\\n73:      */\\n74:     public OffsetAndMetadata(long offset) {\\n75:         this(offset, \\\"\\\");\\n76:     }\\n77: \\n78:     public long offset() {\\n79:         return offset;\\n80:     }\\n81: \\n82:     /**\\n83:      * Get the metadata of the previously consumed record.\\n84:      *\\n85:      * @return the metadata or empty string if no metadata\\n86:      */\\n87:     public String metadata() {\\n88:         return metadata;\\n89:     }\\n90: \\n91:     /**\\n92:      * Get the leader epoch of the previously consumed record (if one is known). Log truncation is detected\\n93:      * if there exists a leader epoch which is larger than this epoch and begins at an offset earlier than\\n94:      * the committed offset.\\n95:      *\\n96:      * @return the leader epoch or empty if not known\\n97:      */\\n98:     public Optional<Integer> leaderEpoch() {\\n99:         if (leaderEpoch == null || leaderEpoch < 0)\\n100:             return Optional.empty();\\n101:         return Optional.of(leaderEpoch);\\n102:     }\\n103: \\n104:     @Override\\n105:     public boolean equals(Object o) {\\n106:         if (this == o) return true;\\n107:         if (o == null || getClass() != o.getClass()) return false;\\n108:         OffsetAndMetadata that = (OffsetAndMetadata) o;\\n109:         return offset == that.offset &&\\n110:                 Objects.equals(metadata, that.metadata) &&\\n111:                 Objects.equals(leaderEpoch(), that.leaderEpoch());\\n112:     }\\n113: \\n114:     @Override\\n115:     public int hashCode() {\\n116:         return Objects.hash(offset, metadata, leaderEpoch());\\n117:     }\\n118: \\n119:     @Override\\n120:     public String toString() {\\n121:         return \\\"OffsetAndMetadata{\\\" +\\n122:                 \\\"offset=\\\" + offset +\\n123:                 \\\", leaderEpoch=\\\" + leaderEpoch().orElse(null) +\\n124:                 \\\", metadata='\\\" + metadata + '\\\\'' +\\n125:                 '}';\\n126:     }\\n127: }\\n128: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-benchmarks/kafka--e678b4b@47ac12bd6042edf23f1dbca3d789db945d62802d/-/blob/clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/clients/consumer/OffsetAndMetadata.java\"}",

    "content": "1: /*\n2:  * Licensed to the Apache Software Foundation (ASF) under one or more\n3:  * contributor license agreements. This can be useful (for example) to store information about which\n28:  * node made the commit, what time the commit was made, etc.\n29:  */\n30: public class OffsetAndMetadata implements Serializable {\n31:     private static final long serialVersionUID = 2019555404968089681L;\n32: \n33:     private final long offset;\n34:     private final String metadata;\n35: \n36:     // We use null to represent the absence of a leader epoch to simplify serialization.\n37:     // I.e., older serializations of this class which do not have this field will automatically\n38:     // initialize its value to null.\n39:     private final Integer leaderEpoch;\n40: \n41:     /**\n42:      * Construct a new OffsetAndMetadata object for committing through {@link KafkaConsumer}.\n43:      *\n44:      * @param offset The offset to be committed\n45:      * @param leaderEpoch Optional leader epoch of the last consumed record\n46:      * @param metadata Non-null metadata\n47:      */\n48:     public OffsetAndMetadata(long offset, Optional<Integer> leaderEpoch, String metadata) {\n49:         if (offset < 0)\n50:             throw new IllegalArgumentException(\"Invalid negative offset\");\n51: \n52:         this.offset = offset;\n53:         this.leaderEpoch = leaderEpoch.orElse(null);\n54: \n55:         // The server converts null metadata to an empty string. So we store it as an empty string as well on the client\n56:         // to be consistent.\n57:         this.metadata = Objects.requireNonNullElse(metadata, OffsetFetchResponse.NO_METADATA);\n58:     }\n59: \n60:     /**\n61:      * Construct a new OffsetAndMetadata object for committing through {@link KafkaConsumer}.\n62:      * @param offset The offset to be committed\n63:      * @param metadata Non-null metadata\n64:      */\n65:     public OffsetAndMetadata(long offset, String metadata) {\n66:         this(offset, Optional.empty(), metadata);\n67:     }\n68: \n69:     /**\n70:      * Construct a new OffsetAndMetadata object for committing through {@link KafkaConsumer}. The metadata\n71:      * associated with the commit will be empty.\n72:      * @param offset The offset to be committed\n73:      */\n74:     public OffsetAndMetadata(long offset) {\n75:         this(offset, \"\");\n76:     }\n77: \n78:     public long offset() {\n79:         return offset;\n80:     }\n81: \n82:     /**\n83:      * Get the metadata of the previously consumed record.\n84:      *\n85:      * @return the metadata or empty string if no metadata\n86:      */\n87:     public String metadata() {\n88:         return metadata;\n89:     }\n90: \n91:     /**\n92:      * Get the leader epoch of the previously consumed record (if one is known). Log truncation is detected\n93:      * if there exists a leader epoch which is larger than this epoch and begins at an offset earlier than\n94:      * the committed offset.\n95:      *\n96:      * @return the leader epoch or empty if not known\n97:      */\n98:     public Optional<Integer> leaderEpoch() {\n99:         if (leaderEpoch == null || leaderEpoch < 0)\n100:             return Optional.empty();\n101:         return Optional.of(leaderEpoch);\n102:     }\n103: \n104:     @Override\n105:     public boolean equals(Object o) {\n106:         if (this == o) return true;\n107:         if (o == null || getClass() != o.getClass()) return false;\n108:         OffsetAndMetadata that = (OffsetAndMetadata) o;\n109:         return offset == that.offset &&\n110:                 Objects.equals(metadata, that.metadata) &&\n111:                 Objects.equals(leaderEpoch(), that.leaderEpoch());\n112:     }\n113: \n114:     @Override\n115:     public int hashCode() {\n116:         return Objects.hash(offset, metadata, leaderEpoch());\n117:     }\n118: \n119:     @Override\n120:     public String toString() {\n121:         return \"OffsetAndMetadata{\" +\n122:                 \"offset=\" + offset +\n123:                 \", leaderEpoch=\" + leaderEpoch().orElse(null) +\n124:                 \", metadata='\" + metadata + '\\'' +\n125:                 '}';\n126:     }\n127: }\n128: ",

  }

98. mcp__sourcegraph__sg_read_file @ -
Input
{
  "path": "examples/src/main/java/kafka/examples/Consumer.java",

Output
{
  "content": "{\"content\":\"1: /*\\n2:  * Licensed to the Apache Software Foundation (ASF) under one or more\\n3:  * contributor license agreements. See the NOTICE file distributed with\\n4:  * this work for additional information regarding copyright ownership.\\n5:  * The ASF licenses this file to You under the Apache License, Version 2.0\\n6:  * (the \\\"License\\\"); you may not use this file except in compliance with\\n7:  * the License. You may obtain a copy of the License at\\n8:  *\\n9:  *    http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\\n10:  *\\n11:  * Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\\n12:  * distributed under the License is distributed on an \\\"AS IS\\\" BASIS,\\n13:  * WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\\n14:  * See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\\n15:  * limitations under the License.\\n16:  */\\n17: package kafka.examples;\\n18: \\n19: import org.apache.kafka.clients.consumer.ConsumerConfig;\\n20: import org.apache.kafka.clients.consumer.ConsumerRebalanceListener;\\n21: import org.apache.kafka.clients.consumer.ConsumerRecord;\\n22: import org.apache.kafka.clients.consumer.ConsumerRecords;\\n23: import org.apache.kafka.clients.consumer.KafkaConsumer;\\n24: import org.apache.kafka.clients.consumer.NoOffsetForPartitionException;\\n25: import org.apache.kafka.clients.consumer.OffsetOutOfRangeException;\\n26: import org.apache.kafka.common.KafkaException;\\n27: import org.apache.kafka.common.TopicPartition;\\n28: import org.apache.kafka.common.errors.AuthorizationException;\\n29: import org.apache.kafka.common.errors.RecordDeserializationException;\\n30: import org.apache.kafka.common.errors.UnsupportedVersionException;\\n31: import org.apache.kafka.common.serialization.IntegerDeserializer;\\n32: import org.apache.kafka.common.serialization.StringDeserializer;\\n33: \\n34: import java.time.Duration;\\n35: import java.util.Collection;\\n36: import java.util.Optional;\\n37: import java.util.Properties;\\n38: import java.util.UUID;\\n39: import java.util.concurrent.CountDownLatch;\\n40: \\n41: import static java.util.Collections.singleton;\\n42: \\n43: /**\\n44:  * A simple consumer thread that subscribes to a topic, fetches new records and prints them.\\n45:  * The thread does not stop until all records are completed or an exception is raised.\\n46:  */\\n47: public class Consumer extends Thread implements ConsumerRebalanceListener {\\n48:     private final String bootstrapServers;\\n49:     private final String topic;\\n50:     private final String groupId;\\n51:     private final Optional<String> instanceId;\\n52:     private final boolean readCommitted;\\n53:     private final int numRecords;\\n54:     private final CountDownLatch latch;\\n55:     private volatile boolean closed;\\n56:     private int remainingRecords;\\n57: \\n58:     public Consumer(String threadName,\\n59:                     String bootstrapServers,\\n60:                     String topic,\\n61:                     String groupId,\\n62:                     Optional<String> instanceId,\\n63:                     boolean readCommitted,\\n64:                     int numRecords,\\n65:                     CountDownLatch latch) {\\n66:         super(threadName);\\n67:         this.bootstrapServers = bootstrapServers;\\n68:         this.topic = topic;\\n69:         this.groupId = groupId;\\n70:         this.instanceId = instanceId;\\n71:         this.readCommitted = readCommitted;\\n72:         this.numRecords = numRecords;\\n73:         this.remainingRecords = numRecords;\\n74:         this.latch = latch;\\n75:     }\\n76: \\n77:     @Override\\n78:     public void run() {\\n79:         // the consumer instance is NOT thread safe\\n80:         try (KafkaConsumer<Integer, String> consumer = createKafkaConsumer()) {\\n81:             // subscribes to a list of topics to get dynamically assigned partitions\\n82:             // this class implements the rebalance listener that we pass here to be notified of such events\\n83:             consumer.subscribe(singleton(topic), this);\\n84:             Utils.printOut(\\\"Subscribed to %s\\\", topic);\\n85:             while (!closed && remainingRecords > 0) {\\n86:                 try {\\n87:                     // if required, poll updates partition assignment and invokes the configured rebalance listener\\n88:                     // then tries to fetch records sequentially using the last committed offset or auto.offset.reset policy\\n89:                     // returns immediately if there are records or times out returning an empty record set\\n90:                     // the next poll must be called within session.timeout.ms to avoid group rebalance\\n91:                     ConsumerRecords<Integer, String> records = consumer.poll(Duration.ofSeconds(1));\\n92:                     for (ConsumerRecord<Integer, String> record : records) {\\n93:                         Utils.maybePrintRecord(numRecords, record);\\n94:                     }\\n95:                     remainingRecords -= records.count();\\n96:                 } catch (AuthorizationException | UnsupportedVersionException\\n97:                          | RecordDeserializationException e) {\\n98:                     // we can't recover from these exceptions\\n99:                     Utils.printErr(e.getMessage());\\n100:                     shutdown();\\n101:                 } catch (OffsetOutOfRangeException | NoOffsetForPartitionException e) {\\n102:                     // invalid or no offset found without auto.reset.policy\\n103:                     Utils.printOut(\\\"Invalid or no offset found, using latest\\\");\\n104:                     consumer.seekToEnd(e.partitions());\\n105:                     consumer.commitSync();\\n106:                 } catch (KafkaException e) {\\n107:                     // log the exception and try to continue\\n108:                     Utils.printErr(e.getMessage());\\n109:                 }\\n110:             }\\n111:         } catch (Throwable e) {\\n112:             Utils.printErr(\\\"Unhandled exception\\\");\\n113:             e.printStackTrace();\\n114:         }\\n115:         Utils.printOut(\\\"Fetched %d records\\\", numRecords - remainingRecords);\\n116:         shutdown();\\n117:     }\\n118: \\n119:     public void shutdown() {\\n120:         if (!closed) {\\n121:             closed = true;\\n122:             latch.countDown();\\n123:         }\\n124:     }\\n125: \\n126:     public KafkaConsumer<Integer, String> createKafkaConsumer() {\\n127:         Properties props = new Properties();\\n128:         // bootstrap server config is required for consumer to connect to brokers\\n129:         props.put(ConsumerConfig.BOOTSTRAP_SERVERS_CONFIG, bootstrapServers);\\n130:         // client id is not required, but it's good to track the source of requests beyond just ip/port\\n131:         // by allowing a logical application name to be included in server-side request logging\\n132:         props.put(ConsumerConfig.CLIENT_ID_CONFIG, \\\"client-\\\" + UUID.randomUUID());\\n133:         // consumer group id is required when we use subscribe(topics) for group management\\n134:         props.put(ConsumerConfig.GROUP_ID_CONFIG, groupId);\\n135:         // sets static membership to improve availability (e.g. rolling restart)\\n136:         instanceId.ifPresent(id -> props.put(ConsumerConfig.GROUP_INSTANCE_ID_CONFIG, id));\\n137:         // disables auto commit when EOS is enabled, because offsets are committed with the transaction\\n138:         props.put(ConsumerConfig.ENABLE_AUTO_COMMIT_CONFIG, readCommitted ? \\\"false\\\" : \\\"true\\\");\\n139:         // key and value are just byte arrays, so we need to set appropriate deserializers\\n140:         props.put(ConsumerConfig.KEY_DESERIALIZER_CLASS_CONFIG, IntegerDeserializer.class);\\n141:         props.put(ConsumerConfig.VALUE_DESERIALIZER_CLASS_CONFIG, StringDeserializer.class);\\n142:         if (readCommitted) {\\n143:             // skips ongoing and aborted transactions\\n144:             props.put(ConsumerConfig.ISOLATION_LEVEL_CONFIG, \\\"read_committed\\\");\\n145:         }\\n146:         // sets the reset offset policy in case of invalid or no offset\\n147:         props.put(ConsumerConfig.AUTO_OFFSET_RESET_CONFIG, \\\"earliest\\\");\\n148:         return new KafkaConsumer<>(props);\\n149:     }\\n150: \\n151:     @Override\\n152:     public void onPartitionsRevoked(Collection<TopicPartition> partitions) {\\n153:         Utils.printOut(\\\"Revoked partitions: %s\\\", partitions);\\n154:         // this can be used to commit pending offsets when using manual commit and EOS is disabled\\n155:     }\\n156: \\n157:     @Override\\n158:     public void onPartitionsAssigned(Collection<TopicPartition> partitions) {\\n159:         Utils.printOut(\\\"Assigned partitions: %s\\\", partitions);\\n160:         // this can be used to read the offsets from an external store or some other initialization\\n161:     }\\n162: \\n163:     @Override\\n164:     public void onPartitionsLost(Collection<TopicPartition> partitions) {\\n165:         Utils.printOut(\\\"Lost partitions: %s\\\", partitions);\\n166:         // this is called when partitions are reassigned before we had a chance to revoke them gracefully\\n167:         // we can't commit pending offsets because these partitions are probably owned by other consumers already\\n168:         // nevertheless, we may need to do some other cleanup\\n169:     }\\n170: }\\n171: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-benchmarks/kafka--e678b4b@47ac12bd6042edf23f1dbca3d789db945d62802d/-/blob/examples/src/main/java/kafka/examples/Consumer.java\"}",
  "structuredContent": {
    "content": "1: /*\n2:  * Licensed to the Apache Software Foundation (ASF) under one or more\n3:  * contributor license agreements. See the NOTICE file distributed with\n4:  * this work for additional information regarding copyright ownership.\n5:  * The ASF licenses this file to You under the Apache License, Version 2.0\n6:  * (the \"License\"); you may not use this file except in compliance with\n7:  * the License. You may obtain a copy of the License at\n8:  *\n9:  *    http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\n10:  *\n11:  * Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\n12:  * distributed under the License is distributed on an \"AS IS\" BASIS,\n13:  * WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\n14:  * See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\n15:  * limitations under the License.\n16:  */\n17: package kafka.examples;\n18: \n19: import org.apache.kafka.clients.consumer.ConsumerConfig;\n20: import org.apache.kafka.clients.consumer.ConsumerRebalanceListener;\n21: import org.apache.kafka.clients.consumer.ConsumerRecord;\n22: import org.apache.kafka.clients.consumer.ConsumerRecords;\n23: import org.apache.kafka.clients.consumer.KafkaConsumer;\n24: import org.apache.kafka.clients.consumer.NoOffsetForPartitionException;\n25: import org.apache.kafka.clients.consumer.OffsetOutOfRangeException;\n26: import org.apache.kafka.common.KafkaException;\n27: import org.apache.kafka.common.TopicPartition;\n28: import org.apache.kafka.common.errors.AuthorizationException;\n29: import org.apache.kafka.common.errors.RecordDeserializationException;\n30: import org.apache.kafka.common.errors.UnsupportedVersionException;\n31: import org.apache.kafka.common.serialization.IntegerDeserializer;\n32: import org.apache.kafka.common.serialization.StringDeserializer;\n33: \n34: import java.time.Duration;\n35: import java.util.Collection;\n36: import java.util.Optional;\n37: import java.util.Properties;\n38: import java.util.UUID;\n39: import java.util.concurrent.CountDownLatch;\n40: \n41: import static java.util.Collections.singleton;\n42: \n43: /**\n44:  * A simple consumer thread that subscribes to a topic, fetches new records and prints them.\n45:  * The thread does not stop until all records are completed or an exception is raised.\n46:  */\n47: public class Consumer extends Thread implements ConsumerRebalanceListener {\n48:     private final String bootstrapServers;\n49:     private final String topic;\n50:     private final String groupId;\n51:     private final Optional<String> instanceId;\n52:     private final boolean readCommitted;\n53:     private final int numRecords;\n54:     private final CountDownLatch latch;\n55:     private volatile boolean closed;\n56:     private int remainingRecords;\n57: \n58:     public Consumer(String threadName,\n59:                     String bootstrapServers,\n60:                     String topic,\n61:                     String groupId,\n62:                     Optional<String> instanceId,\n63:                     boolean readCommitted,\n64:                     int numRecords,\n65:                     CountDownLatch latch) {\n66:         super(threadName);\n67:         this.bootstrapServers = bootstrapServers;\n68:         this.topic = topic;\n69:         this.groupId = groupId;\n70:         this.instanceId = instanceId;\n71:         this.readCommitted = readCommitted;\n72:         this.numRecords = numRecords;\n73:         this.remainingRecords = numRecords;\n74:         this.latch = latch;\n75:     }\n76: \n77:     @Override\n78:     public void run() {\n79:         // the consumer instance is NOT thread safe\n80:         try (KafkaConsumer<Integer, String> consumer = createKafkaConsumer()) {\n81:             // subscribes to a list of topics to get dynamically assigned partitions\n82:             // this class implements the rebalance listener that we pass here to be notified of such events\n83:             consumer.subscribe(singleton(topic), this);\n84:             Utils.printOut(\"Subscribed to %s\", topic);\n85:             while (!closed && remainingRecords > 0) {\n86:                 try {\n87:                     // if required, poll updates partition assignment and invokes the configured rebalance listener\n88:                     // then tries to fetch records sequentially using the last committed offset or auto.offset.reset policy\n89:                     // returns immediately if there are records or times out returning an empty record set\n90:                     // the next poll must be called within session.timeout.ms to avoid group rebalance\n91:                     ConsumerRecords<Integer, String> records = consumer.poll(Duration.ofSeconds(1));\n92:                     for (ConsumerRecord<Integer, String> record : records) {\n93:                         Utils.maybePrintRecord(numRecords, record);\n94:                     }\n95:                     remainingRecords -= records.count();\n96:                 } catch (AuthorizationException | UnsupportedVersionException\n97:                          | RecordDeserializationException e) {\n98:                     // we can't recover from these exceptions\n99:                     Utils.printErr(e.getMessage());\n100:                     shutdown();\n101:                 } catch (OffsetOutOfRangeException | NoOffsetForPartitionException e) {\n102:                     // invalid or no offset found without auto.reset.policy\n103:                     Utils.printOut(\"Invalid or no offset found, using latest\");\n104:                     consumer.seekToEnd(e.partitions());\n105:                     consumer.commitSync();\n106:                 } catch (KafkaException e) {\n107:                     // log the exception and try to continue\n108:                     Utils.printErr(e.getMessage());\n109:                 }\n110:             }\n111:         } catch (Throwable e) {\n112:             Utils.printErr(\"Unhandled exception\");\n113:             e.printStackTrace();\n114:         }\n115:         Utils.printOut(\"Fetched %d records\", numRecords - remainingRecords);\n116:         shutdown();\n117:     }\n118: \n119:     public void shutdown() {\n120:         if (!closed) {\n121:             closed = true;\n122:             latch.countDown();\n123:         }\n124:     }\n125: \n126:     public KafkaConsumer<Integer, String> createKafkaConsumer() {\n127:         Properties props = new Properties();\n128:         // bootstrap server config is required for consumer to connect to brokers\n129:         props.put(ConsumerConfig.BOOTSTRAP_SERVERS_CONFIG, bootstrapServers);\n130:         // client id is not required, but it's good to track the source of requests beyond just ip/port\n131:         // by allowing a logical application name to be included in server-side request logging\n132:         props.put(ConsumerConfig.CLIENT_ID_CONFIG, \"client-\" + UUID.randomUUID());\n133:         // consumer group id is required when we use subscribe(topics) for group management\n134:         props.put(ConsumerConfig.GROUP_ID_CONFIG, groupId);\n135:         // sets static membership to improve availability (e.g. rolling restart)\n136:         instanceId.ifPresent(id -> props.put(ConsumerConfig.GROUP_INSTANCE_ID_CONFIG, id));\n137:         // disables auto commit when EOS is enabled, because offsets are committed with the transaction\n138:         props.put(ConsumerConfig.ENABLE_AUTO_COMMIT_CONFIG, readCommitted ? \"false\" : \"true\");\n139:         // key and value are just byte arrays, so we need to set appropriate deserializers\n140:         props.put(ConsumerConfig.KEY_DESERIALIZER_CLASS_CONFIG, IntegerDeserializer.class);\n141:         props.put(ConsumerConfig.VALUE_DESERIALIZER_CLASS_CONFIG, StringDeserializer.class);\n142:         if (readCommitted) {\n143:             // skips ongoing and aborted transactions\n144:             props.put(ConsumerConfig.ISOLATION_LEVEL_CONFIG, \"read_committed\");\n145:         }\n146:         // sets the reset offset policy in case of invalid or no offset\n147:         props.put(ConsumerConfig.AUTO_OFFSET_RESET_CONFIG, \"earliest\");\n148:         return new KafkaConsumer<>(props);\n149:     }\n150: \n151:     @Override\n152:     public void onPartitionsRevoked(Collection<TopicPartition> partitions) {\n153:         Utils.printOut(\"Revoked partitions: %s\", partitions);\n154:         // this can be used to commit pending offsets when using manual commit and EOS is disabled\n155:     }\n156: \n157:     @Override\n158:     public void onPartitionsAssigned(Collection<TopicPartition> partitions) {\n159:         Utils.printOut(\"Assigned partitions: %s\", partitions);\n160:         // this can be used to read the offsets from an external store or some other initialization\n161:     }\n162: \n163:     @Override\n164:     public void onPartitionsLost(Collection<TopicPartition> partitions) {\n165:         Utils.printOut(\"Lost partitions: %s\", partitions);\n166:         // this is called when partitions are reassigned before we had a chance to revoke them gracefully\n167:         // we can't commit pending offsets because these partitions are probably owned by other consumers already\n168:         // nevertheless, we may need to do some other cleanup\n169:     }\n170: }\n171: ",
    "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-benchmarks/kafka--e678b4b@47ac12bd6042edf23f1dbca3d789db945d62802d/-/blob/examples/src/main/java/kafka/examples/Consumer.java"
  }
}

100. Write @ -
Input
{
  "content": "# Apache Kafka KafkaConsumer API Reference\n\n**Package:** `org.apache.kafka.clients.consumer`\n\n**Version:** Based on Apache Kafka client library (this documentation covers KafkaConsumer behavioral semantics and complete API surface)\n\n## Table of Contents\n\n1. [Overview](#overview)\n2. [Core Types](#core-types)\n3. [Consumer Lifecycle](#consumer-lifecycle)\n4. [Subscription and Assignment](#subscription-and-assignment)\n5. [Polling and Data Fetching](#polling-and-data-fetching)\n6. [Offset Management](#offset-management)\n7. [Flow Control and Position Management](#flow-control-and-position-management)\n8. [Consumer Group Mechanics](#consumer-group-mechanics)\n9. [Rebalancing and Callbacks](#rebalancing-and-callbacks)\n10. [Metadata and Monitoring](#metadata-and-monitoring)\n11. [Error Handling](#error-handling)\n12. [Configuration-Driven Behaviors](#configuration-driven-behaviors)\n13. [Usage Examples](#usage-examples)\n14. [Best Practices and Patterns](#best-practices-and-patterns)\n\n---\n\n## Overview\n\n### What is KafkaConsumer?\n\n`KafkaConsumer` is a client for consuming records from Apache Kafka topics. It handles:\n\n- **Cluster membership**: Automatically maintains TCP connections to broker nodes\n- **Broker failure recovery**: Transparently adapts when brokers fail or partition leaders change\n- **Consumer group coordination**: Works with the Kafka broker's group coordination protocol for distributed, fault-tolerant consumption\n- **Offset management**: Tracks read position and supports automatic or manual offset commits\n- **Load balancing**: Partitions are automatically distributed across group members\n\n### Key Characteristics\n\n- **NOT thread-safe**: All operations must occur on a single thread\n- **Resource-intensive**: Maintains broker connections; must be explicitly closed\n- **Event-driven**: Uses `poll()` as the main entry point for all operations\n- **Transparent rebalancing**: Consumer group membership changes trigger automatic rebalancing via `poll()`\n\n---\n\n## Core Types\n\n### KafkaConsumer<K, V>\n\nThe main consumer class implementing the `Consumer<K, V>` interface.\n\n**Type parameters:**\n- `K`: Type of message keys (e.g., `String`, `byte[]`, custom classes)\n- `V`: Type of message values (e.g., `String`, `byte[]`, custom classes)\n\n### Consumer<K, V> Interface\n\nBase interface that KafkaConsumer implements. Useful for testing and mocking.\n\n```java\npublic interface Consumer<K, V> extends Closeable {\n    // Subscription/assignment\n    Set<TopicPartition> assignment();\n    Set<String> subscription();\n    void subscribe(Collection<String> topics);\n    void subscribe(Collection<String> topics, ConsumerRebalanceListener listener);\n    void subscribe(Pattern pattern);\n    void subscribe(Pattern pattern, ConsumerRebalanceListener listener);\n    void assign(Collection<TopicPartition> partitions);\n    void unsubscribe();\n\n    // Polling\n    ConsumerRecords<K, V> poll(Duration timeout);\n\n    // Offset commits\n    void commitSync();\n    void commitSync(Duration timeout);\n    void commitSync(Map<TopicPartition, OffsetAndMetadata> offsets);\n    void commitSync(Map<TopicPartition, OffsetAndMetadata> offsets, Duration timeout);\n    void commitAsync();\n    void commitAsync(OffsetCommitCallback callback);\n    void commitAsync(Map<TopicPartition, OffsetAndMetadata> offsets, OffsetCommitCallback callback);\n\n    // Position and seeking\n    void seek(TopicPartition partition, long offset);\n    void seek(TopicPartition partition, OffsetAndMetadata offsetAndMetadata);\n    void seekToBeginning(Collection<TopicPartition> partitions);\n    void seekToEnd(Collection<TopicPartition> partitions);\n    long position(TopicPartition partition);\n    long position(TopicPartition partition, Duration timeout);\n\n    // Offset queries\n    Map<TopicPartition, OffsetAndMetadata> committed(Set<TopicPartition> partitions);\n    Map<TopicPartition, OffsetAndMetadata> committed(Set<TopicPartition> partitions, Duration timeout);\n    Map<TopicPartition, OffsetAndTimestamp> offsetsForTimes(Map<TopicPartition, Long> timestampsToSearch);\n    Map<TopicPartition, Long> beginningOffsets(Collection<TopicPartition> partitions);\n    Map<TopicPartition, Long> endOffsets(Collection<TopicPartition> partitions);\n\n    // Flow control\n    void pause(Collection<TopicPartition> partitions);\n    void resume(Collection<TopicPartition> partitions);\n    Set<TopicPartition> paused();\n\n    // Metadata\n    List<PartitionInfo> partitionsFor(String topic);\n    Map<String, List<PartitionInfo>> listTopics();\n    Map<MetricName, ? extends Metric> metrics();\n    Uuid clientInstanceId(Duration timeout);\n\n    // Group management\n    ConsumerGroupMetadata groupMetadata();\n    void enforceRebalance();\n    void enforceRebalance(String reason);\n\n    // Lifecycle\n    void close();\n    void wakeup();\n}\n```\n\n### ConsumerRecords<K, V>\n\nContainer for consumer records returned by `poll()`.\n\n**Key methods:**\n- `List<ConsumerRecord<K, V>> records(TopicPartition partition)` - Get records for specific partition\n- `Iterable<ConsumerRecord<K, V>> records(String topic)` - Get records for specific topic\n- `Set<TopicPartition> partitions()` - Get all partitions in this record batch\n- `Map<TopicPartition, OffsetAndMetadata> nextOffsets()` - Get next offsets after consuming these records\n- `int count()` - Total count of records across all partitions\n- `boolean isEmpty()` - Check if empty\n\n**Behavioral notes:**\n- Implements `Iterable<ConsumerRecord<K, V>>` for easy streaming\n- Contains metadata about next offsets to support automatic offset tracking\n- Records are immutable; iteration is safe from concurrent modification\n\n### ConsumerRecord<K, V>\n\nIndividual message record with metadata.\n\n**Key properties:**\n- `String topic()` - Topic name\n- `int partition()` - Partition number\n- `long offset()` - Message offset in partition\n- `K key()` - Message key (nullable)\n- `V value()` - Message value\n- `long timestamp()` - Timestamp (milliseconds since epoch)\n- `int serializedKeySize()` - Size of key in bytes\n- `int serializedValueSize()` - Size of value in bytes\n- `Headers headers()` - Message headers (if enabled)\n- `long leaderEpoch()` - Leader epoch at time of produce (useful for detecting log truncation)\n\n### OffsetAndMetadata\n\nRepresents a committed offset with optional metadata and leader epoch.\n\n```java\npublic class OffsetAndMetadata {\n    // Constructors\n    public OffsetAndMetadata(long offset)  // offset only\n    public OffsetAndMetadata(long offset, String metadata)  // with metadata\n    public OffsetAndMetadata(long offset, Optional<Integer> leaderEpoch, String metadata)  // full\n\n    // Methods\n    public long offset()\n    public String metadata()\n    public Optional<Integer> leaderEpoch()\n}\n```\n\n**Semantic notes:**\n- Metadata field is useful for tracking commit source or context\n- Leader epoch helps detect log truncation in broker\n- Negative offsets throw `IllegalArgumentException` at construction\n\n### ConsumerRebalanceListener\n\nInterface for rebalance callbacks during consumer group coordination.\n\n```java\npublic interface ConsumerRebalanceListener {\n    /**\n     * Called when partitions are revoked (normal end-of-ownership).\n     * Safe to commit offsets here via consumer.commitSync().\n     */\n    void onPartitionsRevoked(Collection<TopicPartition> partitions);\n\n    /**\n     * Called after rebalance completes with newly assigned partitions.\n     * Safe to initialize positions here via consumer.seek().\n     */\n    void onPartitionsAssigned(Collection<TopicPartition> partitions);\n\n    /**\n     * Called when partitions are lost without revocation opportunity\n     * (e.g., session timeout). DO NOT commit offsets here.\n     * Default implementation calls onPartitionsRevoked().\n     */\n    default void onPartitionsLost(Collection<TopicPartition> partitions) {\n        onPartitionsRevoked(partitions);\n    }\n}\n```\n\n**Callback execution:**\n- All callbacks execute on the consumer thread during `poll()` calls\n- Exceptions from callbacks propagate to the `poll()` caller\n- Order is: `onPartitionsRevoked()` \u2192 rebalance \u2192 `onPartitionsAssigned()`\n- `onPartitionsLost()` only called when partitions reassigned without revocation\n\n### OffsetCommitCallback\n\nCallback for asynchronous offset commits.\n\n```java\npublic interface OffsetCommitCallback {\n    /**\n     * Called when offset commit response received from broker.\n     * @param offsets Map of offsets committed (may be partial if error)\n     * @param exception Exception if commit failed, null if successful\n     */\n    void onComplete(Map<TopicPartition, OffsetAndMetadata> offsets, Exception exception);\n}\n```\n\n---\n\n## Consumer Lifecycle\n\n### Creating a Consumer\n\n#### Constructor Variants\n\n```java\n// With Properties (string configurations)\nKafkaConsumer<String, String> consumer = new KafkaConsumer<>(\n    properties,\n    new StringDeserializer(),\n    new StringDeserializer()\n);\n\n// With Map<String, Object>\nKafkaConsumer<String, String> consumer = new KafkaConsumer<>(\n    configs,\n    new StringDeserializer(),\n    new StringDeserializer()\n);\n\n// Without explicit deserializers (from config)\nKafkaConsumer<String, String> consumer = new KafkaConsumer<>(properties);\n\n// Minimal\nKafkaConsumer<byte[], byte[]> consumer = new KafkaConsumer<>(properties);\n```\n\n**Minimum required configuration:**\n- `bootstrap.servers`: Initial broker addresses (e.g., \"localhost:9092\")\n- `key.deserializer`: Class name of key deserializer\n- `value.deserializer`: Class name of value deserializer\n- `group.id`: Consumer group name (for group-managed subscriptions)\n\n**Important notes:**\n- Consumer acquires resources immediately (broker connections)\n- All configuration keys are documented in `ConsumerConfig`\n- Passing deserializers directly bypasses `configure()` call from config\n\n#### Configuration Properties\n\nKey configuration properties that affect behavior:\n\n| Property | Default | Purpose |\n|----------|---------|---------|\n| `bootstrap.servers` | (required) | Comma-separated broker addresses |\n| `group.id` | \"\" | Consumer group ID (required for group management) |\n| `key.deserializer` | (required) | Key deserializer class |\n| `value.deserializer` | (required) | Value deserializer class |\n| `enable.auto.commit` | true | Enable automatic offset commits |\n| `auto.commit.interval.ms` | 5000 | Auto-commit interval (milliseconds) |\n| `session.timeout.ms` | 45000 | Session timeout before consumer is removed from group |\n| `heartbeat.interval.ms` | 10000 | Interval for sending heartbeats |\n| `max.poll.interval.ms` | 300000 | Max time between poll() calls before removal from group |\n| `max.poll.records` | 500 | Max records returned per poll() |\n| `auto.offset.reset` | \"latest\" | Offset policy when not found: \"earliest\", \"latest\", or \"none\" |\n| `isolation.level` | \"read_uncommitted\" | \"read_committed\" to skip uncommitted transaction messages |\n| `fetch.min.bytes` | 1 | Min bytes broker waits before responding to fetch |\n| `fetch.max.wait.ms` | 500 | Max time broker waits to accumulate fetch.min.bytes |\n\nSee the **Configuration-Driven Behaviors** section for how these affect API behavior.\n\n### Closing a Consumer\n\n```java\n// Simple close (default timeout from config)\nconsumer.close();\n\n// With explicit timeout\nconsumer.close(Duration.ofSeconds(30));\n\n// With close options\nconsumer.close(new CloseOptions().timeout(Duration.ofSeconds(30)));\n```\n\n**Important behaviors:**\n- `close()` without timeout uses `request.timeout.ms` from config\n- Triggers `onPartitionsRevoked()` callback if using group management\n- Blocks until all pending operations complete or timeout expires\n- Safe to call multiple times (idempotent)\n- Should be in try-with-resources or finally block\n\n**Resource cleanup:**\n- Closes broker connections\n- Stops heartbeat thread\n- Flushes pending offset commits (if auto-commit enabled)\n- Revokes all assigned partitions\n\n---\n\n## Subscription and Assignment\n\n### Dynamic Subscription (Group-Managed)\n\nConsumer group coordinator automatically manages partition assignment.\n\n```java\n// Subscribe to specific topics\nconsumer.subscribe(Arrays.asList(\"topic1\", \"topic2\"), listener);\n\n// Subscribe with rebalance listener (recommended)\nconsumer.subscribe(\n    Arrays.asList(\"orders\", \"returns\"),\n    new ConsumerRebalanceListener() {\n        @Override\n        public void onPartitionsRevoked(Collection<TopicPartition> partitions) {\n            // Save offsets for graceful shutdown\n            consumer.commitSync();\n        }\n\n        @Override\n        public void onPartitionsAssigned(Collection<TopicPartition> partitions) {\n            // Initialize any state needed for new partitions\n        }\n    }\n);\n\n// Get current subscription\nSet<String> topics = consumer.subscription();\n```\n\n**Semantics:**\n- Topics can be added/removed dynamically between polls\n- Subscription change triggers rebalance on next `poll()`\n- Regex subscription updates topic list on each `poll()` based on broker metadata\n- Only one subscription mode per consumer (subscribe OR assign)\n\n### Manual Assignment (Standalone)\n\nConsumer directly controls partition assignment without group coordination.\n\n```java\n// Manually assign partitions\nconsumer.assign(Arrays.asList(\n    new TopicPartition(\"orders\", 0),\n    new TopicPartition(\"orders\", 1),\n    new TopicPartition(\"orders\", 2)\n));\n\n// Get current assignment\nSet<TopicPartition> partitions = consumer.assignment();\n\n// Unsubscribe/unassign (resets to empty)\nconsumer.unsubscribe();\n```\n\n**Behavioral differences from subscription:**\n- No rebalancing occurs\n- No group coordination\n- Partitions never reassigned unless manually changed\n- Consumer doesn't heartbeat to broker\n- No `max.poll.interval.ms` enforcement\n- Useful for standalone consumers or precise partition control\n\n### Subscription vs. Assignment\n\n**Use subscription when:**\n- Building fault-tolerant consumer groups\n- Multiple consumers share work\n- Automatic load balancing desired\n- Partition count changes dynamically\n\n**Use assignment when:**\n- Building standalone consumers\n- Precise partition control needed\n- Testing/debugging specific partitions\n- Building custom rebalancing logic\n\n---\n\n## Polling and Data Fetching\n\n### The poll() Method\n\nCore method that fetches records and handles all background operations.\n\n```java\n// Fetch records with timeout\nConsumerRecords<String, String> records = consumer.poll(Duration.ofSeconds(1));\n\n// Process records\nfor (ConsumerRecord<String, String> record : records) {\n    System.out.println(record.key() + \": \" + record.value());\n}\n\n// Get records for specific partition\nList<ConsumerRecord<String, String>> tp0Records = records.records(\n    new TopicPartition(\"orders\", 0)\n);\n\n// Get next offsets after consuming\nMap<TopicPartition, OffsetAndMetadata> nextOffsets = records.nextOffsets();\n```\n\n### poll() Blocking Behavior\n\n**When `poll()` returns immediately:**\n- Records are available in internal buffers (returns data with no additional network wait)\n- Timeout expires (returns empty ConsumerRecords)\n- Fetcher has reached end of assigned partitions\n- Metadata refresh not needed\n\n**When `poll()` blocks:**\n- Waiting for broker to accumulate `fetch.min.bytes` (up to `fetch.max.wait.ms`)\n- Rebalance callback executing (can block longer than timeout)\n- Metadata refresh in progress\n- Broker connection establishment\n\n**Critical semantic:** The provided timeout is NOT guaranteed to limit total execution time. If rebalance callbacks (`onPartitionsRevoked()`, `onPartitionsAssigned()`) take time, the actual `poll()` duration can exceed timeout. This is intentional to ensure callbacks complete without interruption.\n\n**Example of extended blocking:**\n```java\n// Timeout is 1 second, but can take longer if rebalance occurs\nConsumerRecords<String, String> records = consumer.poll(Duration.ofSeconds(1));\n// If rebalance callback takes 3 seconds, this call may take 3+ seconds total\n```\n\n### poll() Frequency and Group Membership\n\nFor group-managed consumers (via `subscribe()`):\n- **Must call `poll()` at least once every `max.poll.interval.ms`** (default 5 minutes)\n- If no `poll()` call within this window, broker removes consumer from group\n- Rebalance is triggered, and partitions are reassigned to other members\n- Useful for detecting consumer deadlock or processing hang\n\nFor standalone consumers (via `assign()`):\n- No polling frequency requirement\n- No heartbeat or session timeout\n\n### Non-blocking Variants\n\n**Why there's no non-blocking variant:**\nKafka consumer design intentionally couples data fetching with background operations (heartbeats, rebalances, metadata refreshes) via `poll()`. This ensures:\n- Rebalance callbacks execute deterministically\n- Position advances atomically with data reception\n- No race conditions with group coordination\n\nFor async processing patterns, use `pause()` to buffer records into application queues.\n\n---\n\n## Offset Management\n\n### Understanding Offsets\n\n**Offset:** A monotonically increasing integer identifying a record's position in a partition.\n\n**Three offset concepts:**\n\n1. **Position**: Offset of next record to fetch\n   - Auto-advances when records returned from `poll()`\n   - Can be queried with `position(TopicPartition)`\n   - Can be set with `seek(TopicPartition, long)`\n\n2. **Committed Offset**: Last offset committed (durably stored in broker)\n   - Retrieved with `committed(Set<TopicPartition>)`\n   - Set via `commitSync()` or `commitAsync()`\n   - Used as restart point on consumer failure\n   - Stored in special `__consumer_offsets` topic\n\n3. **Lag**: Difference between latest offset and committed offset\n   - Indicates how far behind the consumer is\n   - Queryable via `currentLag(TopicPartition)`\n   - Important for monitoring\n\n**Offset invariant:** \"Committed offset should be the NEXT offset to read\"\n```\nConsumed offset 99 \u2192 commit offset 100 \u2192 seek to 100 \u2192 consume record at offset 100\n```\n\n### Automatic Offset Commits\n\n```java\n// Enabled by default with enable.auto.commit=true\n// Commits happen every auto.commit.interval.ms (default 5 seconds)\n\n// Triggered during poll() calls\nConsumerRecords<String, String> records = consumer.poll(Duration.ofSeconds(1));\n// If 5+ seconds since last commit, auto-commit fires BEFORE returning to user\n\n// At-least-once guarantee is harder (records may process multiple times on failure)\n// Exactly-once requires manual commit AFTER processing\n```\n\n**Auto-commit semantics:**\n- Commits the highest offset consumed so far in each partition\n- Happens BEFORE `poll()` returns records to user (important for timing)\n- No coordination with user processing (records may fail to process)\n- Not suitable for exactly-once semantic requirements\n\n**Risks with auto-commit:**\n- Records returned to user but not yet committed (process crashes)\n- Rebalance can revoke partition before implicit commit fires\n- Committed offset may not reflect actual user consumption\n\n### Manual Offset Commits (Synchronous)\n\n```java\n// Disable auto-commit first\nprops.put(ConsumerConfig.ENABLE_AUTO_COMMIT_CONFIG, \"false\");\n\nKafkaConsumer<String, String> consumer = new KafkaConsumer<>(props);\n\ntry {\n    consumer.subscribe(Arrays.asList(\"orders\"));\n\n    while (running) {\n        ConsumerRecords<String, String> records = consumer.poll(Duration.ofSeconds(1));\n\n        for (ConsumerRecord<String, String> record : records) {\n            // Process record\n            process(record);  // May throw exception\n        }\n\n        // Commit only after successful processing\n        try {\n            consumer.commitSync();\n        } catch (CommitFailedException e) {\n            log.error(\"Commit failed, offset tracking may be lost\", e);\n        }\n    }\n} finally {\n    consumer.close();\n}\n```\n\n**Synchronous commit API:**\n\n```java\n// Commit current position in all assigned partitions\nconsumer.commitSync();\n\n// Commit with timeout\nconsumer.commitSync(Duration.ofSeconds(30));\n\n// Commit specific offsets (batch)\nMap<TopicPartition, OffsetAndMetadata> offsets = new HashMap<>();\noffsets.put(tp0, new OffsetAndMetadata(100));\noffsets.put(tp1, new OffsetAndMetadata(200));\nconsumer.commitSync(offsets);\n\n// Commit with timeout\nconsumer.commitSync(offsets, Duration.ofSeconds(30));\n```\n\n**Behavioral semantics:**\n- Blocks until broker responds or timeout expires\n- Throws `CommitFailedException` if commit fails (not retriable from client perspective)\n- Throws `WakeupException` if consumer woken up during commit\n- Throws `InterruptException` if thread interrupted\n- For group-managed consumers, must be called before `onPartitionsRevoked()` completes\n\n### Manual Offset Commits (Asynchronous)\n\n```java\n// Asynchronous commit with callback\nconsumer.commitAsync((offsets, exception) -> {\n    if (exception != null) {\n        log.error(\"Async commit failed for offsets: \" + offsets, exception);\n        // Cannot retry automatically - must handle at application level\n    } else {\n        log.debug(\"Successfully committed offsets: \" + offsets);\n    }\n});\n\n// Continue processing without waiting for response\nConsumerRecords<String, String> records = consumer.poll(Duration.ofSeconds(1));\nfor (ConsumerRecord<String, String> record : records) {\n    process(record);\n}\n```\n\n**Asynchronous commit API:**\n\n```java\n// Commit without callback\nconsumer.commitAsync();\n\n// Commit with callback\nconsumer.commitAsync(new OffsetCommitCallback() {\n    @Override\n    public void onComplete(Map<TopicPartition, OffsetAndMetadata> offsets,\n                          Exception exception) {\n        // Handle response\n    }\n});\n\n// Commit specific offsets with callback\nMap<TopicPartition, OffsetAndMetadata> offsets = new HashMap<>();\noffsets.put(tp0, new OffsetAndMetadata(100));\nconsumer.commitAsync(offsets, callback);\n```\n\n**Important semantics:**\n- Returns immediately (non-blocking)\n- Callback invoked asynchronously when response received\n- Callback exceptions do NOT propagate to `poll()` caller\n- Failed async commits are NOT automatically retried (application must retry)\n- Multiple pending async commits can race; last one wins\n- Useful for fire-and-forget scenarios but requires careful error handling\n\n### Sync vs. Async Trade-offs\n\n| Aspect | Synchronous | Asynchronous |\n|--------|------------|----------------|\n| Blocking | Yes, until response | No, returns immediately |\n| Error handling | Immediate exception | Callback receives exception |\n| Retries | Framework may retry | Application must retry |\n| Performance | Lower throughput (blocking) | Higher throughput |\n| Reliability | Stronger (confirms before proceeding) | Weaker (fire-and-forget) |\n| Use case | Exactly-once semantics | High-throughput, at-least-once |\n\n### Querying Committed Offsets\n\n```java\n// Query committed offset for specific partitions\nSet<TopicPartition> partitions = consumer.assignment();\nMap<TopicPartition, OffsetAndMetadata> committed = consumer.committed(partitions);\n\nfor (TopicPartition tp : partitions) {\n    OffsetAndMetadata offsetMeta = committed.get(tp);\n    if (offsetMeta != null) {\n        System.out.println(tp + \": offset=\" + offsetMeta.offset() +\n                          \", metadata=\" + offsetMeta.metadata());\n    } else {\n        System.out.println(tp + \": no committed offset\");\n    }\n}\n\n// With timeout\nMap<TopicPartition, OffsetAndMetadata> committed =\n    consumer.committed(partitions, Duration.ofSeconds(10));\n```\n\n**Behavioral notes:**\n- Queries broker's `__consumer_offsets` topic (coordinator)\n- Returns `null` for partitions with no prior commit\n- Includes metadata string and leader epoch if set\n- May block if coordinator discovery needed\n\n---\n\n## Flow Control and Position Management\n\n### Seeking to Specific Offsets\n\n```java\nTopicPartition tp = new TopicPartition(\"orders\", 0);\n\n// Seek to specific offset\nconsumer.seek(tp, 100);  // Next poll() fetches from offset 100\n\n// Seek to beginning\nconsumer.seekToBeginning(Arrays.asList(tp));  // Seek to offset 0\n\n// Seek to end\nconsumer.seekToEnd(Arrays.asList(tp));  // Seek to latest+1 (next to be produced)\n\n// Seek to offset from metadata (with leader epoch)\nOffsetAndMetadata offsetMeta = new OffsetAndMetadata(100, 2);\nconsumer.seek(tp, offsetMeta);\n\n// Validate offset was set\nlong position = consumer.position(tp);\nassert position == 100;\n```\n\n**Key semantic:** `seekToEnd()` seeks to the offset AFTER the last message. Reading from this position returns empty until new messages arrive.\n\n### Timestamp-Based Seeking\n\n```java\n// Search for offset by timestamp\nMap<TopicPartition, Long> timestampMap = new HashMap<>();\ntimestampMap.put(tp0, System.currentTimeMillis() - 3600000);  // 1 hour ago\ntimestampMap.put(tp1, System.currentTimeMillis() - 7200000);  // 2 hours ago\n\nMap<TopicPartition, OffsetAndTimestamp> offsets =\n    consumer.offsetsForTimes(timestampMap);\n\nfor (Map.Entry<TopicPartition, OffsetAndTimestamp> entry : offsets.entrySet()) {\n    TopicPartition tp = entry.getKey();\n    OffsetAndTimestamp offsetAndTimestamp = entry.getValue();\n\n    if (offsetAndTimestamp != null) {\n        consumer.seek(tp, offsetAndTimestamp.offset());\n        System.out.println(tp + \": Found offset \" + offsetAndTimestamp.offset() +\n                         \" at timestamp \" + offsetAndTimestamp.timestamp());\n    } else {\n        System.out.println(tp + \": No offset found for that timestamp\");\n    }\n}\n```\n\n**Timestamp seeking requires:**\n- Broker-side log index retention (default enabled)\n- Messages must have timestamp (enabled by default)\n- Timestamp semantics: CreateTime (message creation) or LogAppendTime (broker append)\n\n### Beginning and End Offsets\n\n```java\n// Get beginning offset (oldest available)\nMap<TopicPartition, Long> beginningOffsets =\n    consumer.beginningOffsets(consumer.assignment());\n\n// Get end offset (offset after latest)\nMap<TopicPartition, Long> endOffsets =\n    consumer.endOffsets(consumer.assignment());\n\n// Example: Log all unread messages\nfor (TopicPartition tp : consumer.assignment()) {\n    long start = beginningOffsets.get(tp);\n    long end = endOffsets.get(tp);\n    System.out.println(tp + \": available offsets [\" + start + \", \" + end + \")\");\n}\n```\n\n**Retention semantics:**\n- Beginning offset: Oldest message still retained by broker\n- End offset: Offset of next message to be produced\n- Available messages: `[beginningOffsets, endOffsets)`\n- Older messages may be deleted due to retention policy\n\n### Position Queries\n\n```java\n// Get current position (offset of next record to fetch)\nlong position = consumer.position(tp);\nSystem.out.println(\"Next fetch will read from offset: \" + position);\n\n// After processing records, position advances\nConsumerRecords<String, String> records = consumer.poll(Duration.ofSeconds(1));\nlong newPosition = consumer.position(tp);\nSystem.out.println(\"Position
